520a77823030315011001698_11.t: Milk run or not, we should be careful.
Glory. Razor-clawed street samurai. Her voice is cold and neutral, her expression placid.

520a77823030315011001698_12.t: Words of wisdom from our new addition. I agree.
Glory. Razor-clawed street samurai. Her voice is cold and neutral, her expression placid.

520a77823030315011001698_24.t: Milk run or not, we should be careful.
Glory. Razor-clawed street samurai. Her voice is cold and neutral, her expression placid.

520a77823030315011001698_26.t: I won't argue with the sentiment, but I'd prefer that we exercise caution.
Glory. Razor-clawed street samurai. Her voice is cold and neutral, her expression placid.

52129dc23030310815002cdb_10.t: She's had a stroke. A bad one. She'll be dead before we're even back to the van.

523749c0303031940c0001ec_18.t: As you turn to leave, Glory leans in and whispers to you.
What the hell was that about?

523b948f346238e41d007555_168.t: I trust in Monika's judgment. Therefore I trust in our new leader's judgment.

523cb418346238d026006192_4.t: Thank you for your help at Feuerstelle. I couldn't have done it without you.

523cb418346238d026006192_7.t: Yes. And he's already putting distance between himself and the Schönbuch Forest.

523cb418346238d026006192_13.t: As well as can be expected. My feelings on this are... complicated.

523cb418346238d026006192_20.t: No, it's okay... I *should* talk about this. Running from it won't help anything.
A part of me still loved her, I think. But the rest hated her for what she represented.

523cb418346238d026006192_22.t: I killed the first person I'd ever loved. Whatever else Marta might have been, she'll always be that.
Am I ultimately sorry that she's dead...? Yes. But I'm not sure what the alternative would be.

523cb418346238d026006192_23.t: I don't know. I don't even know if I want her to be.

523cb418346238d026006192_25.t: Let's talk about something else. I'd rather not dwell on this if I don't have to.

523cb418346238d026006192_29.t: Let's talk about something else. I'd rather not dwell on this if I don't have to.

523cb418346238d026006192_31.t: Glory looks you in the eye, and her expression hardens.
Then I'll find her and correct our mistake.

523cb418346238d026006192_32.t: It's her decision now... the ball is in her court. But for what it's worth, so do I.

523cb418346238d026006192_35.t: Thank you for your help at Feuerstelle. I couldn't have done it without you.

523cb418346238d026006192_38.t: There is a long pause before she responds.
You'll need to be more specific.

523cb418346238d026006192_39.t: Ah. That. Well, clearly, *something* happened when I plunged my claw into the Heart of Feuerstelle. But as to what that something was...
Your guess is as good as mine.

523cb418346238d026006192_42.t: Don't bother answering that - it was a rhetorical question. And I can see that you won't be satisfied until I offer you some sort of real answer.
I suppose it's possible that, when the spirit died, some portion of its essence was trapped in the hand that I used to kill it.

523cb418346238d026006192_43.t: We were in a place with different rules, remember? I'm already too far out of my comfort zone to offer you any real answers.
Maybe it reacted to the orichalcum in the balm that I was wearing. I don't know.

523cb418346238d026006192_51.t: Something happened when I plunged my claw into the Heart of Feuerstelle - that much is clear. But as to what that something was...
Your guess is as good as mine. But it's worked out well for us, hasn't it?

523cb418346238d026006192_55.t: She nods, and her expression softens.
For what it's worth, we *did* save a lot of people back there. Harrow is dead, and Feuerstelle is gone. Nobody will ever be corrupted by that place again. For that, you should feel proud.

523cb418346238d026006192_56.t: Glory is beautiful, in a waifish sort of way. Her features are almost elvish in their delicacy, but there's something cold about her that you find slightly unsettling.

523cb418346238d026006192_57.t: What's more unsettling is her chrome. Glory is rocking a heavy loadout of cyberware; from head to toe, she looks to be composed more of plastic and metal than she is of skin and bone.

523cb418346238d026006192_58.t: In the shadows, individuals such as this are anything but uncommon. But Glory's cyberware is first-generation, all of it. Bulky, invasive, practically museum pieces... this chrome was obsolete well before she was born.

523cb418346238d026006192_59.t: Glory shifts her gaze to you, but her expression is as cool and placid as always.

523cb418346238d026006192_61.t: Without shifting your attention away from Glory, you allow your consciousness to partially slip into the astral plane.

523cb418346238d026006192_63.t: As the astral plane shimmers into view, you focus on Glory. You can still see her with your corporeal vision: her delicate frame, the bulky cyberware that breaks her silhouette, the faraway look in her eyes. But laid over the top of her physical body, there is a slight shimmer - her aura, damaged though it may be.

523cb418346238d026006192_64.t: The chrome that Glory is sporting has really done a number on her. Whatever she may have been before she went under the knife, her aura is now reminiscent of the room she's standing in: a cold, dead thing, mockingly sculpted into a facsimile of life. Somewhere within that mutilated aura, a shred of humanity awaits, but finding it isn't going to be easy.

523cb418346238d026006192_65.t: Suddenly, Glory's body language changes. The look on her face is somewhere between suspicion and alarm.
I got something in my teeth? You're staring.
With an aura this shredded by cyberware and surgery, it's going to take a minute to gather any useful information.

523cb418346238d026006192_67.t: As Glory speaks, you continue to focus, sifting through the void left by her vintage chrome to find the tatters of her original essence. Finally, you catch a glimpse of color, a flash of her true self... and behind it, something else.

523cb418346238d026006192_71.t: Trembling, you find yourself back in the mundane world. Glory is still staring at you, her neutral expression ever-so-slightly tinged with annoyance.

523cb418346238d026006192_73.t: Glory stares at you for a hard second, then turns her head away. With a quick, birdlike movement, she thrusts a finger into her mouth and rubs her teeth vigorously.

523cb418346238d026006192_75.t: As your third eye slides shut, the faint shimmer of Glory's aura fades from view, taking any information that you could learn from it along for the ride.

523cb418346238d026006192_77.t: It saw you? That's what you're worried about?
A shrill laugh escapes from Glory's lips.
You'll be fine. Wanna know a secret? It's been watching you for your entire life. You just never knew that it was watching until now.

523cb418346238d026006192_78.t: Some of us have bigger problems. For some of us, it takes a more... direct interest. But I fixed that problem years ago.
She gestures down at the vintage chrome that riddles her small body.
The interest is still there, but it can't do anything about it any more. Not unless I let it. So drop it, okay?

523cb418346238d026006192_79.t: You don't know the half of it. But it isn't dangerous to you any more. I fixed it, understand?
She gestures at the antique cyberware that riddles her body.
It's mostly gone now. The only person it can hurt is me, and only if I let it. So drop it, okay?

523cb418346238d026006192_80.t: Glory's lip curls into a sneer.
It happened a long time ago, and I fixed it. What you saw was nothing compared to how it used be, and you shouldn't've been looking in the first place.

523cb418346238d026006192_81.t: She pauses for a moment, and the hard expression on her face softens.

Look, I understand that you're trying to help me, and I appreciate it. I do. But I'm not ready to talk about this yet. Maybe when we know one another a little better, okay? Until then, just give me my space.

523cb418346238d026006192_84.t: Something about Glory's gaze gives you pause. There's steel in there, but that's nothing unusual; as a rule, people in your line of work don't back down easily. But buried someplace behind the bravado and the determination, there's something else... something that makes your skin crawl.

523cb418346238d026006192_85.t: Glory's expression remains neutral, but she grants you a barely perceptible nod.

523cb418346238d026006192_89.t: I've heard of a lot of lodges. Shamanic lodges, nature lodges, hunting lodges. Can you be more specific?

523cb418346238d026006192_90.t: She pauses for a moment, considering. Finally, she shakes her head.
Nothing is coming to me, sorry. Dietrich might be able to help - he's been in Berlin longer than any of us, and if this "Lodge" does have shamanic connections, he'd be the most likely one to know.

523cb418346238d026006192_93.t: I'm not big on sharing, sport. Personal reasons. You understand, I'm sure.
The edge in her voice tells you that she's not interested in continuing this conversation.

523cb418346238d026006192_94.t: Glory lets out a weary sigh.
Ask your questions, but do it quickly. I have things to do.

523cb418346238d026006192_96.t: It was cheap. It gets the job done.

523cb418346238d026006192_107.t: I don't respond well to threats or ultimatums.
Something in the tiny woman's voice sets you on edge. There is something deeply wrong here. Somehow, you're certain of it.
You'll want to watch that in the future. Now walk away.

523cb418346238d026006192_110.t: Glory's expression remains distant, but her lips curl upward into a hint of a smile.

523cb418346238d026006192_112.t: Without shifting your attention away from Glory, you allow your consciousness to partially slip into the astral plane.

Look, I understand that you're trying to help me, and I appreciate it. I do. But I'm not ready to talk about this yet. Maybe when we know one another a little better, okay? Until then, just give me my space.

523cb418346238d026006192_136.t: If you really want to get into it, we're going to need to go back. Way back.
She stares at you with her cold, dead eyes.
I'm not sure that you want to do that.

523cb418346238d026006192_137.t: There is no hostility in her voice. If anything, she sounds suspicious.

523cb418346238d026006192_138.t: She pauses for a moment, considering. Then she nods.
All right. Fine. We can talk. But we're doing it on my terms.

523cb418346238d026006192_141.t: She takes a deep breath, then slowly releases it. When she speaks, her voice comes out in a cool monotone.
My family lived in Stuttgart. It was a nice enough place, I suppose. My parents worked as wageslaves for IFMU.

523cb418346238d026006192_148.t: What, you mean pick on a victim who could fight back?

523cb418346238d026006192_153.t: Glory pauses, then swallows hard. Ever so slightly, her cheeks flush red. Suddenly she looks self-conscious, as if she's just snapped out of a trance. As quickly as it came, the flush of emotion disappears. Her voice frosts over.
I don't want to talk about this any more.

523cb418346238d026006192_154.t: Glory stares at you through cold, dead eyes.
But I'm not going to be able to get through this in one sitting. You need to understand that.

523cb418346238d026006192_155.t: She turns away from you, her movements mechanical.
Now. Let's get back to the job at hand, shall we?

523cb418346238d026006192_156.t: Glory stares at you through cold, dead eyes.
Anyway, we have a job to do, so let's get on with it. We can chat again later.

523cb418346238d026006192_157.t: Glory stares at you through cold, dead eyes.
Now, let's get back to the job at hand, shall we?

523cb418346238d026006192_159.t: I've heard of a lot of lodges. Shamanic lodges, nature lodges, hunting lodges. Can you be more specific?

523cb418346238d026006192_160.t: She pauses for a moment, considering. Finally, she shakes her head.
Nothing is coming to me, sorry. Dietrich might be able to help - he's been in Berlin longer than any of us, and if this "Lodge" does have shamanic connections, he'd be the most likely one to know.

523cb418346238d026006192_164.t: She pauses to study you for a moment before continuing.

523cb418346238d026006192_166.t: We're one step closer to paying Alice's fee. In the end, that's all that really matters.

523cb418346238d026006192_169.t: A tinge of red crawls up Glory's neck.
She extends her sculpted metal arms. Chrome glints in the flickering light.
I'm... I'm not far from being what that poor troll was. When I think of what they did to him...

523cb418346238d026006192_170.t: She looks you in the eye. Her expression remains neutral, but that slight blush has spread from her neck to her cheeks.
...It wasn't right. And you did the only thing that you could to make it better. You knew that it would cost you, and you did it anyway, because it was *right.* I won't forget that. Not ever.

523cb418346238d026006192_171.t: She looks you in the eye. Transfixes you with her gaze.
You handed him over. Like a piece of merchandise. Like a *thing.* We shouldn't have done that. I should have *stopped* you. But I didn't. And I don't like what that says about me.

523cb418346238d026006192_173.t: You took the time to save Aztechnology's test subjects. In your place, I might not have... I don't think I could've justified endangering the mission.
She pauses for a moment, considering.
Still, it worked out. And I suppose that's all that matters.

523cb418346238d026006192_174.t: You put a stop to it. Buried their research under a ton of concrete, and made us a hefty sum in the process.
All in all, a good day.

523cb418346238d026006192_176.t: She pauses for a moment to consider.
That said... I'll admit that I do enjoy a certain sense of satisfaction thinking about Stahl's head breaking apart. So there's that.

523cb418346238d026006192_177.t: She pauses for a moment to consider.
I suppose that it would have been nice to see Stahl die. But his days are numbered anyway. When the F-State discovers what he was planning, he's going to wish that he had never been born.

523cb418346238d026006192_181.t: For the first time since you met her, Glory's expression seems to brighten. It's subtle, but it's there... you're sure of it.
Boss. A pleasure, as always. Need anything from me?

523cb418346238d026006192_205.t: She studies your face for a moment before responding.
I suppose that you might say that. I don't talk to many people. It just doesn't seem to be worth the effort.

523cb418346238d026006192_206.t: I don't... I don't really *feel* much any more. Not since...
She caresses the chrome of her forearm with a hand made of articulated steel.

523cb418346238d026006192_208.t: She locks eyes with you. Her expression is blank.
I don't want to mislead you. I still don't feel anything... not warmth, or friendship, or even trust.

523cb418346238d026006192_212.t: She stares at you, her face expressionless.

523cb418346238d026006192_214.t: She pauses a moment to consider your words, a troubled look on her face.
I suppose that's true. Feeling discomfort is better than feeling nothing at all.

523cb418346238d026006192_216.t: The thousand-meter stare creeps back into Glory's eyes. A moment later, she's locked away in her own head again, reliving memories that she'd long since filed away.
So a few days after my fourteenth birthday, I began to... express. Magically.

523cb418346238d026006192_218.t: Glory gestures at her cyberware-riddled body.

523cb418346238d026006192_220.t: And you're wrong, by the way. I "went nuts with the discount chrome" for a damned good reason, which I will get to in good time. Now shut your mouth and listen. You might learn something.

523cb418346238d026006192_221.t: There is another long pause, and Glory becomes more animated.
Anyway. I turned fourteen. I Awakened.

523cb418346238d026006192_233.t: Slowly, sadly, Glory shakes her head.
But we'll get to that in a minute.

523cb418346238d026006192_236.t: Glory pauses for a moment and looks down at the floor, then fixes her eyes on you. She is visibly tense.
We're gonna fast forward through the next few years. I was a kid on the street. I got by as best I could, doing whatever I had to do. The rest we can leave up to your imagination. Good?

523cb418346238d026006192_238.t: Glory's clenched jaw relaxes somewhat, and the tension drains out of her shoulders.

523cb418346238d026006192_243.t: Glory pauses. She looks flustered, her composure cracked.

523cb418346238d026006192_244.t: She stares at you for a beat, then nods. A few ragged breaths later, she continues.
Marta told me about a place where she'd been hanging out. She invited me to join her there. It was called Feuerstelle, the "Fire Place," and it was sort of a commune for dispossessed youth.

523cb418346238d026006192_248.t: Glory pauses again, and you recognize the distinctive flush in her cheeks. She seems to have come out of her reverie once again.
That's enough for now. I need to process all of this, and I don't want to continue forward until I've had the chance to do that.

523cb418346238d026006192_249.t: I know that this all happened years ago, but I guess it's still pretty raw to me. So I'm gonna ask you to give me some space for a while.
She turns away, and the frost begins to creep back into her voice.
We can pick it back up later.

523cb418346238d026006192_250.t: All at once, Glory shuts down.
You're right. It was a stupid idea anyway.

523cb418346238d026006192_252.t: I've heard of a lot of lodges. Shamanic lodges, nature lodges, hunting lodges. Can you be more specific?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_61.t: As you approach, Eiger turns to face you. Her rifle has been field-stripped and is lying in pieces on a sheet of butcher paper. Arranged in a neat row along the edge of the paper are bottles of copper solvent, bore cleaner, and lighter fluid.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_68.t: You've got the right attitude. Maybe my concerns are misplaced. Or maybe you're just telling me what I want to hear. Only time will tell.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_70.t: That's fair. I've given you reason to be concerned. In fact, it'd be foolish of you not to be.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_72.t: No need to worry. My job is to support you and the rest of this team, and that's exactly what I'm gonna do. As long as you hold up your end, everything should work out just fine.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_73.t: All right. If that's what you want.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_77.t: You've got the right attitude. Maybe my concerns are misplaced. Or maybe you're just telling me what I want to hear. Only time will tell.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_81.t: No. I'm not in the habit of sharing war stories. Not with people who've never served.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_84.t: She looks you in the eye for a moment, evaluating. Finally, she shrugs.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_88.t: I think that he was trying to proposition me, in his clumsy way. Poking me in front of the other recruits to try to provoke a reaction.
Anyway, he got one. I broke three of his ribs, and the name stuck.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_89.t: Eiger regards you with a critical eye.
You might actually mean that. But then, you may just be trying to get on my good side.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_90.t: For once, we’re in complete agreement. But there was more to it than that.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_94.t: Eiger pauses for a moment, then looks you in the eye.

A word of advice to you. If anyone ever puts you in the position that he put me in - if he demeans you in front of your unit, *whatever* his intent - the appropriate response is to put that person down. Hard.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_97.t: She looks you up and down, appraising you.
Not that you’d’ve succeeded if you tried. But in terms of saving face, it would’ve done wonders.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_99.t: Need anything else? Or can I get back to prepping for our next run?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_100.t: By the way... I am aware that it's more than a little ironic for me to be telling you this. After Monika's death, if you were the one who was dressing me down in front of Amsel and the rest...

523cb4ff346238d026006513_102.t: Anyway. You need anything else? Or can I get back to prepping for our next run?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_103.t: Good. That’s the right attitude. But be careful not to take it too far… you may not need your team’s approval, but you *do* need its respect. Out in the field, nothing will kill you faster than losing the respect of your team.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_105.t: Either you’re trying to be cute, or you’ve completely missed the point. Neither would surprise me.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_113.t: She pauses for a moment before continuing.
By the way... I am aware that it's more than a little ironic for me to be telling you this. After Monika's death, if you were the one who was dressing me down in front of Amsel and the rest...

523cb4ff346238d026006513_121.t: She shakes her head at you.
That's about how I expected you to respond. But it's disappointing, all the same. I didn't overreact, fearless leader. What I did was *necessary.* That's true, whether you can see it or not.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_123.t: Fair enough. I guess that you've earned it.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_129.t: Metzger. Best leader I've ever had the pleasure of serving under.
He was a hell of a man.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_131.t: No. No, that's one story that I won't be telling. They died. That's all that you need to know.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_133.t: All right, fearless leader. Ask away.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_136.t: It was a nice change of pace. A simple job with a straightforward objective.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_138.t: It's a pity that you completely botched it.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_145.t: Saving the clones was a bold move. It would've been easier to leave them where they were and consider them collateral damage.
But it was the right choice.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_154.t: She turns back to the disassembled components of her rifle.
Sure thing, fearless leader. Good talk.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_155.t: Eiger offers you a shallow nod.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_157.t: She pauses for a moment, considering. Finally, she nods.
Yeah. Yeah, I do. You've earned that much.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_160.t: All right. I'll tell you. I suppose that you deserve to know.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_170.t: I'll spare you the gory details. Suffice it to say that my team was wiped out.
Eiger's face clouds again. You can see the tears beginning to well in her eyes.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_171.t: With a visible effort, she clamps down on her emotions and calms herself. When she speaks again, her voice is flat.
I took three rounds myself. Had to drag myself back across the Oder-Neisse line into Germany. I almost died.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_173.t: She looks you in the eye. Her tone is impassive.
Did that tell you everything you wanted to know, fearless leader? Are you satisfied now?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_180.t: Interesting. And probably fair, at least in part. But you don't need to worry about my continued survival. I'll get along just fine, in the shadows or anywhere else.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_182.t: Normally, I'd call that a cop-out answer. But I guess that there's some truth to it. In any case, you don't need to worry about me, fearless leader. My baggage isn't gonna hurt my job performance or anything.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_183.t: All right. Well, was there anything else? Or are you just here to waste my time?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_188.t: It was a nice change of pace. A simple job with a straightforward objective.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_190.t: It's a pity that you completely botched it.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_197.t: Saving the clones was a bold move. It would've been easier to leave them where they were and consider them collateral damage.
But it was the right choice.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_207.t: What the hell do you want?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_208.t: Eiger eyes you warily. Her expression is guarded.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_210.t: Agreed. I'm ready to go over it if you are.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_211.t: A part of me is still having trouble believing it. But it's true... Yuli wasn't lying. I could see it in his eyes.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_214.t: Completely insane. Stories about magical nukes and the end of the world. Fenris shamans. Ragnarok. That kind of thing.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_215.t: Yes, there are, and no, it doesn't. But this... this is just too far out for me to accept. It's far more likely that they released these stories themselves to throw people off of whatever they're really doing.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_221.t: It's possible, I suppose, but I doubt it. Yuli was a bastard, but he was a careful bastard. Anyway, we stopped them, whatever they're up to.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_222.t: Yeah, could be. Anyway, we stopped them at Schattennest, and that's all that matters to me. Whatever their business is... whatever was going to be in those crates that the Engineer was going to have shipped here... it isn't our problem any more.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_224.t: True. But what I've been hearing about Winternight is straight-up mental. We're talking stories about magical nukes and the end of the world. Fenris shamans. Ragnarok. That kind of thing.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_226.t: That son of a bitch said a lot of things. What of it?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_229.t: It takes her a long time to respond. When she does, her voice carries a tone of resignation.
You really want to know? All right, fine. I'll tell you.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_234.t: I know. Sorry. I guess I'm still a little on edge about this... which is exactly what Yuli wanted, of course.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_235.t: You're right, I was out of line. I shouldn't be taking this out on you.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_237.t: Maybe. But I can't ignore the possibility that he *wasn't* playing me. That these things *did* happen.
That'd be a bitter pill to swallow.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_241.t: She gives you a brusque nod.
You're right. It isn't. No more moping about - I promise. And I *will* look into this. Wherever this trail leads me, I'll follow it, and I won't stop until I find the truth.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_242.t: Thanks, but no. Not now, at least. At some point, after we're done with what we're doing, I'll look into this. I'll get to the truth, whatever it might be. But I'm not going to do it today.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_245.t: Now, let's change tack. Is there anything else from the run that you'd like to go over?
She folds her arms across her chest.
If you have any thoughts, I want to hear them.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_247.t: I know it. And I'm glad.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_248.t: Me? Go soft? Not on your life, fearless leader. After we've dealt with our current predicament, I'll dig as deep as I need to to get to the bottom of this. For now, though, we should get back on task.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_250.t: At some point, after we're done with what we're doing, I'll look into this. I'll get to the truth, whatever it might be.
She looks you in the eye.
But I'm not going to do it today.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_251.t: I'm not running from anything, fearless leader. I'm going to look into this, wherever it leads me.
She looks you in the eye.
But I'm not going to do it today.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_256.t: Her lip curls into a smirk.
He did need it, didn't he? You're welcome, fearless leader. It was my pleasure.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_257.t: Uh. Well, okay... thank you. Was there something else that you wanted to ask about?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_258.t: The smirk falls away from her face. She sighs.
Yeah, I saw that coming. But with all due respect, it's none of your business, fearless leader.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_259.t: Yeah. It's a beautiful piece of engineering. Shoots straight, has good stopping power, holds a big magazine.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_263.t: This thing, though... this adds enough utility that I think it's worth the extra weight. I'll find a way to make it work.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_265.t: Anyway, enough talk. You'll see for yourself what this thing can do when I bring it into the field.
She slings the rifle over her shoulder.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_266.t: Yeah, actually. It wasn't too difficult to pull the thing apart and see what made it tick.
Making one of my own was a different story, but I got it in the end.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_271.t: She nods, and tucks the weapon back into her desk.
It is. You wouldn't want to be on the wrong end of one of these.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_272.t: For now, I've only managed to make one voltaic grenade... they aren't the easiest things in the world to manufacture, or the cheapest.
...But I should be able to scrounge up enough raw materials to put a new one together between each run.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_282.t: She crosses her arms over her chest and nods.
My thoughts exactly. And if it turns out that we were wrong, well... we'll just have to burn that bridge when we get there. We can't do anything about it now.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_284.t: She crosses her arms over her chest and nods.
Glad to hear that we're on the same page.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_290.t: It'd be lovely if there were ironclad proof just lying around to tie the Engineer to the Lobatchevskis, but it usually doesn't work that way. You work with the intel you have, and you make the best decisions that you can using that intel. I won't second-guess myself unless something comes up to make me question my conclusions.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_291.t: Good. And... thank you for trusting my assessment about the need for this mission. It *was* important, I promise you that.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_293.t: Your honesty is appreciated, fearless leader. Your tact could use some work, though. Now, is there anything else that you want to talk about?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_294.t: Good. And don't worry, I'll keep my ears open... if anything else comes up, I'll let you know. Now, is there anything else that you want to talk about?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_298.t: The image that greets you is a holopic of a rail-thin ork with jet-black hair.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_309.t: She bites her lip and stares at the image on the PDA for a long moment.
So, long story short... I'm going to have to step out for a mission or two.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_310.t: I know how important it is that we resolve the Feuerschwinge situation. I get it. But I can't just sit back and let this happen.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_313.t: Good. And thank you. Know that I wouldn't be doing this if it wasn't important.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_316.t: She looks you dead in the eye.
You really wanna tell me that we can't spare the time to stop all of that? That you don't think it's *important* enough?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_317.t: Her lip curls, and she shakes her head at you.

All right, fearless leader. You win. I'll stay here - for the team. But when we're done with this Feuerschwinge situation, I'm gone, you hear me? I won't hang around here to watch Schattennest fall, and I won't watch it drag the Kreuzbasar down with it.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_318.t: We stop him. Or rather, *I* stop him.
She looks you in the eye.
I can't ask you to put off our mission for this. But the Engineer has to be stopped, so I'm gonna have to disappear for a run or two.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_327.t: The image that greets you is a holopic of a rail-thin ork with jet-black hair.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_337.t: She bites her lip and stares at the image on the PDA for a long moment.
So. The question remains... what do we want to do about it?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_340.t: She claps you on the shoulder.
Grab your things and meet me at Der Weinkeller. I've got a contact who's gonna join us there, and he can help us figure out our next step.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_341.t: She looks conflicted, but she nods.
Yeah. Yeah, I can't deny that. So what do you propose?

523cb4ff346238d026006513_342.t: Her face flushes. She's clearly upset, but she keeps her cool.
You're right. We don't go off-mission. Not even for this. I just hope that by the time we *do* get around to it, it isn't too late.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_361.t: She nods again, her face expressionless.
There was nowhere else for me to go. Not after I did what I did. But I don't regret it. Not for a second.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_363.t: Yeah. I think that any of us would've. After everything that he did, he deserved to die.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_367.t: She pauses for a long moment, considering. Finally, her shoulders slump.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_371.t: You're right. I am. On some level, I think that I've known that for a long time now.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_372.t: I'm still out at the end of this thing... your decision to let Schattennest fall to the syndicates doesn't sit well with me. Not at all.
We don't see eye to eye, and we probably never will. But thanks for the talk, all the same.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_373.t: You're a good man. One that I'm proud to run with.
And speaking of which, we should probably get back to it.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_374.t: The corner of her mouth curls into a smile.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_375.t: She heaves a sigh, and the corners of her mouth dip down.
I've been fighting that conclusion for a long time. But you're right, of course. I haven't been a soldier for a long time. I'm a murderer for hire. It's time that I accepted it.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_376.t: I'm not proud of what I do. But for what it's worth, I am proud to do it in your company.
And speaking of which, we should probably get back to it.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_377.t: She nods, a humorless expression on her face.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_381.t: She nods again, her face expressionless.
It was a dark time. It has been ever since. But I don't regret what I did. Not for a second.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_382.t: She nods again, her face expressionless.
I couldn't go back. Not after I did what I did. But I don't regret it. Not for a second.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_388.t: It was a nice change of pace. A simple job with a straightforward objective.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_390.t: It's a pity that you completely botched it.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_397.t: Saving the clones was a bold move. It would've been easier to leave them where they were and consider them collateral damage.
But it was the right choice.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_406.t: Eiger glances up from her PDA at the sound of your approach.
Boss. This... this isn't the best of times.
Her body language is self-assured, but you can hear the anxiety in her voice. Something has her worried, and in a big way.

523cb4ff346238d026006513_407.t: Eiger welcomes you with a nod.
Boss. What can I do for you?

52449597346238a0240049c3_6.t: Eiger thrusts a gloved finger into your chest.
And THIS idiot stood by and let it happen.

52449597346238a0240049c3_9.t: Eiger turns to face Dietrich. She towers over him, but he stands his ground.
I respect you, Dietrich. You know that. But you don’t have my training. None of you have.

52449597346238a0240049c3_11.t: Eiger turns her focus back to you.
If you'd been paying attention, you'd have figured all of this out on your own by now. You'd've known that Monika needed watching as much as that door.

52449597346238a0240049c3_13.t: Eiger pushes ahead, heedless of the interruption. Her voice remains measured, but there's fire in her eyes.
How many seconds passed between Monika's first convulsion and her plug getting pulled? Four? Five? Do you know how much damage biofeedback can do to a decker's brain in five seconds?

52449597346238a0240049c3_44.t: All right. So Monika spent her dying breath trying to tell us about a long-dead dragon.
Eiger sweeps her eyes across the group, searching for a glimmer of insight. Finally, she gives up.

52449597346238a0240049c3_68.t: Eiger recoils in surprise, then breaks into a feral grin.
Take a swing, please. I'd *love* an excuse to cave your face in.

52449597346238a0240049c3_72.t: Under a bus is exactly where they belongs.
Eiger turns to face Dietrich. She towers over him, but he stands his ground.
I respect you, Dietrich... you know that. But you don’t have my training. None of you have.

524b4ce43030312c040004e9_10.t: At that moment, Eiger's image winks onto your commlink with a crackling sound. The image is grainy, flickering in and out.
What's going on down there, rookie? Talk to me!

524b4ce43030312c040004e9_12.t: Eiger’s silence is thick, but when she finally responds, her tone is professional.
She cuts out without another word.

524f13a03462384029001a91_0.t: Eiger rises from her corpse-strewn perch at your approach. From the look on her face, it's obvious that she's already sized up the situation.
I knew this was going to happen. I fucking knew it.

524f13a03462384029001a91_1.t: Eiger grits her teeth and shakes her head.

524f13a03462384029001a91_2.t: We can talk about this later. For now, we've gotta get the hell out of here. It's only a matter of time before more of them come pouring out of that exit.
Eiger slaps the side of the van.
Pile in, people. And boss... when we get back to the Kreuzbasar, we're gonna have a little talk.

524f13a03462384029001a91_3.t: Tell me something I don't know.
Eiger slaps the side of the van.
Pile in, people. And boss... when we get back to the Kreuzbasar, we're gonna have a little talk.

52532b32626164681700018f_19.t: Even if... even *if* we'd consider going off-mission...
She shoots you a significant glance.

52532b32626164681700018f_20.t: She turns back to Enstad, staring him down.
...Did you honestly think that we'd sacrifice our professional integrity AND a fifteen thousand nuyen payday for a trinket?

52532b32626164681700018f_26.t: Eiger holds her tongue, but her body language speaks volumes: tight lips, rigid pose, slight color in her pale cheeks. You've seen it before. She's furious.

5258897d6636614001005b04_5.t: Eiger kneels beside the corpse, bending to peer at his face.
Matches the description that Altuğ gave us. It's Green Winters.
She straightens, glancing back over at the body before returning her attention to you.

5258897d6636614001005b04_8.t: Eiger takes another glance at the smoldering corpse, and you can see the doubt growing in her eyes.

5258897d6636614001005b04_10.t: Eiger takes another glance at the broken corpse, and you can see the uncertainty growing in her eyes.
I've seen biofeedback deaths before. Never seen anything like this.

5258897d6636614001005b04_14.t: Eiger kneels beside the corpse, bending to peer at his face.
Matches the description Altuğ gave us. It's Green Winters. I don't see any signs of a struggle. If somebody did this to him while he was jacked in, they were quiet about it.

5258897d6636614001005b04_21.t: I don't even think that's possible.
As Eiger stares into Glory's cold, dead eyes, something in her face changes. All at once, you can see something that you've never seen in Eiger before: uncertainty.

5258897d6636614001005b04_24.t: Eiger kneels beside the corpse, bending to peer at his face.
Matches the description Altuğ gave us. It's Green Winters.
She straightens, glancing back over at the body before returning her attention to you.

525add9b3030315c17005fce_3.t: Stealth is always preferable to open combat.

52604be06361341023004911_57.t: Eiger cuts in. Her voice is soft.
Samuel Beckenbauer. He's a community organizer in the Kreuzbasar. He can get you the help that you need.

5266eef4663661b825002e69_14.t: Wait a second. We don't know for certain...

5266f911663661b825003110_52.t: That's a bit of a leap.

5266f911663661b825003110_56.t: Eiger holds her silence, but concedes the point with a small nod.

52675434626164703000513a_17.t: She turns to stare at you.
What have you gotten us into, fearless leader?

52675434626164703000513a_20.t: I don't like anything about this. But at the end of the day, I guess that it's your call. Make the right one, for all of our sakes.

52675434626164703000513a_46.t: Good to see you're making the right call.

52675434626164703000513a_73.t: Her mouth curls into a frown.
Pretty amateur hour of him to change things up at the last second like this.

52675434626164703000513a_74.t: All right. The second van it is. Come on, let's get moving.

526aaa786261648c3a004bb6_0.t: Eiger indicates a console built into the wall beside the elevator.
This looks like the elevator console. The camera feeds should have been routed here.

527a888f303031240b00306a_5.t: She turns to face the hulking drone APEX has provided.
And any security patrols in the courtyard, we've got this thing.

527a888f303031240b00306a_8.t: She nods, then looks to you.

527a888f303031240b00306a_43.t: With APEX down, the automated systems will be offline. We'll still have plenty of guards to deal with, though.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_47.t: No. It'll be even worse this time. The first time we went in, we had the element of surprise on our side. But now, they'll be expecting us. They're hunting us, remember?

527abf9e636134b83400302e_98.t: This smells like a trap to me. Why else would APEX have let Alice escape?
She turns to address the simulacrum on the screen.
Alice, why would a matrix warfare AI allow you to bring us this information?

527d88376636619c42003e15_0.t: The barrel of Eiger's rifle swings upward. She emerges from cover, turning to face you.

527d88376636619c42003e15_1.t: The light plays off of Eiger's face like a streetlight on rain-slick asphalt. Her face, neck, and chest are sheeted in slick, dark liquid. Blood - a lot of blood.

527d88376636619c42003e15_2.t: I'm not. This little bastard, on the other hand...

Eiger wipes her mouth with the back of her hand, then hoists a corpse up from behind her makeshift barricade. A young man, maybe in his early twenties, his eyes frozen wide in surprise. What's left of his throat hangs down in ragged flaps.

527d88376636619c42003e15_4.t: Even the best shooters have to reload sometime. Did you meet with Alice? Do you have our information?

527d88376636619c42003e15_7.t: Yeah, I know. He had us all patched in on that call. We all saw what happened.

527d88376636619c42003e15_8.t: I figured they'd try to ambush you at the platform... ventilate the train, take you out, and cut off a potential escape route in the process.
It's what I would do. So I hightailed it out here and set up an ambush of my own.

527d88376636619c42003e15_11.t: Anyway. That's enough talk... we've got a job to do.
Eiger swings her rifle up and over her shoulder.
There are an unknown number of hostiles topside. They're well equipped, decently trained, and hell-bent on killing us.

527d88376636619c42003e15_13.t: I'm not saying you aren't tough. But a U-Bahn train isn't exactly armored transport. They'd've punched you full of holes before you even reached the platform.

527d88376636619c42003e15_14.t: Save it. I'm here to protect the optical chip. That thing cost us fifty grand, after all.

527d88376636619c42003e15_16.t: She gestures down at the dead body on the floor.
Case in point. I figured they'd try to ambush you at the platform, so I high-tailed it out here and set up an ambush of my own.

52336c1638336110170073fa_60.t: I don't think it's anything to worry about. Besides, with the whole "dragon" thing, we've got enough on our plates already.

52336c1638336110170073fa_66.t: I'm glad that we got our hands on the Pain Inducer... that thing is amazing. I just wish that we didn't have to kill those company guys to get it. And letting Thorvald go... I'm not sure that was the right move, chief.

52336c1638336110170073fa_68.t: His eyes light up in excitement.

The MKVI was an *incredible* piece of engineering, chief. Never seen anything like it before. And the fact that it was rigger-enabled... oh, man. The potential ramifications of this technology are *staggering.*

52336c1638336110170073fa_74.t: On the plus side, he's dead now! So that's good.
I dunno, boss. If you were hoping for something more profound, I'd talk to one of the others.

52336c1638336110170073fa_77.t: Blitz stubs out his cigarette at your approach.
Chief. I was hoping I'd see you. Do you have another run lined up for us yet? I'm strapped for cash.

52336c1638336110170073fa_84.t: He tries on a thoroughly unconvincing smile, but immediately seems to think better of it. The anxious look returns to his face.

52336c1638336110170073fa_86.t: He hesitates before responding. When he does, his voice carries an edge of annoyance.
No offense, but this is getting a little too personal, chief. My debts are none of your business.

52336c1638336110170073fa_87.t: Blitz shakes his head in exasperation.

All right. Fine. First off, you don't need to worry about anyone coming after me. My debts are to a bunch of old contacts. If I don't pay 'em, I'm gonna lose 'em, and in this line of work it isn't smart to burn bridges.

52336c1638336110170073fa_91.t: Blitz meets your gaze for a moment, then looks away and sighs.
All right. Where to begin? I met Emilie in Drogenkippe. She was the first person who was ever more important to me than myself.

52336c1638336110170073fa_100.t: I looked high, I looked low. I poked into everything I could think of. And my contacts did the same.
She's gone, chief. At this point, I've accepted that. But I still have to repay those debts.

52336c1638336110170073fa_101.t: I dunno... I really *did* look everywhere, and at great personal cost...

52336c1638336110170073fa_102.t: Yeah... yeah, you might be right.
*Fuck.* I'd thought I was *done* with this. But you're right... without some kind of closure, I'm never going to be at peace with this.

52336c1638336110170073fa_106.t: Can't say I didn't warn you. Now do me a favor and get out of here, okay? Let me smoke in peace.

52336c1638336110170073fa_107.t: Well, the last thing *I* want is to talk about it, so we're even.
He offers you a lopsided grin.
Now do me a favor and get out of here, okay? Let me smoke in peace.

52336c1638336110170073fa_156.t: Thanks, chief. I knew that I could count on you. Now, if you'll excuse me, I've got some last-minute stuff to take care of before we get started.

52336c1638336110170073fa_157.t: All right, chief. Best of luck handling the dragon without me. You're gonna need it.

52336c1638336110170073fa_158.t: Yeah. I'm almost certain of it.

52336c1638336110170073fa_159.t: Yeah, chief. I mean, kind of. I think so.
I, uh... I got a package in the mail. It had somebody's thumbs in it.

52336c1638336110170073fa_160.t: There is an awkward pause, then Blitz plows on ahead.
Well, um. You remember those creditors I told you about? The ones who loaned me all of that money?

52336c1638336110170073fa_165.t: Oh, hey, chief! You're back! And you've got the box, right?
He takes an eager step toward you, his hand extended.
Give it here, if you please.

52336c1638336110170073fa_166.t: Hoi, chief! Thanks again for the help with that little side-run... I really do appreciate it.
He offers you a winning smile, but it falls almost immediately.
I, uh... I'm a happy guy. Really, I am.

52336c1638336110170073fa_168.t: Blitz turns the box over and over in his hands, a huge grin on his face.
Let's plug this puppy in and see what it does!

52336c1638336110170073fa_171.t: Slowly, a smile spreads across his face.
This thing is like a master key for communications systems. With this thing plugged into my deck, I could hack the commlink of just about anyone in the same room as me.

52336c1638336110170073fa_172.t: Use your imagination, chief! Just about every security goon in Berlin is wired to a comm system of some kind or another. If I can hijack those comms, then I can cause all sorts of confusion in the ranks on our runs. This is one hell of a toy. I can see why Grinder wanted it.

52336c1638336110170073fa_175.t: Yeah! Um, okay... sure, we can talk about it. Do a little post-mortem on the op, that sort of thing.

52336c1638336110170073fa_177.t: So, uh, anyway... was there anything in specific that you wanted to go over?
He drums his fingers on the table.

52336c1638336110170073fa_179.t: Oh, yeah, chief - it's wiz. I mean, like, *really* wiz. Old Plotz must've been a fair hand at programming, because this didn't come off of any store shelf. Y'know, he was pretty savvy for a dinosaur.

52336c1638336110170073fa_181.t: You won't have to. As soon as you bring me along on another run, I'll show you what I can do.
He cracks his knuckles with a smile.
And believe me, it will be *masterful.*

52336c1638336110170073fa_183.t: 'Course it was, chief! I'm shocked that you'd even ask! What'd you think, that I'd purposefully strand you in a bank vault? I mean, what kind of asshole would I have to be to do that?

52336c1638336110170073fa_184.t: Extenuating circumstances, chief. I would *never* do that to you.
He shakes his head from side to side.
Not unless you forced me to.

52336c1638336110170073fa_187.t: Hey, if you wanna be paranoid, you go ahead and knock yourself out. Now, is there anything else? Go ahead, get it out in the open... better that than letting it fester.

52336c1638336110170073fa_196.t: There is a pause, then Blitz looks you in the eye.

52336c1638336110170073fa_197.t: You just can't give it to me, can you, chief? You can't give me the satisfaction?

52336c1638336110170073fa_198.t: You need something else? Or can I get on with my day?

52336c1638336110170073fa_212.t: Yeah... yeah, that's fair. He's had a lot of experience at planning this sort of thing.
One advantage of being an old guy, I guess.

52336c1638336110170073fa_213.t: Yeah... yeah, that makes sense. He was really great at that, wasn't he? One advantage of being old, I guess... lots of experience.

52336c1638336110170073fa_214.t: Hey, I'm sorry about that "vault door" thing. I'll admit it, that was an oversight on my part.

52336c1638336110170073fa_215.t: Well, that's a relief. I mean, of course you do!

52336c1638336110170073fa_224.t: Blitz continues smiling at you for a few seconds, then looks away.
I, uh... I stole the file from Aztechnology.

52336c1638336110170073fa_225.t: A tinge of red crawls into Blitz's cheeks. He shuffles his feet.

52336c1638336110170073fa_229.t: I've gotta admit, this has me worried. I'm even more torn up now than I was before.

52336c1638336110170073fa_231.t: You're right. I've gotta pay her back for that... if this gets out, I'll never live it down.

52336c1638336110170073fa_234.t: My head *is* in the game, chief. Don't you worry about that.

52336c1638336110170073fa_237.t: Who am I kidding? You're right... I won't be able to stop thinking about this until I've found her. So I might as well keep trying. Thanks, chief. You've been a huge help.

52336c1638336110170073fa_238.t: Maybe you're right. I'll try to let it go.
I just hope that I'm wrong about all of this.

52336c1638336110170073fa_251.t: Blitz stares past you as he speaks, avoiding eye contact.

52336c1638336110170073fa_254.t: No, boss... 'course not. The debts don't mean a thing to me.

52336c1638336110170073fa_302.t: He frowns, then shakes his head.

52336c1638336110170073fa_303.t: He pauses for a moment, considering. Then, all at once, he seems to perk up. His chest puffs out, and he nods once, decisively.

52336c1638336110170073fa_307.t: Blitz claps his hands, then rubs them together in excitement.
Get set, chief. You're about to witness an *event.* When my *gung-ho personality* and *legendary skills* come together, the whole *world* stops and takes notice!

52336c1638336110170073fa_311.t: Thanks for the encouragement, chief. I knew that this was the right thing to do, of course, but you've helped to quiet any lingering doubts I might've had.

52336c1638336110170073fa_314.t: He recoils as if you'd punched him.

52336c1638336110170073fa_316.t: Blitz deflates before your eyes. He looks down at his shoes.

52336c1638336110170073fa_317.t: He nods excitedly. If he caught the dismissive tone of your words, he doesn't show it.

52336c1638336110170073fa_320.t: She's good. Better than I ever thought she was. I couldn't trace the damned thing. I *could* keep digging, though. I mean, if she noticed me doing it before, I must've been close.

52336c1638336110170073fa_321.t: Oh ye of little faith! Word to the wise, chief... when Blitz is on the job, you should always expect *results.*

I can, uh... I can tell you what her note said, if you want.

52336c1638336110170073fa_401.t: Well. Um. Emilie found me. And, um...

52336c1638336110170073fa_405.t: And that's about it. She's gone.

52336c1638336110170073fa_408.t: As he mouths the words, you can see the hope on his face flicker and die. If anything, he looks even more miserable than he did before.

52336c1638336110170073fa_409.t: He glances at you with red-rimmed eyes, then averts his gaze. Without a word, he turns the terminal so that you can read the message on the screen.

52940263636134d43100009a_170.t: He sneers at you through the glass.

Idle curiosity. That's your rationale. You were *curious.* I'd wager almost anything that over the next five years, your new friend will kill and absorb hundreds of deckers. That's what it was designed to do. Murder is in its *nature*.

52940263636134d43100009a_172.t: He sneers at you through the glass.

"Wrong." An interesting choice of words. I'd wager almost anything that over the next five years, your new friend will kill and absorb hundreds of deckers. That's what it was designed to do. Murder is in its *nature*.

52940263636134d43100009a_176.t: I promise you, Hermie... it will work.

52940263636134d43100009a_179.t: I am *not* a first-year biology student. I am at the forefront of my field, and I have told you, it *will* work! I've spent decades on this, studying it, *proving* its effectiveness!
Another spasm wracks his chest. He leans against the desk, gasping for breath.

52940263636134d43100009a_182.t: Vauclair stares at you through red-rimmed eyes for a long moment.
And acting on faith makes for bad science. That is your point, yes?

52940263636134d43100009a_183.t: If the draconic threat could be stopped by the "scientific community", we wouldn't be having this conversation. I won't deny the risks. But this...

52940263636134d43100009a_184.t: And it is a risk *worth taking*.

52940263636134d43100009a_185.t: This is wearying. I've heard these arguments before... more often than I can count. They were rejected *decades* ago.
The old man shakes his head at you.
You are wasting your breath. I advise you to accept this, and move on. It will all be over soon.

52940263636134d43100009a_186.t: This is wearying. I've heard these arguments before... more often than I can count. They were rejected *decades* ago.
The old man turns to study a monitor at his side, a stony expression on his face.
You are wasting your breath. I advise you to accept this, and move on. It will all be over soon.

52940263636134d43100009a_187.t: This is wearying. I've heard these arguments before... more often than I can count. They were rejected *decades* ago.
The old man turns to study a monitor at his side. He rubs the bridge of his nose between a pair of bony fingers. He looks tired.
You are wasting your breath. The Firewing *will* fly tonight. And you will watch it all from a screen, trapped in that room.

52940263636134d43100009a_188.t: This is wearying. I've heard these arguments before... more often than I can count. They were rejected *decades* ago.
The old man turns to study a monitor at his side. His hand tightens on a pack of cigarettes on his desk, crumpling it.
You are wasting your breath. The Firewing *will* fly tonight. And you will watch it all from a screen, trapped in that room.

52940263636134d43100009a_189.t: This is wearying. I've heard these arguments before... more often than I can count. They were rejected *decades* ago.
The old man turns to study a monitor at his side. His expression is stiff, but his motions are jerky. Erratic.
You are wasting your breath. The Firewing *will* fly tonight. And you will watch it all from a screen, trapped in that room.

52940263636134d43100009a_190.t: You are exactly right, shadowrunner. There is too much at stake. Too much at stake to not *act*.

52940263636134d43100009a_191.t: He takes another drag on his cigarette.

After all, science has always attracted its share of doomsayers. During the development of the atomic bomb, Compton and Oppenheimer expressed concern that a fission explosion could ignite the Earth's atmosphere. Fermi even took bets on it.

52940263636134d43100009a_192.t: The group's research later indicated that the "ultimate catastrophe" was unlikely, so they continued forward.
He takes a long drag on his cigarette.
Yes... I will show the same resolve that those great minds did. I will not be deterred.

52940263636134d43100009a_196.t: He shoots you an icy stare.
Say what you like - it matters little to me. My Panacea will change the world. In the end, you will thank me.

52940263636134d43100009a_199.t: My plan did not kill my brother.
Vauclair's fist slams into the table, sending his cigarette flying in a cascade of sparks and ash.
Your *ALLY* KILLED HIM! It ATE his MIND! And YOU SET IT FREE!

52940263636134d43100009a_200.t: He runs a hand through his unkempt hair, blinking through a haze of sudden tears. His voice comes out in a gasping sob.
Damn it, Hermie... why couldn't you just let me go?

52940263636134d43100009a_201.t: He blinks at you through red-rimmed eyes, and an expression of pure hate twists his weathered features. His voice goes cold.

Oh no, shadowrunner. You will *not* use my brother's death as tool against me. You've cast your lot with that... *thing.* You set it loose to kill with impunity. You are a moral leper, and I am finished with you.

52940263636134d43100009a_204.t: His voice thunders through the intercom.

I hold in my hands the power to end draconic life. To lift our species from the brink of disaster. To save everybody - even short-sighted fools such as yourself - from extinction. And I choose to exercise that power.

52940263636134d43100009a_206.t: Vauclair's fist slams into the table, sending his cigarette flying in a cascade of sparks and ash.
NO! *Nothing* can make that right. Nothing.

52940263636134d43100009a_207.t: He runs a hand through his unkempt hair, blinking through a haze of sudden tears.
Damn it, Hermie... why couldn't you just let me go?

52940263636134d43100009a_208.t: No... no. I'm afraid it does. I loved my brother, more than you can know, but...

52940263636134d43100009a_209.t: He wipes away the tears. Looks you in the eye.
This… this changes nothing. My brother’s death only strengthens my resolve. Were I to stop now, his loss would be in vain... he would want me to continue.

52940263636134d43100009a_212.t: His face twists into an ugly sneer.
You've watched *footage.* And that makes you think that you *know* the man. You know *nothing.*

52940263636134d43100009a_216.t: Vauclair straightens. He looks angry, but behind the anger you can sense a deep fatigue.

52940263636134d43100009a_217.t: Vauclair straightens. It looks like you've touched a nerve - his entire body is trembling with fury. He takes a deep breath to calm himself.

52940263636134d43100009a_218.t: Vauclair straightens. His expression is livid, but his body language tells a different story... the rage that shook him seems to have burned itself out. Now, he just looks tired.

52940263636134d43100009a_219.t: Vauclair straightens. He rubs the bridge of his nose between a pair of bony fingers. The fire that burned in him seems to have guttered out. Above all, he looks worn down.

52940263636134d43100009a_221.t: Vauclair straightens, but only partially. His expression is stiff, but his motions are jerky. Erratic. He waits for a long time before looking up again.

52940263636134d43100009a_225.t: My brother is *dead,* and you make light of it. I suppose that I shouldn't be surprised. Rest assured, Berlin will burn tonight. And my brother - wherever he is - will understand.

52940263636134d43100009a_226.t: A dry chuckle turns into a rasping cough.

I'll take your opinions on what is right with a grain of salt, shadowrunner. When viewed through the lens of history, my Panacea will be remembered as the key to our species' salvation. I won't live to see it, but it will happen, regardless of your beliefs to the contrary.

52940263636134d43100009a_227.t: It is not MURDER, shadowrunner. It is *sacrifice.* Murder is selfish. But sacrifice... to sacrifice is to do that which is *necessary.*

52940263636134d43100009a_228.t: He stares at you for a long moment, then shakes his head in resignation.
Why am I even talking to you? What do I hope to accomplish? You will never understand. Besides which, I have work to do.

52940263636134d43100009a_231.t: His voice thunders through the intercom, cutting you off.
But *this* solution is *here.* Now. In my hands. And I choose to take it.

52940263636134d43100009a_233.t: A pointless argument. Any power vacuum created through dragonkind’s extermination will be filled by metahumanity. Any backlash will right itself over time.

52940263636134d43100009a_235.t: He uses the tip of his cigarette to gesture at the glass.
So says the career criminal trapped in the containment room.

52940263636134d43100009a_236.t: We have suffered losses, yes. They were tragic. But the people here accepted that there would be risks. They made their own choices. I won't take the blame for that.

52940263636134d43100009a_238.t: He glares at you through the glass.
Is there some point that you're trying to make? Or are you just trying to anger me?

52940263636134d43100009a_240.t: The muscles at the corners of his mouth tighten.
Why am I even talking to you? You're trapped in there, and I've still got work to do. Audran will be here for you soon enough.

52940263636134d43100009a_241.t: The old man turns away from you, studying some invisible detail on his console.

52940263636134d43100009a_242.t: The old man turns away from you, studying some invisible detail on his console. He looks irritated.

52940263636134d43100009a_243.t: The old man turns away from you, studying some invisible detail on his console. He looks irritated, and tired.

52940263636134d43100009a_244.t: The old man turns away from you, studying some invisible detail on his console. He rubs the bridge of his nose between a pair of bony fingers. The fire that burned in him seems to have guttered out. Above all, he looks worn down.

52940263636134d43100009a_245.t: The old man turns away from you, studying some invisible detail on his console. His hand tightens on a pack of cigarettes on his desk, crumpling it. He waits there a long time before looking up again.

52940263636134d43100009a_246.t: The old man turns away from you, studying some invisible detail on his console. His motions are jerky. Erratic. He waits there a long time before looking up again.

52940263636134d43100009a_247.t: He sneers at you, parchment-thin skin sliding over his bony face.
You've succeeded. I think that I'm just about done with you. And besides, why am I talking to you? You're trapped in there, and I've still got work to do. Audran will be here for you soon enough.

52940263636134d43100009a_248.t: He grinds his teeth in exasperation.

Another pearl of wisdom from the shadowrunner. The Great Dragons represent an incalculable threat to our survival as a species. To spare them for fear of some unknown, nebulous danger would be absurd. Besides which, there is *nothing* that is worse than a dragon. I am in a position to know.

52940263636134d43100009a_249.t: He lets out a startled chuckle.
What does that matter? Even if your spirit's stories are true, the *dragons* are what threaten us now.

52940263636134d43100009a_251.t: I will *not* just sit back and let them take over the world. I must act.
His bony hands clench into fists.

52940263636134d43100009a_258.t: A crude analogy... but yes. All things being equal, a careful approach would be best. Unfortunately, all things are *not* equal. We don't have time to deal with each dragon on an individual basis. We must wipe them out in a single stroke, and *that* is what Panacea will do.

52940263636134d43100009a_259.t: He sneers at you, parchment-thin skin sliding over his bony face.
Well, in that case, perhaps I should get back to work. I wouldn't want to *bore* you. It's past time that this conversation came to an end.

52940263636134d43100009a_264.t: He slams his fists against the glass, his face contorted into a mask of rage and grief.
HOW *DARE* YOU PRESUME TO SPEAK FOR MY BROTHER! To hell with letting you live - you're going to have *front and center* seats for Feuerschwinge's return! I will see to it MYSELF! I'm... I...

52940263636134d43100009a_268.t: The old man continues to stare at his feet. The blood has drained from his cheeks, leaving them sickly pale.
If Hermie were here, he would not support this. He would point out the risks, the unknowns, the sacrifices... much as he always had, before he--

52940263636134d43100009a_269.t: Vauclair's jaw clenches. The muscles of his throat tighten as he forces himself to swallow.

52940263636134d43100009a_271.t: Vauclair remains silent for some time. Finally, the old man begins to speak. He sounds hollowed-out. Gutted.
For over forty years, I ignored the rest of the world. I ignored the media and the military, the Board of Directors at Diehl and my little brother. No - *especially* my little brother.

52940263636134d43100009a_273.t: He makes a sweeping gesture, a look of contempt on his face.

You see this lab? The complex you're standing in, and the plan that it was built to serve? I sacrificed *everything* for this. My job, my health, my family... I was prepared to sacrifice *Berlin.* To feed those people to the same fires that I spent my youth trying to extinguish.

52940263636134d43100009a_274.t: And all the while, Hermie was trying to tell me that I *wasn't* right, and I refused to listen. My refusal killed him - my little brother. The person I loved most in the world.
He covers his face with a withered hand.
If only I had heard him... none of this would have happened.

52940263636134d43100009a_279.t: He stares at his trembling hands, a sad smile on his face.

More than four decades of planning, all undone in the span of a single conversation. My brother's words, delivered through the mouth of a criminal. I finally listened, Hermie. Forty-two years too late.

52940263636134d43100009a_284.t: A bitter half-grin spreads across his face.
You still plan to kill me? Good.
His hands curl into fists, and he slams one down on his control panel. A hollow ringing sound fills the air, and Vauclair winces in pain.
I have nothing more to live for anyway. You'd be doing me a favor.

52940263636134d43100009a_285.t: Bitterness creeps into the old man's voice. He sounds hollowed-out. Gutted.
For over forty years, I ignored the rest of the world. I ignored the media and the military, the Board of Directors at Diehl and my little brother. No - *especially* my little brother.

52940263636134d43100009a_294.t: He stares at his hands, trembling.
I must admit, your arguments have been... quite compelling. Enough to make me question my own conclusions. So why the sudden change of heart?

52940263636134d43100009a_295.t: Oh? And tell me, why the sudden change of heart?

52940263636134d43100009a_309.t: The drone's minigun spins up with a hum.

52940263636134d43100009a_310.t: The old man shoots you a withering look, then turns his head to cough into his sleeve.
As I thought. Another waste of time. Why am I talking to you at all? There's still work to be done.

52940263636134d43100009a_316.t: PANZERDROHNE pivots on its axis to face you.
YOU WILL NOT ASSIST VAUCLAIR, $++(l.name). YOU WILL ELIMINATE HIM WITH MY ASSISTANCE.

52940263636134d43100009a_318.t: PANZERDROHNE pivots on its axis to face you.
YOU WILL NOT ASSIST VAUCLAIR, $++(l.name). YOU WILL ELIMINATE HIM WITH MY ASSISTANCE.

52940263636134d43100009a_321.t: PANZERDROHNE pivots on its axis to face you.
YOU WILL NOT ASSIST VAUCLAIR, $++(l.name). YOU WILL ELIMINATE HIM WITH MY ASSISTANCE.

52940263636134d43100009a_327.t: PANZERDROHNE pivots on its axis to face you.
YOU WILL NOT ASSIST VAUCLAIR, $++(l.name). YOU WILL ELIMINATE HIM WITH MY ASSISTANCE.

52940263636134d43100009a_330.t: The old drone pivots on its axis to face you.
NEGATIVE. YOU WILL NOT ASSIST VAUCLAIR. YOU WILL ELIMINATE HIM WITH MY ASSISTANCE.

52940263636134d43100009a_343.t: You're right, shadowrunner. My plan *is* insane. But no more so than the world that we live in.
He turns to tap a series of buttons on his control console, then lights another cigarette.
Desperate times call for desperate measures. Nothing ventured, nothing gained - history has repeatedly shown this to be true.

52940263636134d43100009a_346.t: Still tapping at his console, his speech patterns shift. His words sound well-rehearsed, and he delivers them as a dry, pedantic monologue.

...After all, science has always attracted its share of doomsayers. During the development of the atomic bomb, Compton and Oppenheimer expressed concern that a fission explosion could ignite the Earth's atmosphere. Fermi took bets on it.

52940263636134d43100009a_347.t: The group's research later indicated that the "ultimate catastrophe" would not happen, so they continued forward.
He takes a long drag on his cigarette.
I will show the same resolve that those great minds did. I will not be deterred.

52940263636134d43100009a_348.t: His voice thunders through the intercom, cutting you off.
Of *course* I wish that there were a better way to save us. One without such a high human cost. I wish that Hermie hadn't had to die.

52940263636134d43100009a_349.t: But you go to war with the weapons you have. And Panacea is the best solution that I've been able to find. We won't get another chance like this - not any of us. I have to take it.
Through the speakers, you can hear the sound of heavy footsteps approaching.

52940263636134d43100009a_356.t: Vauclair stops. Bows his head, and closes his eyes.
I am a man of science, shadowrunner. A better path may exist, but I do not know what it is. I wish that I didn’t have to sacrifice so many. I wish... I wish that Hermie hadn't had to die.

52940263636134d43100009a_357.t: But this solution is here. Now. In my hands. And I choose to take it.
Through the speakers, you can hear the sound of heavy footsteps approaching.

52940263636134d43100009a_378.t: Vauclair sighs. Shuts his eyes tight, brings a shaking hand to his temple.
I'm so sorry. But this sacrifice cannot be helped.

52940263636134d43100009a_379.t: He continues his work, filled with a feverish energy.
Sadly, there is not. If there were, I would gladly take it. For Panacea to work, Berlin must burn. A tragic sacrifice, but one made for the greater good.

52940263636134d43100009a_383.t: Good or bad is immaterial. I do what is *necessary.*

52940263636134d43100009a_384.t: I am doing what is *necessary.* And in case you've forgotten, *you* are trapped and powerless to do anything.

52940263636134d43100009a_385.t: He trembles, wracked again by violent coughing. As the coughing subsides, he takes another long drag on his cigarette, the ash creeping closer and closer towards his mouth.

52940263636134d43100009a_389.t: You want to know what happens tonight that badly? What your friends died for... what Hermie died for...? Fine.
Vauclair turns his attention back to the computer, stubbing out his cigarette. He grabs a fresh one from the pack and lights it.

52940263636134d43100009a_390.t: Vauclair starts, and the sudden movement of his arm sends his overflowing ashtray flying from the table in a cloud of ash.
My brother? Hermie? It's been years... Have you seen him? How is he?

52940263636134d43100009a_391.t: Vauclair starts, and the sudden movement of his arm sends his overflowing ashtray flying from the table in a cloud of ash. A look of dread fills his face.
Up until... the end? What do you mean? Where is Hermie? Explain yourself!

52940263636134d43100009a_392.t: Vauclair starts, and the sudden movement of his arm sends his overflowing ashtray flying from the table in a cloud of ash. A look of dread fills his face.
Blood... bloody corpse? What do you mean? Where is Hermie? Explain yourself!

52940263636134d43100009a_395.t: I know that I have wronged you, and for this I am sorry.
He sighs a long, rattling sigh that quickly turns into a violent fit of coughing. The sound echoes over the loudspeaker.

52940263636134d43100009a_399.t: Nevertheless, a life is still a life.
He covers his face with his pale, nicotine-stained hands.
Sending APEX and Audran after you was my doing, and I accept responsibility for that.

52b20f7b626164081c0068f6_7.t: Damn it. Damn it to hell.
Vauclair takes a drag on his cigarette. The smoke spills out of his mouth as he speaks.
This isn't good, Audran. Without the AI's protection, this facility is vulnerable. We don't have much time.

52b20f7b626164081c0068f6_65.t: Damn it. Damn it to hell.
Vauclair takes a drag on his cigarette. The smoke spills out of his mouth as he speaks.
This isn't good, Audran. Without the AI's protection, this facility is vulnerable. We don't have much time.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_0.t: Vauclair looks up at the ork. His voice trembles slightly.
There has been a change of plans.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_6.t: This must come as a shock to you. I am sorry for that.
Your belief in our cause was second only to my own.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_10.t: Vauclair stares at you, a pensive expression on his face.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_11.t: He turns to face his huffing security captain.
You're late, Audran. And there have been some... unexpected developments in your absence.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_13.t: The old man gestures at you with a frail arm.
Now, as for our friend here...

52d1da4c636134f049006669_17.t: There was some... disagreement... within the group.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_18.t: A sacrifice for the greater good. Just like the one that I'm about to make.
Now you understand the weight that I must carry. It isn't fun, is it?

52d1da4c636134f049006669_19.t: Without waiting for a response, he stubs out his last cigarette.
Audran, watch over our guest, if you will. See to it that they's well treated - they's a friend now. I will begin the Panacea infusion process.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_20.t: Vauclair stubs out his last cigarette, then turns to Audran.
Watch over our guest, if you will. I will begin the Panacea infusion process. And Audran... see to it that their stay here is comfortable. No mistreatment.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_22.t: As Vauclair turns to leave, he gives you one final backward glance.
You've made the right choice, shadowrunner. Just sit on your hands for the next few hours, and I promise you: the next time you set foot out of this lab, the world will be a different place.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_37.t: Vauclair brings his cigarette to his mouth and inhales sharply, holding it for a long moment.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_38.t: It is time, Hermie... let's do some good.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_39.t: Vauclair stubs out his last cigarette with a heavy sigh.
I will begin the infusion process. Audran, please make sure our guests here are securely locked away before you come down.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_40.t: The old man glares at you through the glass.

I go now to do a hard thing - perhaps even an evil thing. But a thing that is *necessary,* for the good of us all. And as for you, shadowrunner... I suggest that you sit back and wait for the inevitable. It won't be long now.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_43.t: Fire flashes in Vauclair’s eyes. His back straightens.
You do NOT know me, shadowrunner, nor can you possibly understand me. What I've been through. What I know with *absolute certainty.*

52d1da4c636134f049006669_47.t: Fire flashes in Vauclair’s eyes. His back straightens.
And what do you presume to know of dragons? I have spent my *entire life* studying them.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_51.t: Oh, he may create a dynasty that will stand for a time, but his dust will not reap its benefit.
Vauclair's voice is steady now. For a moment, his age, illness, and fatigue fall away.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_55.t: Fire flashes in Vauclair’s eyes. His back straightens.
You do NOT know me, shadowrunner, nor can you possibly understand me. What I've been through. What I know with *absolute certainty.*

52d1da70636134f049006672_0.t: My investors. A consortium of economic and political interests from around the globe. They bankrolled the project after my own fortune was depleted.

52d1da70636134f049006672_1.t: And now, they’re going to deploy my weapon. They plan to flood the Firewing’s body with the stuff and set her loose, collateral damage be damned. (
He shakes his head in resignation.
An atrocity. A calamity even worse than the one that I wanted to save us from.

52d1da70636134f049006672_2.t: He shakes his head. A palpable aura of bitterness surrounds him.

Our cause is just. I’d thought that our supporters were just as well. I was a *fool.* These people don't care about the *human* cost of their actions. All that they see is the power vacuum that the death of the dragons will create, and their only care is in how they'll exploit it.

52d1da70636134f049006672_6.t: Vauclair fixes the ork with a hateful stare.
Never, Audran. I will *not* be a part of this!

52d1da70636134f049006672_7.t: Spittle flies as Vauclair's hoarse voice turns into a desperate shriek.

52d1da70636134f049006672_18.t: He knocks twice on the transplast window.
Best of luck with that. You're not going *anywhere.*

52d604ba6361342c3f0063f9_5.t: He continues to stare at you.

52d604ba6361342c3f0063f9_8.t: His lip curls into a sneer.

Somehow, I doubt that. And speaking of which, did the Doc tell you that you're standing in the animal research lab? It's overflowing with test subjects, and they don't play well with others. Nasty, dangerous things. And it's well past their feeding time.

52d604ba6361342c3f0063f9_10.t: And if *I* die? Well, so what?
He holds his scars to the light.
You think that life has been a bundle of laughs for me? I'll happily trade mine to make a different world.

52d604ba6361342c3f0063f9_18.t: He pauses for a moment, considering.
You probably shouldn't count on that, though.

52d604ba6361342c3f0063f9_30.t: The armored ork turns to stare at you through the glass.
The Doc may trust you, but I don't. So you're gonna sit right there where I can see you, and you aren't gonna move until Berlin burns to the ground.

52d604ba6361342c3f0063f9_33.t: All right. You say that you're gonna stand down and let the Doc do his thing? Fine.
He shifts into a relaxed posture.
Let's just sit here, then. You don't make any sudden moves, and I don't start pushing buttons.

52d604ba6361342c3f0063f9_34.t: He glances at a chronometer mounted to his wrist.
It shouldn't take long now. The Doc is probably already pumping the dragon's body full of live virus.

52f2877c323437940500498c_0.t: Once again, a transplast window separates you from Adrian Vauclair. But this time, his steadfast will is gone. He stands motionless - hands at his sides, eyes dead. Spent. The strength that drove him, the steely resolve that couldn’t be shaken by sickness or time or age, is gone.

52f575e4643431e11418ab00_3.t: A heavy sigh comes over the speakers.
Are we resorting to name-calling? Truly? You are a thief, a murderer, and a criminal. You have no moral ground here. How many people did you and your team butcher to get here? Dozens, easily.

52f575e4643431e11418ab00_4.t: A heavy sigh comes over the speakers.
