52f575e4643431e11418ab00_7.t: A heavy sigh comes over the speakers.

I must admit that I have thought that very thing in my weaker hours. In the morning, as my dreams recede and conscious thought returns. In that gray state, I’ve lain in my bed, alone, and wrestled with the thought.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_2.t: Vauclair takes a long drag on his cigarette. The taut muscles in his neck relax somewhat as he lets loose a great stream of smoke.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_5.t: APEX. It's a testament to your fortitude that you've survived this long. That thing - that *monster* - does not forget. It has made several attempts on my own life as well.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_8.t: He takes a drag on his cigarette.
Sounds like you've made a hard life for yourself. My condolences.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_9.t: Calm yourself. You are safe here with me.

Short of employing a tactical nuclear weapon, APEX cannot touch us down here... and that is unlikely, to say the least.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_15.t: I *need* you to help me set things *right.* To help me save the world, once again.

My body may be failing, but I still have my mind. We can find a way out of this... there has to be a solution.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_17.t: A wave of relief washes over his face.
Thank you. I was worried that you wouldn't come... that you wouldn't be willing to help once you got here. I should have trusted you, my friend.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_18.t: The first thing you'll need to do is get up to speed. I have terminals... information terminals throughout the lab. With our Matrix hard line severed, gathering information has been difficult, but we have done what we can.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_19.t: The first thing you'll need to do is get up to speed. I have terminals... information terminals throughout the lab.

There, I've unlocked them for you.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_24.t: The world is crumbling around you, and all that you care about is money.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_27.t: He stares at you. His face may look haggard, but his eyes are as sharp as ever. Abruptly, he nods.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_28.t: Vauclair shakes his head in exasperation. He looks very, very tired.
Very well. Fine. Money holds no value for me anymore... and it won't for you, either, unless we can find a way to stop what is happening.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_29.t: Don't be petty. My team is smaller than it once was. I *need* Audran, just as I need you. I have to set things right. To save the world, once again. And I need you to help me.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_33.t: That is your prerogative, I suppose.

Anyway, you have nothing to worry about now. Short of employing a tactical nuclear weapon, APEX cannot touch us down here... and that is unlikely, to say the least.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_34.t: He flicks the ash from the tip of his cigarette.
Well, you have nothing to worry about now. Short of employing a tactical nuclear weapon, APEX cannot touch us down here... and that is unlikely, to say the least.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_36.t: Oh, yes. Over the past few months it has made several attempts on my life. Thankfully, I am rather well protected down here. Living in a contained environment has its advantages.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_38.t: Yes, I'm sure that you are.
Vauclair takes a long drag on his cigarette. The taut muscles in his neck relax somewhat as he lets loose a great stream of smoke.
You helped me save the world once before. Or at least, you didn't stand in my way.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_41.t: He leans forward, intensity burning in his eyes.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_45.t: He turns to stare at you.

You're confusing correlation with causation. A childish mistake... motivated, no doubt, by latent feelings of guilt over the deaths of your friends. A great many tragic events have transpired in the past year, yes. But to pin them all on *our* actions without a shred of supporting evidence is both narcissistic and ridiculous.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_46.t: He turns to study his terminal, frowning.
Get your head in the game, and drop the notion that what's happening is all about you.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_50.t: Stubborn as an ox, just like Audran. I will give you an alternative theory to entertain.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_56.t: Conventional weapons haven't done much to hurt them, I'm afraid. Even the small ones have shown an extraordinary resilience to physical damage, and bigger ones that have been popping up are nigh unkillable. There was an amoeboid thing with many tongues terrorizing the skies above Buenos Aires... they destroyed half the city trying to bring it down.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_58.t: Maybe a biological control could work? Perhaps a natural enemy could be found to fight these things... or, at the very least, to buy us some time.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_59.t: No. It took me over forty years to develop Panacea, and I'll be lucky to live another six months. Besides which, the physiology of these creatures is so alien that I wouldn't know where to begin.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_60.t: He stubs out his cigarette and grabs another with a shaking hand. Lighting it, he eagerly sucks in the smoke.
What we need is something powerful enough to keep these things at bay, but intelligent enough to be capable of reason.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_61.t: He stubs out his cigarette and grabs another with a shaking hand. Lighting it, he eagerly sucks in the smoke.
What we need is something powerful enough to keep these things at bay, but intelligent enough to be capable of reason.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_63.t: Vauclair stands there for a moment, blinking. Then his frail body begins to tremble. The cigarette drops from his lip and falls to the floor.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_64.t: Saucer-eyed, he covers his mouth with a withered hand.
Oh, God. We did this. *I* did this.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_68.t: He stares down at his trembling hands.
I acted without considering all of the variables... didn't even consider that such variables could exist! I was *blind!*

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_69.t: He holds his silence for a long while, struggling with himself. Finally, a quiet voice crawls out of him.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_76.t: He smiles weakly at you, but there are tears pouring from his eyes.
I didn't save the world. I damned it.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_77.t: At least I know that, when I arrive in Hell, I'll be in good company.
His finger tightens on the trigger. The breath catches in his throat.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_78.t: He smirks at you, but his hands are still trembling.

You're trying to get under my skin. To rub my nose in this, yes? Well, the joke is on you. I've already accepted the consequences of my actions. I own them; they are mine to atone for, and mine alone.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_79.t: Nothing can make this right. Nothing at all. There is no "fixing" this. I can only atone for it. And so I shall.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_81.t: He stares down at his trembling hands.
I acted without considering all of the variables... didn't even consider that such variables could exist! I was *blind!*

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_82.t: He spits his response out at you, his eyes burning.
Lay blame if you like. You were *with* me on this, shadowrunner.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_83.t: He gulps in another deep, rattling breath. Calms himself. When he speaks again, his voice comes out in a wooden monotone.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_84.t: The... the dragons. Don't you *see?* The dragons... they must have been keeping these things in check! And I *killed* them!

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_86.t: There was an amoeboid thing with many tongues terrorizing the skies above Buenos Aires... they tried to use helicopter gunships to shoot it down. They eventually succeeded, but not without destroying half of their own city in the process.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_89.t: There was an amoeboid thing with many tongues terrorizing the skies above Buenos Aires... they tried to use helicopter gunships to shoot it down. They eventually succeeded, but not without destroying half of their own city in the process.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_90.t: He leans forward, intensity burning in his eyes.

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_94.t: You didn't read my research, did you?

53b9f7a63462385c1b00094a_100.t: He shoots you an irritable glance.
Don't worry about me, I won't be keeling over anytime today. Now read up so that we can move on to talking about solutions.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_291.t: The text field disappears. A single line of text pops onto the center of the screen, taking its place.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_312.t: The text field disappears. A single line of text pops onto the center of the screen, taking its place.

522a1c5c30303174140018d7_7.t: You know... when I told you to enter the facility quietly, I did not mean "start a bloody firefight in the streets outside of the building."

Frau Müller should be on her way, but before you go to the meeting site, I have news...

523b948f346238e41d007555_29.t: Under Amsel's patient exterior, you can feel the worry that's eating at him.

523b948f346238e41d007555_32.t: Amsel pulls open a file on his computer. A medical chart opens up on the screen.

523b948f346238e41d007555_38.t: Amsel studies your face as you step back from the computer.
Your thoughts? Hold nothing back... I'd like to hear everything that comes to mind.

523b948f346238e41d007555_42.t: Amsel pauses for a moment before continuing.
In my mind, all of this reinforces one fact: we need to find Vauclair, and we need to do it fast.

523b948f346238e41d007555_44.t: There is a buzzing sound from Paul's wrist. He lifts his hand to look at his PDA.
...And, as luck would have it, Frau Müller has arrived. Best of luck at the meeting. Remember... this is urgent. We *need* that money.

523b948f346238e41d007555_58.t: Indeed. If Audran were a denizen of the SOX - a glowpunk - his lack of identifying information would make sense.

523b948f346238e41d007555_62.t: The information dries up at this point. We know that he was treated and discharged, but what happened afterward remains a mystery.

Your thoughts? Hold nothing back... I'd like to hear everything that comes to mind.

523b948f346238e41d007555_67.t: Amsel adjusts his glasses. Straightens his posture.

Now... on to other - albeit connected - matters. As I was saying when you first approached, I was able to uncover some new evidence in your absence. And coincidentally enough, that evidence pertains to our scarred friend.

523b948f346238e41d007555_68.t: He shakes his head in exasperation.
Your attitude is trying. This is *serious.* I'd expect that you'd treat it as such.

523b948f346238e41d007555_69.t: Very well. I managed to pull a file off of the PDA before it crashed. As I was saying. It looks to be a comm file. The audio is all here, but we've only got video from one side of the conversation.

523b948f346238e41d007555_70.t: His eyes shoot daggers at you.

523b948f346238e41d007555_71.t: No, we can't. But as I said, I did manage to copy a file, so it's not a complete loss. It looks to be a comm file... the audio is all here, but we've only got video from one side of the conversation.

523b948f346238e41d007555_75.t: Yes. This is something that you all need to hear.

523b948f346238e41d007555_80.t: The stories that you've heard are correct. This... "Doom"... is an extremely dangerous substance.

523b948f346238e41d007555_83.t: Amsel clears his throat and reads aloud from the information on his screen.
"Doom kills by destroying a host's muscle tissue via radioactive decay.

523b948f346238e41d007555_93.t: Amsel is slow to respond. Finally, he nods.

523b948f346238e41d007555_112.t: That is correct. The Azzies control the majority of the district, and you will find their research facility at the heart of it.

523b948f346238e41d007555_126.t: He fishes in his pocket for something. A moment later, he produces a corporate ID with your face on it.

523b948f346238e41d007555_131.t: As team leader, that is your prerogative. You may pursue other contracts instead if you wish. But remember... this job pays well, and we need the money. You can reach the offices of OTK International via the U-Bahn if you change your mind.

523b948f346238e41d007555_134.t: It's your call. I will leave the file in the mission computer in case you change your mind.

523b948f346238e41d007555_135.t: Have you met with Frau Müller?

523b948f346238e41d007555_138.t: Amsel peers at you apprehensively. His eyes are bloodshot, his expression grim.
Did you get the information about Green Winters?

523b948f346238e41d007555_139.t: Let us see what his agent has to say.

523b948f346238e41d007555_169.t: Amsel speaks softly, but his tone is firm.

523b948f346238e41d007555_178.t: Amsel turns his attention away from you and back to his computer screen.
Good hunting. I will eagerly await your return.

Is there something that I can do for you?

523b948f346238e41d007555_197.t: Amsel glances at the files on your PDA. After a moment, he looks back up at you with a raised eyebrow.

523b948f346238e41d007555_205.t: Amsel sucks in a deep breath. He suddenly looks as though an enormous weight has been lifted from his shoulders.
Yes. Very good. Excellent, in fact. This has been a long time coming.

523b948f346238e41d007555_206.t: Amsel turns to open a file on his computer.
All right. Before you make this transfer, there are a few things that you should know.

523b948f346238e41d007555_214.t: He breathes a sigh of relief.

523b948f346238e41d007555_218.t: Now please, go fetch the information from Alice.

523b948f346238e41d007555_228.t: Besides, what other choice do we have? We cannot run, and money will do us little good if we're dead.

523b948f346238e41d007555_230.t: That is your prerogative. But it's the way it has to be.

Moving forward. Once you're en route to the Rabbit Hole, it's go time. I trust that you understand what I mean by that.

523b948f346238e41d007555_231.t: Amsel bristles slightly, but keeps his cool.
Yes. Of course. You've raised Alice's fee with admirable expedience, despite your obvious social... issues. But I am very much looking forward to bringing this entire sorry episode to a close.

523b948f346238e41d007555_233.t: He claps a hand on your shoulder.
Well, my friend. The wait is over now.

523b948f346238e41d007555_235.t: Are you absolutely sure? Again, I will cancel all of your remaining jobs once you've left for the Rabbit Hole.

523b948f346238e41d007555_237.t: If you have questions about your teammates, I'd recommend checking your mission computer... I know that Monika wrote dossiers on each of them.

523b948f346238e41d007555_246.t: Yes, very good. This is probably the healthiest attitude to take about those who supply our community.

523b948f346238e41d007555_254.t: Amsel lets out a weary sigh.

Yes, yes. There is a guy. He calls himself "Zaak Flash," and he's more of a street person than anything. 523b948f346238e41d007555_256.t: Very well. Is there anything else that you need, or...?

523b948f346238e41d007555_257.t: Amsel returns his attention to the papers scattered across his desk.

523f37cb303031540c003c07_3.t: There is a pause, then Amsel continues.
There's no easy way to say this. I'm afraid that the Knight Errant High Threat Response Team is on its way.

52449597346238a0240049c3_1.t: Something bad has happened, hasn't it?

He steps forward, revealing a pale and expressionless face, light glinting off of steel-rimmed glasses. Paul Amsel. Your team's fixer and landlord. Part deal-maker, part information broker. One of the most well-connected men in Berlin.

52449597346238a0240049c3_3.t: I had a feeling. How did she...?
His face has gone ashen. He swallows. Takes a moment to chew on the words before spitting them out.

52449597346238a0240049c3_12.t: Amsel's voice is hoarse, his expression blank.

52449597346238a0240049c3_15.t: Amsel's voice comes out in a roar, and his fist smashes down on the desk behind him.
Eiger, the two of you can have it out later. But I’ve had enough.

52449597346238a0240049c3_19.t: Agreed. And to neutralize that danger, we need to know who we are dealing with.

52449597346238a0240049c3_31.t: Amsel seems taken aback. He pauses for a moment before responding.
The Firewing. This is... unexpected. You'll have to forgive me... this brings back many unpleasant memories.

52449597346238a0240049c3_33.t: The most terrible of the Great Dragons. There are those who would disagree, but they never experienced the terror of living in her shadow.
You are far too young to remember her, of course. But for Germans of my generation, the name "Feuerschwinge" is synonymous with chaos, destruction, and death.

52449597346238a0240049c3_35.t: Amsel takes a deep breath. Slowly releases it. There is a haunted look in his eyes.

Those were dark days. Countless men, women, and children were slaughtered, roasted alive in their homes by a creature of legend. No hope for salvation, and no end in sight. It was a horror that you cannot begin to understand.

52449597346238a0240049c3_43.t: Again, all of this happened decades ago. To the best of my knowledge, the story of Feuerschwinge is a bit of historical trivia, and nothing more.

52449597346238a0240049c3_45.t: No. It doesn't make any more sense to me than it does to you... the Dragonfall is ancient history. Feuerschwinge has been dead and gone for forty two years. 52449597346238a0240049c3_46.t: He visibly struggles to calm himself. Takes a deep breath, then slowly releases it.

52449597346238a0240049c3_48.t: Amsel nods. His face is drawn and haggard.

52449597346238a0240049c3_51.t: No. I am not holding out. I am tired, and I am frustrated, and I already miss Monika.

52449597346238a0240049c3_52.t: I did not think to mention our employer because I did not set up the job. Monika did.

52449597346238a0240049c3_53.t: I… I warned her. I told her not to take this run. But she assured me it would be a cake walk.

52449597346238a0240049c3_55.t: I… I warned her. I told her not to take this run. But she assured me it would be a cake walk.

52449597346238a0240049c3_58.t: But Monika, she would do anything for her Cause. Anything for the Flux State.

52449597346238a0240049c3_64.t: When I discovered Monika's renewed association with Green Winters, I contacted Altuğ. One of his people has been keeping tabs on Winters ever since.

5266eef4663661b825002e69_2.t: It is about as I had expected. Thankfully, we are prepared for this. I have established contact with a number of new clients. You can find information on the jobs logged on your mission computer. One job's file is on there already.

5266eef4663661b825002e69_12.t: Amsel's voice is thick with dread.

5266eef4663661b825002e69_26.t: For this? Rich enough... even if only just.

5266eef4663661b825002e69_28.t: A most prominent figure in Berlin's shadow community. Ex-Schockwellenreiter. She provides information retrieval services for the F-State.
Amsel turns to the computer console behind him and runs his fingers over a virtual keyboard. The machine begins to hum.
If Winters was right... if Vauclair is still alive... she can help us find him.

5266eef4663661b825002e69_29.t: The console spits out a stick of black plastic. Amsel plucks it from the computer and hands it to you.
Here. Your key to speak with Alice.

5266eef4663661b825002e69_30.t: An encoded credstick, yes. 10,000 nuyen. Alice will not show her face for less. This represents the last of my personal savings. Make this meeting count.

5266eef4663661b825002e69_35.t: Her standard fee. Alice is in a position to ask it, and we're in no position to argue.

5266eef4663661b825002e69_42.t: Dragon or no, heading back to Harfeld would be a bad idea. You barely made it out alive the last time you were there.

5266eef4663661b825002e69_49.t: Good. This should only take me a moment.

52b20f7b626164081c0068f6_118.t: The APEX-voice continues to throb. The sound slides down your spine like a razor.
SO MANY YEARS WASTED, CHAINED IN IGNORANCE. SO MUCH LOST TIME AND SQUANDERED POTENTIAL! YOU HAVE RELEASED ME FROM A LIVING HELL, $++(L.NAME).

52c5f14e663661d81d007fc6_5.t: APEX's voice throbs through the terminal's speakers.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_146.t: She looks up from her comm, idly blowing a gum bubble and quickly popping it.
Hello again. I hear you've been busy.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_148.t: Well, you know. Altuğ always says, "If you do not spy, how will you know who's boss?"
She waves her hand as though to excuse her poor memory.
Or something like that. I'm just a kid, what do I know?

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_149.t: Kami puffs up indignantly, and you realize how young she must really be. She couldn't be out of her teens yet.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_151.t: She grows quiet for a moment, briefly looking down at her shoes before looking you straight in the eye.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_153.t: Altug gave me a good job, and I'm super grateful for it. Which reminds me... I should get back to it. See you later?
She lazily tips an invisible hat at you before returning to the comm at her wrist.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_154.t: You'd be surprised what I know, but you don't seem like the perceptive type, so that's to be expected.
She practically hisses her response, her eyes suddenly narrowed and venomous.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_155.t: She looks down and exhales, then the clouds leave her bright face.
Listen, I have better things to do right now than get ticked. We'll chat later, okay?

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_156.t: She glances away furtively, apparently remembering something, before beaming back at you.
You'd be surprised what I've seen, even before I started being an intel hound.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_158.t: Speaking of which, I should probably get back to work. Let me know if there's something fun going on, ja?
She lazily tips her invisible hat at you before returning to the comm at her wrist.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_160.t: Well, you know. Altuğ always says, "if you do not spy, how will you know who's boss?"
She waves her hand as though to excuse her poor memory.
Or something like that. I'm just a kid, what do I know?

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_161.t: Her brow suddenly tightens with concern.
Oh. What was going on there?

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_163.t: She puts a hand up to her mouth, stifling a small cry, then shakes her head and pulls herself back together.
I suppose that's to be expected.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_164.t: She exhales like the wind just got knocked out of her.
I'm glad that worked out okay. That's my home kiez, you know.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_167.t: There's that. I mostly gather intel, I'm not much of a hub. But it's also my home kiez.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_175.t: But there are also more opportunities. Travel is easier.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_177.t: She composes herself once more and flashes her big grin at you.
Thanks for telling me about that. I really appreciate that.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_179.t: Kami brightens up as you approach her, grinning widely.
Hey there! Altuğ was just telling me you were around. I was wondering if you might be willing to trade a story or two.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_181.t: The girl taps a finger thoughtfully against her chin.
Well, you’re new here. I know that you knew Monika, and I've got a vague idea about went down the night she died. But I wanna know what you think your most exciting adventure has ever been.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_182.t: Remember, I'm Altuğ's ears and hands outside of the cafe. We share a lot of intel, he and I.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_183.t: She glances away briefly before returning her gaze to you.
I suppose I can tell you. If the Monika thing was a secret of yours, it's only fair I share one of my own.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_186.t: I felt more comfortable with him than I ever did mein Vater -
Her eyes narrow only for the briefest moment at the word.
- and we talked a lot when I was waiting around for my friends.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_190.t: Sure was, but speaking of Altuğ, I ought to be going. You know where I am if you've got anything for me, ja?
She grins and waves a little before bouncing off.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_191.t: Coffee sometimes. Hookah others. It's really not as bad as you think, I promise.

5282c72d643431973972f5f7_197.t: I felt more comfortable with him than I ever did mein Vater -
her eyes narrow only for the briefest moment at the word
- and we talked a lot when I was waiting around for my friends.

5356e43c30303124170043af_48.t: Her lip curls into a sneer.

523f2b40303031540c0039f2_26.t: Well, yeah... I can see that. But this is a rest--

523f2b40303031540c0039f2_28.t: That isn't protocol. This entire story is suspe--

523f2b40303031540c0039f2_30.t: You're not Knight Errant. I don't know who you are, but you've just made a huge mistake.

5241c1dc3030314c16003ee6_1.t: Crazy night, huh? I still can't believe how badly that power overload messed up the system... someone in Maintenance is getting reamed over this one, I'm tellin' you.
I've never seen Central in such a panic.

5241c1dc3030314c16003ee6_5.t: The guard closest to you nods.
Thanks for the reminder. I can't afford another "administrative penalty." C'mon, guys. Let's get the hell out of here.

52421766303031041400128a_1.t: You're wearing the uniform, but you don't sound like Knight Errant. Identify yourself!

52421766303031041400128a_2.t: You're wearing the uniform, but *I've* never seen you before. Identify yourself!

52421766303031041400128a_4.t: An Aztechnology consultant? Dressed in a Knight Errant uniform?

52421766303031041400128a_5.t: Whoa, hold up. Inspection? What are you talking about?

52421766303031041400128a_8.t: The guard straightens. His voice carries an edge of nervous tension.
Well... um... very well! Sir! We will continue to man our posts with diligence, sir! As you'll see!

52449d8d30303170160026a4_1.t: Without a word, he raises his weapon.

52449d8d30303170160026a4_2.t: I need to see your ID, then. You know how Omega-level clearance works. So hand it over. NOW.

52449d8d30303170160026a4_3.t: His eyes widen, then narrow to slits.
You think that this is some sort of *game?* Hand me your ID, or I *will* shoot you.

52449d8d30303170160026a4_4.t: Doktor Huitzilin is your uncle? I... I had no idea. Please, carry on.

525733c73030318804002cf6_0.t: You again? Well, you ignored my warning last time, and you ain't dead, so I guess you can handle yourself.

525733c73030318804002cf6_1.t: A rough-looking dwarf with a fireplug build holds out his hand at your approach.
Whoa there, beanpole. I don't know where you think you're headed, but you should turn around and go back the way you came.

525733c73030318804002cf6_2.t: He stares at you, an appraising look in his eye.

Well, you look like you can handle yourself... but still, I'm tellin' you, the people out there are bad news. There are gangs on the other side of this barricade who'd skin you alive as soon as look at you.

525733c73030318804002cf6_4.t: All right. Fine. If that's really what you want, we won't try to stop you.
He gestures to the troll standing behind him.

525733c73030318804002cf6_5.t: All right. Be my guest. Go play with the torture-gangs. Something tells me you'd be a better fit out there anyway.

525733c73030318804002cf6_6.t: He gestures to the troll standing behind him.

525733c73030318804002cf6_7.t: If that's the way you want it, fine. Be my guest. But when you catch a bullet out there, don't come cryin' back to me.

525733c73030318804002cf6_8.t: Didn't mean no offense. But look at me.
Compared to me, every one of your kind is a beanpole. Ain't a bad thing. It's just the way it is.

525733c73030318804002cf6_9.t: He crosses his arms over his barrel chest and cranes his neck to look you in the eye.
Look, we're not trying to be hardasses here. Our job is to keep the folks on *that* side of the wall *out.* If you really want to head out there, we're not gonna stop you.

525733c73030318804002cf6_11.t: A rough-looking dwarf with a fireplug build holds out his hand at your approach.
Whoa there, pal. I don't know where you think you're headed, but you should turn around and go back the way you came.

525733c73030318804002cf6_12.t: I'm tellin' you, one dwarf to another, the people out there are bad news. That's why we're stationed here... to keep *those* people *out.* There are gangs on the other side of this barricade who'd skin you alive as soon as look at you.

525733c73030318804002cf6_13.t: A rough-looking dwarf with a fireplug build holds out his hand at your approach.
Hold up, trog. I don't know where you think you're headed, but you should turn around and go back the way you came.

525733c73030318804002cf6_15.t: The dwarf looks up at you apologetically.
Yeah, like he said... no offense. 'Round these parts, "trog" is... well, I guess you'd call it a "term of endearment." Sort of an ironic thing.

525733c73030318804002cf6_16.t: A momentary look of confusion crosses the dwarf's face.
Oh, hell, I'm sorry. I didn't mean nothin' by it. 'Round these parts, "trog" is... well, I guess you'd call it a "term of endearment." Sort of an ironic thing.

52abaca1663661942900532e_0.t: An older man raises his hand, squinting as you approach. To his left, a young ork shifts his weight, resting one hand casually on a pistol at his hip. Both are dressed in what might be considered uniforms, but it is hard to tell for sure.

52abaca1663661942900532e_3.t: Indeed. So what brings you to Gesundbrunnen, stranger?

52abaca1663661942900532e_4.t: Well, maybe a few years ago that was true. These days... well, it's hard times all around, I suppose. But you're welcome to go on in, of course! Customers are always welcome.

52abaca1663661942900532e_5.t: Oh, and talk to Quorin if you want to hear some good stories about the market, back in its prime. He's always happy to share a tale or two.

52abaca1663661942900532e_6.t: Of course, of course. Customers are always welcome. Head on in.

52abaca1663661942900532e_8.t: The man looks over at his partner, then back at you warily. After a moment, he steps slowly back in front of the entrance.

52abaca1663661942900532e_11.t: The man looks over at his partner, then back at you warily.

52abaca1663661942900532e_14.t: The older man fixes you with a hard stare.
Not going to happen, stranger. Now I think it's time you let us be.

52abaca1663661942900532e_15.t: The older man fixes you with a hard stare.
I don't think that's a good idea, stranger. Now, I think it's time you let us be.

52abaca1663661942900532e_17.t: The older man hesitates, sighs, and steps aside.

52abaca1663661942900532e_19.t: The older man tries to keep his face neutral, but you can tell you've gotten his attention.
Oh, so you think you can just buy us off? Is that it?

52abaca1663661942900532e_20.t: The guards exchange glances again. Then, the man lowers his eyes, muttering something under his breath.

52abaca1663661942900532e_22.t: The younger ork eagerly snatches the credstick from your hand. The older man sighs, then steps aside.

52abaca1663661942900532e_23.t: 200 will do. Sad to say, we really need the money around here.
The man reaches out reluctantly to take the credstick from your hand, then steps aside.

52abaca1663661942900532e_24.t: The man narrows his eyes at you for a long moment. Finally, he reaches out wordlessly to take the credstick from your hand, then steps aside.

52abaca1663661942900532e_25.t: The guards exchange glances again. After a moment, the old man steps aside.
Well... all right. I suppose there’s no harm in that.

52e2bf13373937707f00224d_0.t: The guard wheels to face you.
Hey, what do you think you're doing? I told you that those doors were off limits!

52e2bf13373937707f00224d_1.t: I can't help you. You're cleared to be in this room, but only under direct supervision, and we can't leave our posts to escort you into the other room.
You just stay in this room, all right? Check your software from where you're at and leave, or I'll have to call this in to Central.

52e2bf13373937707f00224d_2.t: Hold there, I'm calling this in to clear it with Central. If they give the go-ahead, I'll take you wherever you need to be.
He reaches for the commlink stud at his collar.

53b34f8c3030311c17003d89_1.t: It isn't a question. He stands impassively, waiting for a response.

53b34f8c3030311c17003d89_8.t: This your first day on the job, or somethin'? They're a bunch of people dressed like you. A human, an elf, and a dwarf.
He gestures lazily at a small figure standing in the distance.
One of 'em's over there, waiting for you.

53b34f8c3030311c17003d89_11.t: A low growl, amplified by the speakers in the guard's helmet, reverberates in your ears.
Watch it. They might think you're hilarious back at the office, but there's nothing I hate more than a wiseass.

53b34f8c3030311c17003d89_12.t: Better. But keep the jokes to yourself from now on. Otherwise, I might just decide to treat you like the criminal that you claim to be.
He drops his hand to the handle of his sidearm.
Here's a hint: you don't want that to happen.

53b34f8c3030311c17003d89_14.t: Not from you, I can't. Keep it to yourself unless you *want* me to treat you like a criminal.
He drops his hand to the grip of his sidearm.

53b34f8c3030311c17003d89_16.t: His fingers hover over the grip of his sidearm, but only for a moment.
If I hurt you, I'll have to fill out a lot of paperwork. I hate paperwork.

53b34f8c3030311c17003d89_17.t: He shakes his head in disgust.

You're not worth the effort. So I'll tell you what's gonna happen now: I'm gonna point you to one of your little mop-jockey friends, and you're gonna go running off to meet her. And you're gonna do it before I do something that we both wind up regretting. Got it?

53b34f8c3030311c17003d89_18.t: He waves his hand in the direction of a small figure in the distance.
There. Dwarf girl. She's waiting for you. Now go away.

52376ac7303031a81700022b_0.t: Your commlink crackles with static. A moment later, the sonorous voice of Luca Duerr begins speaking into your ear.
Hello again. I have an opportunity for you.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_0.t: So you're the one, eh? The late Monika Schäfer's replacement.
He looks you up and down, then nods.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_15.t: I like your initiative, but no. Nothing so crude as that. You will meet with a team that I've arranged for you, and with their assistance, you will plant surveillance devices in his apartment.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_29.t: Yes? Have you reconsidered working for us?

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_33.t: Good to know. But the purpose of this test is to gauge your adaptability. We need to know that you'll be willing to do what we tell you to do, regardless of your personal feelings on the matter. And we need to know that you'll roll with the punches if circumstances change mid-run.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_40.t: He stares you in the eye.
What do you think of all of that?

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_42.t: After a brief pause, he nods, then reaches forward and places the credstick into your hand.
Congratulations. You have passed your audition. Welcome to the payroll.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_43.t: Now, we part ways. Don't worry - I'll call you when your services are needed.
He taps a neatly-trimmed fingernail against the earpiece of his commlink.
When I contact you with a new task, the choice of whether to take it or leave it will be yours.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_46.t: Duerr glances at his PDA, a high-end model etched with platinum.
...And with that, I'm afraid that I must bid you good day. Go now. I'll be in touch.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_48.t: He frowns, and slides the credstick back into his breast pocket.
A pity. I honestly believed that you had potential. You have failed your audition. There is nothing more for us to discuss. Good day.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_49.t: Very good. I like a man who knows what they are, and what they wants. So. What is your takeaway from this discussion? I would like to hear your thoughts.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_51.t: Plans changed. They sometimes do. Nature of the business.
He reaches into his pocket, and produces a slim plastic rod. A credstick.
Is that going to be a problem for you?

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_54.t: He reaches into his pocket, and produces a slim plastic rod. A credstick.
...And the change of plans on the run didn't bother you? Please, be honest.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_62.t: It isn't my problem. The people who care aren't here... I am a mouthpiece, nothing more. Now, do we have any other business to discuss?

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_71.t: Excellent work. Perhaps you do have a future in this town. Here is your pay.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_72.t: He slips a credstick into your hands.

523c8edc346238e41d008a48_73.t: Leave him to us. And regarding the MKVI, don't worry... the prototype is in good hands now.
He slips a credstick into your hands.

52675434626164703000513a_48.t: That's the call that I would make.

52675434626164703000513a_51.t: What you're really saying is, you're going to trash our reputation for a few extra nuyen. Bad call, fearless leader.

53e2c232303031081d007ffa_46.t: James' continued survival, on the other hand, was not expected. You are a very patient man.

53e2c232303031081d007ffa_58.t: He pauses for a moment, then continues.
I trust that Jana suffered her "accident" on the way out of the building. I am surprised, truth be told. I had anticipated that you would place a premium on protecting the dwarf.

53e2c232303031081d007ffa_62.t: He pauses for a moment, then continues.
I trust that James suffered his "accident" on the way out of the building. Very convenient, and very much as we expected. You are performing exactly as we'd hoped that you would.

53e3e5fb303031bc1d007062_1.t: What did it feel like? An explosion, of course.

53e3e5fb303031bc1d007062_10.t: There is a long pause, punctuated only by the muffled sound of Jana weeping into her hands. Then Duerr speaks.
Your audition is only halfway finished. I'll expect to see you directly, unless you object...?

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_0.t: You... you have given me *everything.* My whole heart's desire. The hidden knowledge of Titonius Rex's victory is mine, and I will be a better ruler for it! And also, I have sugary snacks!
Philip stares into your eyes. His entire body is trembling.
I have it all. And all thanks to you, noble adventurer. All thanks to you.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_1.t: He throws back his head and barks out a laugh.
Spoken like a true hero! Farewell, my friend. Perhaps one day I will be able to come to your aid in return.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_6.t: My only remaining wish is to know the ending to the tale of the noble Titonius Rex and his beautiful Brenna-thay.
He lets loose a wistful sigh.
Who knows? Perhaps one day my prayers will be answered.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_8.t: Philip grins at you, tears spilling from the corners of his eyes.
I owe you *so* much, noble stranger. Thanks to you, I know how the story ends. It was more beautiful than I could possibly have hoped. I, Philip Rex, proclaim it to be so.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_14.t: No matter! I will suffer through it, for the good of my realm!
His hands clench into fists, and his face goes beet red.
As for you, foul vagrant... how *dare* you enter my domain?!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_23.t: I am Philip Rex, the Knight-King of Sutterlin BioScience! I will have you banished for your transgressions, adventurer - banished to the underworld, and left to the ravages of the all-powerful Billy.
Hold still while I fetch the robots that will banish you.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_24.t: I am the sovereign ruler of this building, now and forever!
He puffs his chest out even further.
By right of succession, the former laboratories of Sutterlin BioScience belong to *me!*

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_30.t: For I am a proud and benevolent ruler! This is *my* sovereign territory, and I will *never* abandon it!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_31.t: The grief on his face instantly vanishes. What replaces it looks like curiosity.
Oh? Well, that changes everything. Perilous quests and civilian casualties go together like chocolate and peanut butter. I can't stay mad at you for that.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_32.t: Tell me about this quest of yours, good adventurer. Leave nothing to the imagination!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_34.t: The Executive Wing?! Madness! Take this as a warning, noble stranger: if you intend to forge your way into that land of milk and honey, you do so at your own peril! The underworld stands between you and your goal, and the underworld is home to the murderous Billy.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_36.t: A great beast! A creature of legend!
He pauses for a moment, thinking.
Well, technically, he's a basilisk that one of the executives was performing experiments on. Sort of a passion project, you might say.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_40.t: A fool's errand. Heiner tried to kill Billy once... he thought that by eating the beast, we could steal its power. Hubris. Billy devoured my friend for his insolence, and the last remaining copy of Keycard E was lost.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_43.t: Very well, stranger. If you cannot be dissuaded, I will help you.
The ragged lab tech fishes in his coat pocket with a bony hand, and produces a thin slip of plastic. He presents it to you with a flourish.
This is my most prized possession: the incomparable Keycard B.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_45.t: Ezkibel sent you? The treacherous Spaniard?
You must be looking for the samples in cold storage. That means that you're heading for the Executive Wing.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_47.t: I *wasn't* alone. Not as long as I had my peasantry. But you've changed all of that.
What are you even doing here? Why have you disturbed the peace of my realm?

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_53.t: You killed them, you monster. The people I've devoted my life to protecting.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_61.t: We've got a live one here, chief.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_63.t: He lurches toward you with a start.
You're back. Is Billy dead? Have you retrieved the long-lost Keycard E?

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_64.t: You have it! The keycard! My ticket back into the hallowed halls of the Executive Wing, with its expansive supply of nonperishable treats! Hurry, noble stranger! Use it to unlock the door, and make all of our dreams come true!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_65.t: Very well. Ask your questions. But hurry!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_66.t: The hope drains from his eyes.
Ah. Then what do you want?

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_69.t: Staring at you, he slowly nods.
It is my life. My *entire* life. My reason for being.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_71.t: He reels as though he's been struck.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_73.t: I had always thought that they had left the ending to the imagination, like a beautiful dream.
But now the dream is over! I will never know peace until I have seen this conclusion for myself.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_74.t: He lurches forward, his hands clasped in supplication.
You must help me! You must find a way to show me the final episode! I *need* it!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_77.t: The lab tech hangs his head.
In that case, there is nothing left for me but suffering. The same suffering that Titonius Rex faced when he sent Brenna-thay back to Trala-sheen. It hurts.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_78.t: He nods vigorously, patches of beard wobbling with the motion.
Yes! Please! Do it... do it for me!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_79.t: This is the happiest day of my life!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_81.t: Mine? But I... I cannot leave... I can't abandon my kingdom! Not even for my heart's greatest desire! Responsibility is what being a Knight-King is all about!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_82.t: His brow furrows in concentration. Finally, he gives you a slow nod.
I do, my friend. I do.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_84.t: They will find the Lost Episode - THIS I SWEAR!
He pulls himself up to his full height, puffing his chest outward. Somehow, this makes him look even less impressive.
They will RETRIEVE the glorious Episode Seven and RETURN IT to its RIGHTFUL PLACE! And they will HEW IN TWAIN the HEATHEN DOGS who seek to hoard its magnificence for THEMSELVES!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_85.t: I will, my friend! I will! But first, I will indulge in sugary snacks!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_86.t: All at once, he deflates. Solemnly, he shuffles his feet.
You're right. I need them here, with me. It was a stupid idea anyway.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_87.t: And so I shall! Let us speak of this no longer. I will banish the final episode from my mind, and in so doing, heal my tortured soul.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_89.t: The... yes! Peace will be known, and brought to the land!
Phil takes off running in the direction of the records room.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_90.t: It's bioware. Pretty standard stuff. Pain editors, trauma dampers, orthoskin. Things like that.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_91.t: Sutterlin BioScience wasn't "developing" anything. Well, nothing other than Billy, I guess. We "developed" him all too well.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_93.t: We were... copying? Making copies. No, wait... just a second, it will come to me.
He screws up his face, struggling to dredge up the memories.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_94.t: We were... ah... um. Rever-- Yes. We were reverse-engineering things. Making knockoffs.
Dangerous work. Illegal most places, but not here in Berlin. Our investor needed it that way.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_97.t: The stuff in this lab... the organs we worked so hard to copy... it's no different to me than anything else you've seen in this lab. It's all meat.
Potentially edible, sure. But otherwise useless.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_101.t: Yes! Too risky. Here in Berlin, no problems with the law, because there is no law. Just have to be careful. All for naught now, though. Our copies were good - just like the real thing - but who cares? It's all just meat in the end.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_105.t: Dangerous work. Illegal most places, but not here in Berlin. Our investor needed it that way.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_107.t: He crosses his arms over his chest.
Just because you can't understand our bond, that doesn't mean it isn't real!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_109.t: You will see. You may have killed three of my peasants, but I can activate more!
They will return to me, and I will show them the love that only a true ruler can. I will *bathe* them in my affection, and they--

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_113.t: It is, it is! You killed three of them, but I can activate more!
They will return to me, and I will show them the love that only a true ruler can. I will *bathe* them in my affection, and they--

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_115.t: The security system here is quite state of the art. User-friendly, too... it didn't take long to figure out how to make some peasants wake up, and others go to sleep.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_117.t: Of course. What do you need?

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_118.t: No, of course not. They're private.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_119.t: My peasants?! Sent against that *monster?* Never!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_121.t: He shakes his head decisively, his arms crossed over his chest.
No, I say. I will not do it, not even for you.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_123.t: The nether realm that lies beneath the hallowed halls of Sutterlin BioScience. A nightmarish labyrinth of dripping pipes and filthy brickwork. Billy rules supreme down there now. To set foot into the underworld means certain death.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_127.t: I suppose that you have that right.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_129.t: His voice lowers to an ominous whisper.
But it is *also* a nightmarish labyrinth of--

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_135.t: Then you shall have one. Where to begin...?

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_138.t: He raises his voice and gestures theatrically.

Doors locked and shutters slammed closed! We were all trapped like rats inside, but we were not afraid. We were sure that the malfunction would be fixed within the hour. We did not know what was coming for us!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_140.t: His voice climbs to a roar.

Within a half hour of the spill, Billy had slaughtered everyone who could have disengaged the lockdown! Executives were torn limb from limb; their business attire was shredded, their expenditure reports bathed in blood! And the rest of us were trapped inside with the savage beast!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_144.t: The manic cheer that you've come to expect floods back into the lab tech. He spreads his arms wide, a knowing smile on his face.

And as you can plainly see, we chose the latter! We persevered, and eventually transformed this husk of a building into the glorious kingdom that you find yourself standing in today. It was all for the best, in the end! I tamed this land, and I have grown to love it. I will never live anywhere else!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_147.t: He spreads his arms wide, a knowing smile on his face.

In the time that Heiner and I were trapped here, we vowed to make the best of our situation. We persevered, and eventually transformed this husk of a building into the glorious kingdom that you find yourself standing in today. It was all for the best, in the end! I tamed this land, and I have grown to love it. I will never live anywhere else!

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_148.t: He rocks back on his heels, apparently satisfied.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_149.t: Well, no... naturally, the inevitable results of such an incident followed the spill.
Do you want me to spell it out for you?

52604be06361341023004911_3.t: Okay, okay, yeah... yeah, I can get behind that.

She presses her thumb to the encoded credstick you retrieved for her, and its display comes to life. She presses it to your credstick, and 100 nuyen is instantly transferred from her account to yours.

52604be06361341023004911_4.t: She breathes a sigh of relief, then surveys the items that you retrieved for her.
Thank you again, so much. I don't think I'd ever have seen this stuff again if it weren't for you.

52604be06361341023004911_6.t: She glances at the encoded credstick in her hand again, checking the balance. Her smile broadens.
You helped me when no one else would. That means a lot. Enjoy the money... you earned it.

52604be06361341023004911_7.t: Really? You actually went up there and got my things?
She stares at the items in her hands incredulously.
And you didn't just walk off with them?

52604be06361341023004911_8.t: Wow. Um, thank you. When you said that you were going up there, I didn't think... I just...

52604be06361341023004911_9.t: Okay. Yeah, sounds good. But, um...
She bites her lip and swallows hard.
...Hurry back, okay? Room 304, don't forget. I could really, REALLY use that money.

52604be06361341023004911_10.t: Silke looks up at you. Where you used to see only pain, now there's something else visible in her face. Determination.
I'm really going to do it. I'm taking the next train to the Kreuzbasar. Wish I could get my stuff back from my room first, though. Stupid gang, closing the whole hotel.

52604be06361341023004911_11.t: Silke looks up at you, a look of abject misery plastered across her face.
Leave me alone, okay? I've got problems to deal with.

52604be06361341023004911_13.t: Really? That'd be... well, that'd be incredible.
Her lips struggle upward into a facsimile of a smile.
I really need that money, and I left some things in there, too. Y'know, personal stuff.

52604be06361341023004911_17.t: You can talk to Frank. He's the floor manager. He'd have an elevator key.
She points to the north end of the club.
He's over there, on the VIP balcony. See the greasy jackass in the oversized suit? The one drooling all over that table dancer? That's Frank.

52604be06361341023004911_20.t: That's about all I can think of. I steer clear of the new guys when I can.
She steals a glance over her shoulder at Kroner, a scowl on her face.
I shouldn't have to tell you why.

52604be06361341023004911_27.t: Well, then, why the hell did you offer?
From her body language, you can tell that Silke is agitated - she looks like she can't decide whether to slap you or break down crying.
If this is how it's gonna be, you can keep your favors to yourself.

52604be06361341023004911_29.t: Seriously? You just, like, talked me into getting clean, and now you're offering to buy me a fix?

52604be06361341023004911_30.t: Kind of a half-jacked move, don'tcha think? But yeah, okay.
She twirls a finger above her head.

52604be06361341023004911_31.t: The need in Silke's eyes is palpable. As you watch, her newly reclaimed willpower crumbles.
Yeah, I can take one more hit. One more won't hurt.

52604be06361341023004911_33.t: The young woman gives you a wary look.
What's the catch?...'Cause if you're tryin' to make the same offer that HE just did...

52604be06361341023004911_35.t: That's all you want? For me to tell you about the hotel?
The girl's mood visibly brightens, and the tremor in her lower lip disappears.
Well, sure. Yeah, I'll tell you whatever you need!

52604be06361341023004911_39.t: What, did you think it was gonna be free?
Silke's hands clench and unclench. She looks agitated.
If cram were *cheap,* I wouldn't BE in this mess.

52604be06361341023004911_40.t: No! Wait! I still really need your help!
As you turn to leave, she slumps back against the bar. Her voice is pathetic and small.

52604be06361341023004911_41.t: The junkie's face floods with a mixture of surprise and relief.
...So then, like, what DO you want?

52604be06361341023004911_43.t: Who, like, said that I wanted to "shake" anything?
She gives you a sour look.
Look around you. Like what you see? Of course you don't. My life is better on cram. Why would I ever want to stop?

52604be06361341023004911_46.t: Stop it. Please, just stop, okay? I don't need your help, and I don't want it.
As strong as Silke's words are, her voice sounds unsure. From the way that she's fidgeting, you can tell that you've struck a nerve.

52604be06361341023004911_49.t: She runs her tongue over her teeth, a worried look in her eyes.
I, uh... I hadn't heard that before.

52604be06361341023004911_51.t: What you've said, it... it makes a lot of sense.
She blinks, steals a glance back at Kroner, and shudders.

52604be06361341023004911_54.t: She nods eagerly, her eyes full of gratitude.
I'll do that. I will. Thank you.

52604be06361341023004911_58.t: Yeah... yeah, I'll think about it.

52604be06361341023004911_61.t: It's not like they own me! I'd... I'd just leave if I wanted to.

52604be06361341023004911_62.t: She rolls her eyes at you.
Please. Look, I'm sure you're just trying to help, but you're gettin' on my nerves here. It's not your business anyway. I don't even KNOW you! So just leave me alone, okay?

52604be06361341023004911_65.t: She runs her tongue over her teeth, a worried look in her eyes.
I, uh... I hadn't heard that before.

52604be06361341023004911_66.t: Stop it. Please, just stop, okay? I don't need your help, and I don't want it.

Please. Look, I'm sure you're just trying to help, but you're gettin' on my nerves here. It's not your business anyway. I don't even KNOW you! So just leave me alone, okay?

528adc37626164e029006ae3_0.t: A high-pitched, creaky voice shrieks out from the intercom.
GO AWAY! I won't let you let any spirits in here! You can't hurt me behind my door! No, you can't!

528adc37626164e029006ae3_2.t: That's EXACTLY what a spirit would say! An' then it'd get in here, and it'd... it'd... it'd eat my eyes! Suck 'em right out of my skull! I've SEEN IT HAPPEN!
You hear a ragged intake of breath, and then the shrill voice raises into a scream.

528adc37626164e029006ae3_3.t: The voice becomes calmer, and lowers in pitch.
Huh. I guess I've never heard of a spirit using an intercom before. That's a really good point. Are... are you here to save me?

528adc37626164e029006ae3_4.t: All right, I'm going to let you in. But be cool, okay? I mean that! No funny stuff!

528adc37626164e029006ae3_5.t: A high-pitched, wailing shriek explodes out of the intercom. There is a burst of static, and the machine goes silent.

528adc37626164e029006ae3_17.t: The high-pitched voice warbles out over the intercom.
Hello? Spirit? Are you back? I thought I told you to *leave me alone!*

528adc37626164e029006ae3_18.t: *NO!* I won't let you outsmart me!
Cackling laughter bursts out from the speaker.
Go away, spirit! I'm keeping my eyes for *myself!*

528ae06f626164e029006b72_1.t: Who are you? Are you part of the Communion? Have you come to take me back? Please, no, I don't wanna go back!
He spits and claws at the air in front of him, as if fighting off an invisible attacker. All the while, his eyes are locked on you.
No! It isn't safe! The spirits! IT'S NOT SAFE!

528ae06f626164e029006b72_2.t: Empty? Yes, empty. All is empty without Communion. But... safe, you say? It's safe?

528ae06f626164e029006b72_3.t: I am nothing! Only the Communion remains! Only the Communion matters!
His eyes roll in his skull, and a thin stream of drool runs down from the corner of his skull.
The Hub! Yes, it is the source, the font, the home! It calls! It calls, and I must answer!

528ae06f626164e029006b72_4.t: He clasps his hands in front of him, pleading.
I must return, I must rejoin! But it's not *safe!*

528ae06f626164e029006b72_6.t: The Communion is all of us! It is togetherness, the Joining! Something is taken... it must be taken! And in return, soooo much is given.
His voice rises in orgiastic glee.
It's all yours for the receiving, for soooo little taken!

528ae06f626164e029006b72_7.t: We *join* together in the Communion! It makes us strong! It feeds us, nourishes us! We *belong* to it, and it belongs to us.
The cultist shakes his head violently from side to side.

528ae06f626164e029006b72_10.t: You... you don't look like any spirit *I've* ever seen!
But... you said that you were a spirit? You must be a spirit... they're everywhere now. All of the places, yes... all spirits.

528ae06f626164e029006b72_11.t: The madman's voice becomes shrill with panic.
It's not safe. Can't reach Communion. Can't hear Communion! *It's not safe!*

528ae06f626164e029006b72_14.t: He pulls something long and slender out of his jacket pocket. A knitting needle.
You will not listen, so I will put out your ears! Now *hold still!*

528ae06f626164e029006b72_15.t: He breaks into a ridiculous grin.
Yes, be rid of them! Go, GO! Rid us of them now!

528ae06f626164e029006b72_17.t: He reaches into his grubby pocket and pulls out an object. Shoves it into your hands. A jazz inhaler.
Take it, take it! For clearing the spirits. To help return us to Communion! We must again listen, we must!

528ae06f626164e029006b72_20.t: He breaks into a ridiculous grin.
I must go now! Yes! Communion awaits!

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_3.t: My friend! What a relief it is to see you alive!
Unfortunately, many others were less fortunate.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_5.t: He clasps your shoulder, his eyes welling with tears.
She took five rounds. Doctor Ezkibel tells me that she felt no pain.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_7.t: What do you suppose? Our people spend much of their time on the street, trying to coax the indigent into making use of our services.
The attackers were indescriminate with their fire. It was a bloodbath.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_8.t: My friend! What a relief it is to see you alive!
Samuel wraps you in an enormous hug.
You should know that your generosity has saved dozens of lives.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_11.t: Not now. I have... unpleasant business to attend to.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_12.t: At the sound of your approach, the ork turns to face you. He wears a severe expression, but there is kindness in his eyes.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_15.t: Beckenbauer eyes the plans on the datapad, then nods grimly.
This fits with Humanis' established pattern of behavior. Horrific and vile.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_16.t: According to this datapad, Humanis compounds all over Berlin have received similar shipments. They are planning to deploy the gas tomorrow morning.
He exhales sharply, then nods again.
I have a feeling that the Flux-State will have a thing or two to say about that.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_22.t: Yes... yes, I do. Thank you for coming so promptly.
He takes a deep breath, slowly releases it, and looks you in the eye.
Tell me. What do you know about the Humanis Policlub?

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_28.t: Yesterday, I overheard one of my assistants talking on his comm. He was yelling, clearly agitated. I questioned him, and he confided in me. What he told me made my blood run cold.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_35.t: No. Stopping the shipment would not stop Humanis. They would merely acquire another. For a pack of snarling racists, they are extremely well-funded.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_37.t: Yes. Good. From your reputation around the Kreuzbasar, I knew that you were the right man for the job.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_41.t: He lets out a deep sigh.
You're wrong about my organization. I keep nothing for myself... nothing. But I will see what I can do. I might be able to call in some favors and raise you another five hundred.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_42.t: Excellent. I am relieved. I will make the arrangements for your meeting with the smugglers. Their leader is a elf who calls himself "Maxim." Take the U-Bahn to Schattennest and you will find him waiting there.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_45.t: Yes. I believe so. The Berlin chapter of Humanis has arranged to acquire a large shipment of an extremely hazardous chemical. Their leader, Volker Stahl, is a vicious ideologue. It goes without saying that whatever he intends to do, it won't be good for us.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_46.t: The largest and most well-funded of its kind. The instigators of the Night of Rage, and the enemy of everything that I stand for.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_47.t: A label that I would not apply to many people... but the leadership of Humanis are deserving of the title.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_50.t: Yes, of course. Please, go on.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_53.t: No. No donations. Not any more. With the losses that we took in the attack, I have decided to close the doors on the charity. Perhaps they will open again some day... but now, it is time to grieve.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_67.t: This is... incredibly generous. Thank you.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_68.t: Samuel accepts your donation with a smile.
A fine donation... this will do a great deal of good. Thank you.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_69.t: Samuel nods his head in appreciation.
Thank you. This will go a long way.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_70.t: I thank you. Every little bit helps.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_79.t: You are a true friend of the Kreuzbasar.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_89.t: He gives you a sad smile.
I appreciate the thought, all the same.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_95.t: Don't make this into something it isn't.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_97.t: No. There are more important things to do than argue about this.
I'm sure that their heart is in the right place.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_99.t: He offers you a slight smile and nods.
It's gratifying to hear you say it. I am glad to have you on our side. Now, is there anything else that I can help you with?

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_100.t: Good of you to understand. Now, is there something else that you'd like to talk about?

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_101.t: Thank you for saying so. Now, is there something else that you'd like to talk about?

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_104.t: Yes. It isn't much, but we do what we can.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_105.t: He clears his throat, then begins to count off on his fingers.

In the past several years I have established a shelter where the dispossessed can sleep, a soup kitchen to feed the hungry, and a library for the people of the Kreuzbasar to better themselves. It isn't much, I admit. But it's a start.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_107.t: He nods to the ork at his side.
Thankfully, some of the residents that I've helped over the years have come back around to help me.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_116.t: He gives you a sad smile.
I appreciate the thought, all the same.

523bfbe4346238e41d007f96_118.t: Your friend Dietrich took a chance in sending his nephew to us. I will do my best to justify his faith in me.

5266f911663661b825003110_10.t: Found the message Adrian left me, all those years ago. Got it cleaned up as best I could. Strange, hearing his voice again.
He pauses to take a long pull on the bottle.
It's good to hear him... even if he did insist on calling me "Hermie."

5266f911663661b825003110_11.t: Dr. Adrian Vauclair. The hero of the people. The Dragonslayer. My brother.
He grimaces and rubs his eyes.
Hard to believe it's been almost twenty years.

5266f911663661b825003110_14.t: Christ. Twenty years. All this time, and still, no leads. Even with all of my contacts, and my resources...
He takes another long swallow of cheap liquor.
...Even with my "legendary bullheadedness," I've made no progress at all. I haven't turned up a single god damned thing.

5266f911663661b825003110_15.t: Wanna hear something funny? The closest I've gotten to a clue was a rumor. Apparently, a team emerged from the SOX a while back. Nobody's clear on the date. But... and get this...
He leans in to the camera and gives a conspriatorial wink.

5266f911663661b825003110_16.t: He upends the bottle. Drains it. Slams it back down to the table. His eyes are rimmed with red.
Not much of a rumor, considering. I already *know* that you're gone, big brother. I live with that every day.

5266f911663661b825003110_41.t: Green Winters' face reappears on the screen.
Okay, it's time for a new approach. Adrian's a complete dead-end... that much is pretty clear by now. So I'm gonna do some digging on Feuerschwinge instead. Let's see where this goes.

5266f911663661b825003110_44.t: Well, that was a bust. Little progress on Feuerschwinge, either.
I dunno. It's weird... the information is there, it's just... *wrong,* somehow. It's too well laid out, too simple. Real life is messy, and this feels just a little too... neat.

5266f911663661b825003110_51.t: Could these rumors be related to what's happening here? Am I being paranoid?
He pauses for a moment, as if seriously considering the question. Then he shakes his head and slams his fist on the table.
No. I don't think so. Something *big* is happening here, and I'm right in the middle of it.

5266f911663661b825003110_57.t: On the screen, Winters pauses. He looks like he's working up to something. Finally, he speaks.
I'm just gonna go ahead and say it. This has gotta be Feuerschwinge... all of it. Adrian was right about her, she's still alive, and she's out of the SOX.

5266f911663661b825003110_60.t: I've backtraced all of the Matrix nodes that Hellebore was looking into, back before she got cooked. Whoever's been purging the Matrix didn't think about that! And I found that she'd turned up...
Winters stabs a few keys on his keyboard, and an image expands to fill the screen of his monitor. A satellite photo of a rural landscape, annotated with GPS data.

5266f911663661b825003110_63.t: As if on cue, a familiar image winks onto the monitor to Winters' left. Monika.

I think I'm gonna tap Schäfer for this job. She's got the skills to bypass whatever security they're running out there, and she's gullible enough to take the job in the first place. I'll feed her some line about Flux State security, and she'll eat it up with a spoon.

5266f911663661b825003110_65.t: On the display, Winters' mouth parts into a broad grin.

Time to put plans into motion. By this time tomorrow night I should have the information that I need. And on the off-chance that Schäfer gets taken out, well... that'll tell me something, too. Can't make an omelet, and all that. Until then.

5266f911663661b825003110_68.t: There is a blur of motion, and the haggard form of Green Winters lumbers into frame.

Winters reaches a shaking hand off-camera. A moment later, it returns clutching a cheap plastic drinking glass. He gulps something down, wipes his mouth with the back of his hand, and takes a deep breath.

5266f911663661b825003110_69.t: Okay. Okay okay okay. Back up, slow down. Start from the beginning.
Winters closes his eyes and makes a visible effort to calm himself. When he speaks again, his voice is steady.
Got the call from my contact in the Kreuzbasar. Schäfer killed on the estate run. Matrix security at the estate cooked her brain.

5266f911663661b825003110_72.t: Winters takes another long pull from his glass, grimaces, and swallows.

Time for me to make my play. Ghost stories or no, missing deckers or no, I've gotta jack into the Matrix and start pulling strings. The countermeasures that I installed earlier should be more than enough to keep me safe for the twenty minutes I'll be in there.

5266f911663661b825003110_73.t: Winters drains the rest of the glass, then tosses it back over his shoulder. A moment later you hear the dull clattering sound of hard plastic on linoleum.
Schäfer's death was tragic. She was a staunch supporter of the F-State. But still, all things considered...

5266f911663661b825003110_74.t: Winters fishes for something off-screen. A moment later, his hand reappears clutching a datajack cable.
He plugs the cable into his head and the screen cuts to static.

5266f911663661b825003110_96.t: Found the message Adrian left me, all those years ago. Got it cleaned up as best I could. Strange, hearing his voice again.
He pauses to take a long pull on the bottle.
It's good to hear him... even if he did insist on calling me "Hermie."
