5266f911663661b825003110_154.t: Winters fishes for something off-screen. A moment later, his hand reappears clutching a datajack cable.

5266f911663661b825003110_160.t: The screen jumps, and Winters reappears in a different location. He is now clutching a mug of soykaf in both hands, and there are bags under his eyes.

5266f911663661b825003110_173.t: Winters grabs a thick, leather-bound tome from a shelf behind him, licks a finger, and begins to leaf through it.
All right, so let's run down the list of major dragons that *could* be helping her.

5266f911663661b825003110_175.t: So he's definitely got the means... but I can't see how he'd have the motive. He actively prevented the Firewing's rescue back in 2012, after all. Same thing is true for Nebelherr, so let's scratch the both of them off the list.

5266f911663661b825003110_177.t: There's Celedyr, out in Wales... he's pretty heavily invested in Transys Neuronet, so he's got the money. But he's too busy dealing with the "BTL Killer" scandal that Transys is going through out in London to get his claws dirty in the SOX.

5266f911663661b825003110_178.t: He pauses. Shakes his head. Then he slams the tome shut.
...No. This is a waste of time. The more I think about it, the more convinced I am: the Firewing is acting alone. Dragons don't cooperate unless they absolutely have to.

5266f911663661b825003110_202.t: Well, I was always more of a kid than he was, of course. He's fifteen years older than me... in a way, he was more like a parent than a brother.
Winters pauses. His gaze drops to an invisible point on the table in front of him.
So I was thinking about this time when we were on holiday. Our parents took us out the island of Poel to enjoy the beach.

5266f911663661b825003110_212.t: Winters' face winks back onto the screen.
So there you have it. That's what they had to say about Adrian a mere two years after his disappearance. My brother, the footnote.

5266f911663661b825003110_245.t: He takes a moment to calm himself.
Okay. So these notes... they pretty much confirm something that I'd always suspected. The Luftwaffe didn't shoot down Feuerschwinge.

5266f911663661b825003110_250.t: Winters pushes his chair back and slaps the table in front of him.

*This* is why Adrian was always so cagey about the subject of Feuerschwinge after the Dragonfall! He knew for a *fact* that she was alive, even if only technically. Her body crashed down into the SOX, sure, but she wasn't dead!

5266f911663661b825003110_263.t: Winters swipes a bottle off the table in front of him, drains it, and drops it to the floor.
Call it whatever you want. But Adrian, you're the only one who's still this obsessed with the damned things! There aren't that many of them, and they're *not* taking over the world!

5266f911663661b825003110_265.t: Now you're just being maudlin. Look, Adrian--

5266f911663661b825003110_268.t: Green Winters chuckles uncomfortably. Raises his hands in the air in a supplicating gesture.

5266f911663661b825003110_270.t: You're hopeless, Adrian. Fine, by all means, keep caring... just keep your conspiracy theories to *yourself.* I'm telling you this for your own sake.

5266f911663661b825003110_272.t: Winters' expression turns serious. His voice lowers.
It's not just your credibility that I'm worried about. Talk is talk, but sometimes...
...Sometimes I worry that you're really gonna go back to the SOX.

5266f911663661b825003110_274.t: Winters cuts his brother off. A new intensity burns in his voice.
Please, big brother. Just drop all of this. Stop hunting the Firewing, and start living your life again! If you won't do it for yourself, then for Christ's sake, do it for me.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_7.t: The Turk is pale. His eyes, sunken into his head, are ringed by dark circles.
We are closed today, efendi. I would have locked the door but… the lock is broken. Now, I must stand guard.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_9.t: No, my friend. I am not. I will not be right for a long time.
He steadies himself on the counter.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_10.t: He rubs his hand across his mouth.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_11.t: During the attack on the Kreuzbasar... they broke through the door, heavily armed. Two of them. They broke through the door and just started shooting. No questions. No robbery. Just… shooting. Kami… my girl… my little street rat… they…
He holds his hands over his face. Collects himself.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_19.t: The Turk leans into you, keeps his voice low.
I have information regarding that matter my people were looking into, efendim. The one involving the surveillance device on my data tap.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_21.t: Can’t you see that I am speaking to an honored guest, you tower of quivering corpulence?

523bfc69346238e41d008008_22.t: Now then, as I was saying, we were fortunate that you happened upon that surveillance tap when you did. The people on the other end were planning a very bad thing, to be sure.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_30.t: The Turk flashes his wry smile and winks at you knowingly.
I am told you have been quite busy of late, O Wanderer of the Shadows! My street rats tell me that business is picking up.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_36.t: As you desire, O Ample One!
The man is a moron but he eats his weight in baklava and pastries, so who am I to care?

523bfc69346238e41d008008_38.t: The man behind the counter looks right past you and at the dog following close behind.
Dante! I will fetch his water dish. And perhaps a coffee for our friend here?

523bfc69346238e41d008008_40.t: The man behind the counter has the broad smile and open demeanor of a classic Turkish street vendor.
Welcome, Honored Efendim! Welcome! And how can Burakgazi serve you, today? You would like a cup of coffee, perhaps?

523bfc69346238e41d008008_44.t: I am delighted, O Excellent One! With that out of the way, let us return to our pressing business.
He barks a stream of rapid-fire Turkish, and the gum-chewing young woman comes hurrying out to the counter.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_46.t: Burakgazi gives Kami a small nod, and she hurries out of the room. When she is gone, he returns his attention to you.
Please express my condolences as well. I only just heard the news. Monika was an important part of this community.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_52.t: He frowns and shakes his head but his eyes are watching you closely.
No, no. Clearly, you are mistaken.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_53.t: Burakgazi's eyes narrow. Without a word, he reaches into his pocket and fishes out a dirty and well-worn bill, then flicks it onto the counter in front of you.
There. For your discretion. As my people say: Don't tell your secret to your friend - he will tell it to his friend.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_55.t: Burakgazi's eyes narrow. Without a word, he reaches into his pocket and fishes out a pair of crumpled and well-worn bills, then flicks them onto the counter in front of you.
There. Now you are sure of it. Don't tell your secret to your friend - he will tell it to his friend.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_56.t: Burakgazi raises an eyebrow and pauses for a moment, considering.
Yes... yes, I think that we can come to some arrangement.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_57.t: A few minutes and a diagram scrawled on a napkin later, Burakgazi nods at you.
Yes... yes. This should be doable. I thank you for your expertise, and for your discretion. Don't tell your secret to your friend - he will tell it to his friend.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_59.t: Of course they were! I would be surprised they weren't! This is Berlin, after all. In the Flux, everyone spies! If you do not spy, how will you know who is in power and who will be in power next? If you are to stay here, efendim, you must get used to it. "Who enters the Turkish bath, will sweat," as my Uncle Taydemer always says.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_61.t: With that out of the way, let us return to our pressing business.
He barks a stream of rapid-fire Turkish, and the gum-chewing young woman comes hurrying out to the counter.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_62.t: Oho! Tell me, O Listener at Keyholes, what did you hear on this surveillance tap you found?

523bfc69346238e41d008008_63.t: News travels fast in Berlin. These two are known to me. Is there more?

523bfc69346238e41d008008_64.t: Most excellent. It is indeed fortuitous that you discovered this information, though it is not unexpected. I will have one of my people “attend” this council meeting and report back.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_65.t: Indeed they did, Honored Efendim! Indeed they did! And it was most fortunate that you discovered it. It seems there was a group that wished to destabilize the Kreuzbasar - to create a vacuum that they could fill with their political ambitions.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_70.t: When he hears Amsel's name, the Turk's voice lowers and his accent becomes less exaggerated. His eyes take on a knowing look.
Ah, very good. Please express to Herr Amsel my appreciation of his patronage. If he needs any more "catering jobs" seen to in the future, I am always happy to provide.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_71.t: Yes. Yes, of course, O Wise One! Kami! Come!
A young woman bustles in from the back room. Her gum chewing is loud enough to hear over the noise of the coffee grinders. Burakgazi spits something out in rapid-fire Turkish.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_75.t: He smiles big. The Turkish vendor again.
You are already being compensated, Most Masterful of Mercenaries! Do you not recall? Kami is fetching your information for you. Returning the favor would be the courteous thing to do.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_76.t: Altuğ's voice lowers to nearly a whisper.

The errand is simple. Hardly worthy of you. I have installed a number of data taps to Berlin's fiber optic network... as part of my civic duty, you understand. These taps provide free Matrix access for all who live in the Kreuzbasar.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_82.t: Burakgazi locks eyes with you, and his voice grows cold.
Please inform Herr Amsel that his information is regrettably unavailable at this time.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_85.t: Oh ho! Yes, of course I can.
Burakgazi rummages through his pockets and fishes out a crumpled 100 nuyen bill. He thrusts the coffee-stained wad of paper into your hands.
There. You have been paid. Now, let us get on with it, eh?

523bfc69346238e41d008008_87.t: Burakgazi offers you a strained smile.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_88.t: A young woman comes bustling in from the back room. Her gum chewing is loud enough to hear over the noise of the coffee grinders. Burakgazi spits something out in rapid-fire Turkish.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_89.t: Burakgazi gives you a slow nod, his brow furrowed.
Yes. I see. Well, perhaps it would be best if you just didn't worry about it. You just stand in the corner, there, and I will take care of the rest.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_90.t: The coffee shop owner offers you a smile.
Of course, of course! Herr Amsel is too kind.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_91.t: Burakgazi turns his head and calls into the back room.
A young woman bustles in from the back room. Her gum chewing is loud enough to hear over the noise of the coffee grinders. Burakgazi spits something out in rapid-fire Turkish.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_93.t: Yes, for individuals of refined taste, I offer genuine bean coffee from my native Turkey.
The cafe owner looks you in the eye. The tone of his voice grows low and serious.
This is a top-shelf item, my friend, and not for the general public. Only for those few discerning connoisseurs who can properly appreciate it.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_95.t: Burakgazi leans in close, lowering his voice to a conspiratorial whisper. Long hours in the shop have perfumed his body with the commingled scents of coffee, incense, and applewood tobacco.
Trust me, if your coffee experience has been limited to soykaf, you must not deny yourself this opportunity. You will see God.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_98.t: Burakgazi hands you a ceramic travel cup, which he then fills to the brim with dark, steaming liquid. The scent is intoxicating.
Is there anything more that I can get you?

523bfc69346238e41d008008_100.t: Of course. My family's hand-picked coffee bean is not for everyone. Forgive me for failing to recognize that it was not for you.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_101.t: You did, did you? A bit presumptious of you, is it not?

523bfc69346238e41d008008_102.t: I see. Well... that sounds like a tidy little arrangement you've made then, efendim. The kind of arrangement I might make myself.
The kind of arrangement Monika might have made.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_108.t: Islay, you say? Might I examine the merchandise?
He takes the bottle and his smile fades for a moment as he become all business examining it with a critical eye. Then, as quickly as it faded, the huge smile is back.
Quite a little treasure you have found, I'm sorely tempted to ask from where, in hopes of procuring more you see. But no, it would not be polite.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_115.t: Altug smiles at you from behind the counter.
Welcome back, Honored Efendim. How may I serve you?

523bfd34346238e41d0080bf_1.t: From the back of the store, the voice of the shopkeep cuts you off.
Don't mind the fool in the chair. He roars like a traumatized walrus, stewing all day in his own sweat.
The man behind the bar glares at Goldschmidt, his upper lip curled in disgust.
I tolerate him only because he takes his soykaf by the bucket.

523bfd34346238e41d0080bf_3.t: Once again, the shopkeep cuts in.
To bow to me, you would first have to vacate your chair.
The shopkeep claps his hands together, clasping them in front of his chest.
I shall summon a team of determined young men and an ox to assist with the task. With luck, you will be on your feet by nightfall.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_4.t: Not this you, and not today.
Within three cycles, what the world has lost, it shall rue. But the Ice Storm will come, the Codex will be broken, and woe to the Dancer. This I prophesy.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_18.t: When a dragon falls, the sound resonates across time and space.
Within three cycles, what the world has lost, it shall rue. But the Ice Storm will come, the Codex will be broken, and woe to the Dancer. This I prophesy.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_26.t: “Good” is a children’s term in a world without connections. Without nuance.
Before the next cycle’s end, the Feuerschwinge Wyrm will speak again with the voice of the Maiden. The Dancer will spin, the Seal will melt, and the Calm Lake will burn.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_31.t: Absinthe speaks slowly, choosing her words.
The Feuerschwinge Wyrm is not a minor player in the great cycle. The effects of her release will resonate across time and space.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_32.t: Before the next cycle’s end, the Feuerschwinge Wyrm will speak again with the voice of the Maiden. The Dancer will spin, the Seal will melt, and the Calm Lake will burn again. This I prophesy.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_38.t: Before the next cycle’s end, the Feuerschwinge Wyrm will speak again with the voice of the Maiden. The Dancer will spin, the Seal will melt, and the Calm Lake will burn again. This I prophesy.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_48.t: But the Firewing - and the electric godling that controls her - are not.

Within this cycle, the world will rue this event, yet celebrate it nonetheless. The Feuerschwinge Wyrm will speak again with the voice of the Hunter. The Dancer will stumble, the Golden Man will eat 9 and 30, and the Scales will clatter to the ground. This I prophesy.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_56.t: Within this cycle, the world will rue this event, yet celebrate it nonetheless. The Feuerschwinge Wyrm will speak again with the voice of the Hunter. The Dancer will stumble, the Golden Man will eat 9 and 30, and the Scales will clatter to the ground. This I prophesy.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_60.t: No, within this cycle, the world will rue this event, yet celebrate it nonetheless. The Feuerschwinge Wyrm will speak again with the voice of the Crone. The Dancer will fall, the Sky will sing, and the Cold Lands will turn to ash. This I prophesy.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_70.t: We cannot judge this man, nor their actions, Half-Dream. We have lived long enough to know that the spectrum of the world contains myriad shades of gray...

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_71.t: But hear me: Within this cycle, the world will rue this event, though for reasons none can guess. The Feuerschwinge Wyrm will speak again with the voice of a Stranger. The Dancer will falter, the Seal will remain fixed, and the Calm Lake will boil. This I prophesy.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_76.t: No, within this cycle, the world will rue this event, yet celebrate it nonetheless. The Feuerschwinge Wyrm will speak again with the voice of the Crone. The Dancer will fall, the Sky will sing, and the Cold Lands will turn to ash. This I prophesy.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_102.t: A Great Dragon is not like other Awakened creatures. Whereas a basilisk is nothing more than a Komodo dragon whose genetic structure was mutated by magic’s return, a Great Dragon IS a creature of magic. A creature that has seen more than one cycle of the world. This is the sixth.

523c8e7f346238e41d008a2d_122.t: Or that the Half-Dream is willing to impart.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_3.t: As she smiles up at you, her eyes fixed on yours, a curious feeling of weightlessness fills your chest. It feels as though you're floating in a warm, calm sea. A gentle current pulls you closer to Absinthe, and the sensation is pleasant. As you drift, the golden specks in her eyes begin to move.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_7.t: She nods, taking care to avert her gaze. Very well. I have work to do anyway.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_8.t: She nods, taking care to avert her gaze.
You'll want to talk with Aljernon. This is his establishment. He can help you. Now, if you'll excuse me, I have work to do.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_11.t: Welcome back. You are always welcome here.
It's the funniest thing. When I look at you, do you know what I see?

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_12.t: An old primary school, decrepit and crumbling. I see you standing in a basketball court with splintery floors and faded lines.
The air is thick here, stagnant. The whole place smells like an old sock.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_18.t: Absinthe's voice slides into your ears like a living thing.
You won't catch sight of me that way, stranger. My true self is hidden, tucked away behind a veil of time and aether. Be thankful. You wouldn't like what you'd see.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_21.t: Believe me. It is for the best.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_32.t: She stares at you for a moment, then nods.
Very well. The choice is yours.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_35.t: One of the attackers came in here. Did you know that? He pushed his way past Aljernon's wards and forced himself into this shop.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_37.t: I lifted the veil. I showed him my true face, and destroyed his mind.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_46.t: She looks into your eyes, and you can feel your head swimming.

Your kind fears what it can see. Corporations. Dragons. Violence, and poverty, and the random cruelty of fate. If you could perceive reality the way that I do, you would know that there are much greater things to fear.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_48.t: Go now. Talk to Aljernon - after all that has happened, I'm sure that he'll be glad to see you. But remember what I've told you.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_56.t: He left under his own power. Scampered off into the street.
Her shoulders raise in a slight shrug.
The last I saw of him, he was cracking his own skull open with a rock.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_58.t: Was it? Then I am sorry. I didn't mean to offend.
I merely comment on what I see.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_59.t: You are confused. I didn't *do* anything. I merely related what I saw.
What I shared with you... it was an observation. Nothing more.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_62.t: Hold a moment, I'll call Aljernon for you.
She pauses, squinting at you. Her mouth dips into a frown.
Curious... when I look at you, I see red.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_63.t: She nods, and the frown slowly falls off of her face. Her voice becomes dreamy.
Red splattered like paint across great metal arms. Small rooms drenched in it, with droplets oozing down from the ceiling.
She offers you a cautious smile.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_64.t: Welcome back to Aljernon's. Enjoy your time here in the light.
A half-smile flits across her lips.
Soon enough, you'll be wallowing in darkness.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_66.t: She pauses for a moment, considering.
Or am I speaking with another's voice...? Sometimes it's difficult to tell where I end and the dreams begin.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_67.t: Dark things lie ahead for you. You'll wade through a sea of corpses before this night is through, with something at your back that I cannot see.
I... I can't look any longer. I'm sorry.

523c8e98346238e41d008a36_75.t: Absinthe keeps her eyes hidden from view.
Please excuse me. I have work to do.

53d2d63f63613404050083f9_0.t: When you struggle your way back to consciousness, you find yourself lying in a heap on the ground. Absinthe offers a hand to help you to your feet.

53d2d63f63613404050083f9_13.t: She looks into your eyes, and you can feel your head swimming.

Your kind fears what it can see. Corporations. Dragons. Violence, and poverty, and the random cruelty of fate. If you could perceive reality the way that I do, you would know that there are much greater things to fear.

53d2d63f63613404050083f9_15.t: Go now. Talk to Aljernon - after all that has happened, I'm sure that he'll be glad to see you. But remember what I've told you.

527028b86636614822004e81_0.t: Ezkibel's expression brightens when he catches sight of you.
Ah, you're back. Do you have my merchandise?

527028b86636614822004e81_2.t: He greedily snatches the samples from your hands.
It took you long enough... yes, this is *exactly* what I've been waiting for. Good work.

527028b86636614822004e81_11.t: He greedily snatches the samples from your hands.
So you do! Yes... yes, this is *exactly* what I've been waiting for. Good work.

527028b86636614822004e81_13.t: As per our agreement, there will be no monetary compensation for this run. But if you'll give me a moment, I will add the samples that you've recovered to my inventory.
He looks over the samples that you've brought him, and eagerly begins punching their serial numbers into his PDA.
I think that you'll be pleasantly surprised.

527028b86636614822004e81_14.t: And now, if you'll excuse me...
His sentence trails off as he returns his attention to his merchandise.

527028b86636614822004e81_17.t: A flicker of irritation passes over his face. He reluctantly turns back to face you.
Yes, yes... I suppose that I do. Go ahead, tell me. I'm all ears.

527028b86636614822004e81_18.t: That is frustrating. But I suppose that's all water under the bridge. I have my merchandise, after all.

527028b86636614822004e81_21.t: He pauses for a moment, considering, then shrugs.
As far as I'm concerned, he can stay there for as long as he likes. The lease is paid through 2061, and I don't need the building for anything.

527028b86636614822004e81_23.t: I will transfer two thousand nuyen into your account for the job. And if you'll give me a moment, I will add the samples that you've recovered to my inventory.
He looks over the samples that you've brought him, and eagerly begins punching their serial numbers into his PDA.
I think that you'll be pleasantly surprised.

527028b86636614822004e81_25.t: Doctor Ezkibel gives you a once-over and nods.
I see that you made it through the attack relatively unscathed.

527028b86636614822004e81_26.t: As you approach the elf, you notice that he's in mid-conversation. His lips move rapidly, and his voice comes out in a low, quiet tone. The glossy plastic shell of a high-grade commlink glints on his wrist.

527028b86636614822004e81_31.t: He presses a button on his commlink and looks up at you, a million-dollar smile on his face.
Sorry about the wait, my friend. Welcome to the TRIAGE Cyberclinic.

527028b86636614822004e81_32.t: He extends his hand to you.
I am Dr. Xabier Ezkibel. And your name is...?

527028b86636614822004e81_52.t: Why are you trying to talk yourself out of a job?
He rubs the bridge of his nose between his thumb and forefinger.
I can see that you still don't get it, so I'll be blunt. You're the best in town at what you do, full stop. And I need the best, even if that means hiring you.

527028b86636614822004e81_60.t: What didn't they? Theft, medical malpractice, the sale of stolen goods. Other, more colorful accusations that do not bear repeating.

527028b86636614822004e81_76.t: All right. Forget about it. The job is off the table. So do you want something else, or are you leaving?

527028b86636614822004e81_88.t: Ezkibel's eyes widen. His voice explodes out in a harsh whisper.
Shut up! Shut *up,* damn you! Do you want to get us both *killed?*

527028b86636614822004e81_98.t: Please don't call her that. Her name is Kim. And yes, an attacker did break in partway through the operation. He fired his weapon into the wall and shattered some very delicate equipment.

527028b86636614822004e81_104.t: Lucky him, you mean. Poor old guy had the bladder capacity of a pygmy shrew. He was in desperate need of my help. But on to more important things, eh? What can TRIAGE do for you today?

527028b86636614822004e81_107.t: Ezkibel takes the purchase order from your hand and scans it over.
Oh, yes... yes, this arrived just this morning. I can certainly install it for you, but it won't be cheap.

527028b86636614822004e81_108.t: Look, we're talking about neurosurgery here. It's risky, delicate work... a damn sight more difficult than stamping out a chip.

527028b86636614822004e81_110.t: As your doctor, I'm obligated to tell you that I wouldn't recommend it.
But if your mind's made up, be my guest. Go out looking for discount brain surgery. See where that gets you.

527028b86636614822004e81_122.t: The smile dies on his face. He lowers his hand.
I was just being polite. I know who you are... word travels fast in the Kreuzbasar. So tell me. What do you want?

527028b86636614822004e81_124.t: You step away from the doctor and wait for him to finish. Soon enough, he presses a button on his commlink and looks up at you, a million-dollar smile on his face.
Sorry about the wait, my friend. Welcome to the Triage Cyberclinic.

527028b86636614822004e81_126.t: Welcome to the Triage Cyberclinic, my friend.
He extends his hand to you.
I am Dr. Xabier Ezkibel. And your name is...?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_1.t: The distinctive circuit-pattern tattoos that line her face shimmer and pulse with light.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_3.t: Yeah, I know who you are.
She takes a drag on her cigarette.
How you holdin' up, champ? I heard what happened to your boss.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_5.t: She smirks, then lets the smoke escape from the corner of her mouth.
Don't I know it. So... you need something? Or are you just going to stand there staring?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_7.t: I met her a couple of times. Seemed nice enough.
She takes another drag on her cigarette.
But I mostly knew her by reputation. You can't live in this kiez without hearing about the Queen of the Kreuzbasar.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_8.t: She pauses, then slowly lets the smoke escape through her nostrils.
Can't say that I was a fan.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_12.t: Then why did the place start falling apart the second that she got geeked?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_13.t: She takes another drag on her cigarette. The smoke spills out of her mouth as she speaks.
Either Monika controlled the Kreuzbasar, or she had her fingers in so many pies that she might as well have. Comes out to the same thing either way.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_14.t: Lucky Strike taps out her cigarette and leans toward you, a predatory grin on her face.
You and your friends, you followed Monika, too. Obeyed her orders without question. Right?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_17.t: Huh. I've gotta admit, I wasn't expecting that... she was your friend, after all. And you're following right along in her footsteps.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_19.t: Easy there, tiger. No need to get hostile.
She raises her hands in supplication.
And of course, I don't have a problem with you helping your friends.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_21.t: The last thing that we need is for you to eat a bullet, and for this whole place to fall apart again.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_22.t: There is a long pause. Finally, she shrugs.
Fair enough, I suppose. But a word of advice: don't take too much on yourself. Spread the responsibility around.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_23.t: I don't. But in a place like this, stacking too much power on any one person is dangerous. Not just for that person, but for everybody. The last thing that we need is for you to eat a bullet, and for this whole place to fall apart again.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_24.t: Respectable enough, I guess. If your pals are bound and determined to follow someone, it might as well be you, right?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_26.t: I wasn't trying to. Remarkable thing about living in a free society: we're all allowed to have different opinions.
Don't take it personally. I'm sure that you wouldn't like my friends, either.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_28.t: Fair enough. You're probably right - I didn't know her like you do. I'm sure that she was a lovely person. All I really know is that your friend's death left a power vacuum, and that the Kreuzbasar is less stable because of it.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_29.t: Your call. You started talking to me, not the other way around.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_30.t: And at the end of those debates, Monika would put her foot down, and you'd wind up doing whatever she said. Right?
Don't bother answering. It was a rhetorical question.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_33.t: Ooh, touché. All right, let's assume that there *are* a bunch of different things going on.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_35.t: She takes another drag, the light dancing in her eyes.
Oh, was that insensitive of me? Sorry. Guess I thought you'd have thicker skin.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_38.t: She raises a hand in mock-salute.
Here's to your old boss, the least anarchistic "anarchist" I've ever met.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_39.t: Then why did this entire kiez start falling apart the second that she got geeked?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_41.t: Why do you consider hearing my opinions to be "difficult"? Is it because they're different from yours? Maybe I should just tell you what I think that you want to hear instead?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_44.t: She smirks, then lets the smoke escape from the corner of her mouth.
Yeah, well. Sorry for your loss, and all that. A lot of people are still torn up about it. So... you need something? Or are you just going to stand there staring?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_45.t: Yeah. I'm not really the chatty type.
She takes a drag on her cigarette.
How you holdin' up though, champ? I heard what happened to your old boss.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_49.t: She takes a drag on her cigarette.
Well, color me intrigued. What'd you wanna talk about?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_51.t: She tips back her glass and swallows.
Sure, I've done a few jobs for them. They pay well. The whole "ominous secret organization" thing they try to keep going is a little weird, but whatever - money is money, and they've got it.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_53.t: Afraid that I can't help you dig any deeper - he's an occasional client.
She takes a sip of scotch and chases it with a long drag on her cigarette.
Even if I knew more about Duerr, I wouldn't tell you. Shadowrunners who kiss and tell have a funny way of dying young.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_56.t: She gestures with her cigarette, the tip glowing red in the gloom.
I mostly visit your lovely little kiez between runs to restock and relax. It's a good place for that, nice and slow-paced. It's...
She pauses for a moment to search for a word.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_58.t: She takes another sip of scotch.
Better that it stay like this. If you need me for a run, I'm available - you just need to pay me first. Keeps things clean and simple.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_60.t: Not quite. You're a client, not a friend. No offense, mind you. But my crew is back in Mitte, and we're doing just fine on the job front.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_62.t: Me? No. Never developed a taste for it.
She tilts her glass, swirling the amber liquid inside.
I'm not a big fan of sweet things - never have been, not even when I was a kid. I've always preferred smoke.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_64.t: No skin off my back. Ask away.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_65.t: Glad to be of service. Now, do you have another question, or...?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_67.t: Yeah. Sure. Just decided to waltz through a mostly-deserted dive bar on your way from point A to point B.
Sounds plausible. But whatever... was there something that you wanted to talk about?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_68.t: She raises her glass in salute.
Now, was there something that you wanted to talk about...?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_69.t: She releases a plume of smoke from the corner of her mouth.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_73.t: Sure, I've done a few jobs for them. They pay well. The whole "ominous secret organization" thing they try to keep going is a little weird, but whatever - money is money, and they've got it.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_75.t: Afraid that I can't help you dig any deeper - he's an occasional client.
She takes a sip of scotch and chases it with a long drag on her cigarette.
Even if I knew more about Duerr, I wouldn't tell you. Shadowrunners who kiss and tell have a funny way of dying young.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_76.t: They're good people. They get the job done. What else do you need to know?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_78.t: Hähnchen is our decker and requisite tough guy. Troll, huge, covered in tats and piercings - you know the type. I'm pretty sure that he and Barrakuda have a thing going on, but they'll never cop to it.
She presents herself to you with an ironic smile.
And then there's me. Between us, we keep our bases covered.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_83.t: You're a good runner with a good crew - but it'll never be my crew. No hard feelings, though - I'll still moonlight for you whenever you need me.
She lifts the glass to her lips, closes her eyes, and drains it.
You know my price, and you know where to find me.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_86.t: She raises her glass and takes a moment to study the amber liquid inside.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_89.t: Fair enough. Was there something else, or...?

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_90.t: It is now. Wasn't always, though.
She stretches her neck so that the circuit pattern on her face can catch the light.
What you're looking at used to be tattoo magic. Got it done when I was in Tokyo, doing a job for the Yakuza.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_96.t: She pauses for a moment, considering. Finally, she shrugs.
Sure, I'll tell you. Why not? Maybe knowing will keep you from making the same mistake.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_104.t: "Cursed." Huh. Funny choice of words, but I guess that it applies.

534ea4d33234372c130020f0_108.t: Hey, I don't mean to be rude, but this is my off-time. I intend to spend it smoking and drinking myself into a stupor - not talking.
If you wanna chat later, we'll chat. For now, give me some breathing room.

52055c5d303031a4020072b7_2.t: The corner of her mouth lifts into a smirk.
Step aside. Oh, and... I've gotta ask. Is the display on your trid flashing twelve o'clock back at home?

52055c5d303031a4020072b7_3.t: Monika doesn't even bother jacking in to the terminal, and her cyberdeck remains slung over her shoulder. Her fingers flicker over the terminal's virtual keyboard, and a moment later, a happy chime chirps out of the security node's speakers.

520a77823030315011001698_3.t: Monika Schäfer. You ran with her back in the day. Watched her get her first datajack. Now she’s your team leader, and a drek-hot decker to boot.

520a77823030315011001698_10.t: Monika's smile returns, more wistful this time. The moonlight catches her face at a strange angle.

520a77823030315011001698_14.t: You people need to relax. We're professionals, remember?
Monika raises her arm and speaks into her wrist-mounted commlink. A darkened face shimmers on the viewscreen.

520a77823030315011001698_18.t: The commlink goes dark. Monika winks at you as she drops her arm.
See? We're professionals. All right, people. Enough chatter. Our client wants the data from the vault, so we get him the data from the vault. Quick, quiet… and quick.

520a77823030315011001698_23.t: Monika's smile returns, more wistful this time. The moonlight catches her face at a strange angle.

520a77823030315011001698_25.t: Monika's smile returns, more wistful this time. The moonlight catches her face at a strange angle.

520a77823030315011001698_27.t: Monika Schäfer. You ran with her back in the day. Now, she’s your team leader. Your decking skills may be sharp, but hers are novahot. Running with Monika is like taking a masterclass in IC-breaking.

521289d63030314008002d2b_3.t: Monika combs a hand through her hair, parting it to reveal the black plastic sheath of her datajack.
A quick jaunt into the Matrix, a little digital hand-waving, and I'll have this thing wide open. Be right back.

521289d63030314008002d2b_12.t: Monika stares at you for a moment, clearly irritated. Then that twinkle appears in her eye again. She smiles at you as she speaks into her commlink.

521289d63030314008002d2b_15.t: Monika stares at the screen on her wrist for a moment, clearly irritated. Then that twinkle appears in her eye again. She smiles at you as she speaks into her commlink.

521289d63030314008002d2b_18.t: Monika smiles, but shakes her head no.
No offense, but this is my team. And at least for now, I handle the grid work.

521289d63030314008002d2b_20.t: Monika stares at the screen on her wrist for a moment, clearly irritated. Then that twinkle appears in her eye again. She smiles at you as she speaks into her commlink.

521289d63030314008002d2b_23.t: You heard what I said, Eiger.

521289d63030314008002d2b_26.t: Monika's tone is all business. Evidently, she has heard enough.
The decision’s made. You have your answer.

521289d63030314008002d2b_29.t: There's a thin line between concern and insubordination. You let me know if she crosses it.

521289d63030314008002d2b_35.t: Monika turns towards the door, fingers poised on the controls of her cyberdeck, then glances back at you with a grin.
See you on the other side.

521289d63030314008002d2b_38.t: Monika nods at you. The smile has disappeared from her face.

52129dc23030310815002cdb_4.t: Suddenly, Monika's eyes flutter open. Muscle tremors continue to distort her face, and blood oozes between her lips. You see the muscles in her jaw tensing and the look of concentration in her eyes. She's struggling to speak.

52129dc23030310815002cdb_5.t: Slowly, painfully, Monika wrestles her jaw open. The blood welled up in her mouth comes pouring out in a slick, covering her chest. She expels a thick, guttural sound that might be a word. Satisfied, she closes her eyes and forces her mouth to make the shapes she needs.

52129dc23030310815002cdb_6.t: With an effort, Monika opens her eyes again and meets yours. You see pain and fear in her gaze... and something else. Hope?

527abf9e636134b83400302e_214.t: The screen flickers to life, and Monika's face appears. Her hands are on her hips, and she's wearing a wry smile.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_219.t: She half-turns away from the camera, then returns her attention to you. The image is uncannily lifelike.
Oh, and by the way, ace... if you botch this, I'm coming back and haunting your ass. I mean that.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_562.t: The image of Monika appears on the screen.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_563.t: Paul. A talented and well-connected fixer, with contacts all over Berlin.
He's more to me than that, of course. But that's neither here nor there.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_567.t: Can't think of much else to say about Dietrich. Just a good, reliable guy.
She pauses for a moment. Whe she speaks again, you can hear a hint of guilt in her voice.

He is getting a little long in the tooth for a shadowrunner... I'm not sure how many more years he has ahead of him in this game. But I'll be happy to run with him for as long as he's able. We'll see, I suppose.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_569.t: The image of Monika winks onto the screen.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_573.t: Monika pauses for a moment, then shrugs.

That's really all that I know about Glory. She keeps to herself, barely talks, and spends most of her off-time staring at nothing. I've seen this kind of behavior before, of course... something *bad* happened in her past. Deep down, I think she's still running from it. And I'm sure that her cyberware ties into it somehow.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_576.t: The image of Monika winks onto the screen.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_580.t: One of these days, this situation is going to come to a head. I just hope that it doesn't happen soon.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_582.t: The image of Monika smiles on the screen.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_584.t: There aren't many people left from the old days. Far too few. And my secret weapon...
...Well, they was always the best of 'em.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_585.t: She takes a deep breath, then shakes her head with a sigh.
God, that was sappy. Mental note: re-record this before they get a chance to see it.

52a12078626164280300561d_1.t: The voice is drowned out by a flurry of static. Monika's voice shrieks out of the speakers, pleading.
No! Please, ace, don't do it! I never wanted to hurt you... please, don't kill me! I don't want to die *again!*

52a12148626164280300562e_0.t: Monika's voice is alive with excitement.
Almost there, ace! My command sequences have been overwritten! Quick, reconnect the hardline to free me!

52a6a4e0626164280e005b27_0.t: The Monika-image's eyes glimmer with excitement.
Yes... yes! Good job, ace! Control nodes hacked... Matrix connection severed... beginning control sequence rewrite!
It glances over its shoulder at you, and you can see the hunger in its eyes.
Protect that access terminal! Don't let them hurt it!

52b20f7b626164081c0068f6_1.t: The Monika-image reappears on the screen. Her face is contorted in agony.
What have you done, ace?! What have you *DONE?*

52b20f7b626164081c0068f6_3.t: Before I'm gone... there's something that you should see.
She smiles at you, and shafts of light spill out from between her teeth.
The truth. I wanted to tell you earlier, but I couldn't.

52b20f7b626164081c0068f6_15.t: Her face is breaking apart. Rupturing at the seams. Blue-white light floods out through the lesions. When she speaks, her voice is hollow.
That's what you're up against. That's what you need to stop. And if you don't, the F-State will fall.

52b20f7b626164081c0068f6_16.t: I could have helped you, ace. I wish that you'd let me. But now it's too late.
Monika's face splits down the center. Light floods out of the wound to fill the screen.

52b20f7b626164081c0068f6_114.t: You've done it, ace! You've *done it!*
Monika's voice begins to warble and pulse. It deepens. Something about it reminds you of an irregular heartbeat.

52d733b0373937b808003e15_0.t: Monika's panicked voice pours out through the facility's speakers.
One of my security nodes has been reactivated, ace! You'll have to regain control of it before I can continue freeing myself!

52d888ab373937f0430013b7_6.t: Monika's face appears on the console's display. The image wears an expression of pure horror. Her voice shrieks out over the facility's speakers.
What are you doing, ace? Why are you trying to *kill* me? We... we had a *deal!*

52f95704636134041f00dd54_7.t: An all-too-familiar face appears on the screen. She's frowning, a look of incredible sorrow in her eyes.

52f95704636134041f00dd54_13.t: Best of luck with that, ace. Come meet me in the Matrix any time... and if you don't, I'll most certainly come for you.

52f95704636134041f00dd54_15.t: The Monika-image shakes its head sadly.

523b948f346238e41d007555_21.t: The voice bleats out from Amsel's speakers, filling the room.

523b948f346238e41d007555_26.t: The ork with the nightmare face nods.
Send me a comm when the job is done. Audran out.

524da75c303031e410001654_0.t: The ork suddenly pauses, inclining his head to one side and covering his ear with a finger. His Vindicator starts to spin down.
He grunts, briefly tapping the finger against his ear.

524da75c303031e410001654_1.t: Squads three and five report to main entrance. Do *not* let the intruders escape.
Without another word, he slings his weapon over an armored shoulder and turns to leave the room.

528120256361344046004c8b_5.t: The image sharpens. In the center of the screen stands an enormous ork in battle armor, a hand cannon leveled at something just out of frame. His face is a shiny mat of grafted tissue, his smile like something out of a nightmare.

528120256361344046004c8b_12.t: Now we press our advantage. Jam their comms, Ortega.
The sounds of hurried motion recede into the background.
All right, remember, people: Amsel was the brains of the operation, but the rest will put up a lot more fight. Flush 'em out and take 'em down, one by one.

528120256361344046004c8b_14.t: You hear the roar of another gunshot, and the screen goes dark.

528a7f23663661e0250012de_0.t: The scarred ork shouts at you from across the room.
How the *fuck* are you still alive?! And what do you think you're doing down here?

528a7f23663661e0250012de_1.t: His deformed face twists into a vicious smile.
Yeah. Best of luck with that.

528a7f23663661e0250012de_2.t: Oh, I get it! You made a deal, didn’t you? You made a deal with *her.*

528a7f23663661e0250012de_3.t: That bitch in the jar upstairs helped you, and in return, you're gonna help her climb back into her meat body again. Am I right?
And what did she promise you? Gold? Jewels? Eternal life? She offered all of that stuff to me, too. For decades.

528a7f23663661e0250012de_7.t: The ork stares at the dragon's sinuous form with something close to awe.

528a7f23663661e0250012de_8.t: His deformed face twists into a vicious smile.
Doesn’t matter. With that monster's help, I'm gonna change the world. At the very least, the dragons will die, and at most, who knows? We'll just have to wait and see.

52c5eae2663661d81d007fa6_0.t: The burly ork’s face, raw and slick with scar tissue, is contorted in a grimace of pain. You’ve seen this kind of wound before. He doesn’t have long.
Idiots. Forty years... forty years and we finally had a chance to make a difference… and you blew it.

52c5eae2663661d81d007fa6_2.t: He coughs again, writhing in agony on the floor. Then his eyes focus again, and he speaks.

Vauclair was right… the wyrms... taking over the world. But they're just the latest... latest in a long line to fuck us over. Corporations... politicians... dragons... all a big joke, with the rest of us... the punchline.

52c5eae2663661d81d007fa6_3.t: Another bloody cough. Sounds like he's struggling against a collapsed lung.
World... whole world is... going to shit. We're on... on a runaway train to Hell, and someone......Someone has to make it... jump the tracks...

52c5eae2663661d81d007fa6_4.t: He tries to speak, but the only thing that pours from his mouth is blood. A wet whisper, devoid of meaning.

52c5eae2663661d81d007fa6_7.t: My party... I’ll die when I’m ready.

52c5eae2663661d81d007fa6_8.t: He smirks through the pain, then coughs up a great wad of bloody phlegm.
You looking for my secret motivation? Like, maybe... I was burned by a dragon as a kid... and now I want them all dead? Gonna have to... disappoint you.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_3.t: You're gonna have to repeat that.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_14.t: The ork sneers at you from behind the glass, his scarred features writhing in the dancing light of Vauclair's monitor.
Yeah, I saw that. Good job catching the bastard, Doc. I'll take care of him for you, same way I did his old fixer.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_16.t: He looks from you to the scene of carnage in the lab, and a broad grin creeps onto his face.
Somebody have a little accident in there? Looks messy.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_23.t: I find that hard to believe.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_25.t: The ork sneers at you from behind the glass, his scarred features writhing in the dancing light of Vauclair's monitor.
Yeah, I saw that. Good job catching the bitch, Doc. I'll take care of her for you, same way I did her old fixer.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_28.t: The ork sneers at you from behind the glass, his scarred features writhing in the dancing light of Vauclair's monitor.
Looks like you trapped the thugs who were attacking the manor.

52d1da4c636134f049006669_33.t: Audran twists suddenly towards the glass.

52d1da70636134f049006672_5.t: The procedure is about to begin, Doctor.
He smiles, revealing a snarled tangle of pointed teeth.
Would you like to initiate the process yourself? This *is* the culmination of your life's work, after all.

52d1da70636134f049006672_8.t: Audran shrugs. He looks utterly unfazed by Vauclair's display. He lazily raises his pistol.

52d1da70636134f049006672_11.t: Audran turns to stare at you through the glass. His lips curl into an insolent smile.
Now. What to do with you...?

52d1da70636134f049006672_13.t: He knocks twice on the transplast window.
Best of luck with that. You're not going *anywhere,* slag.

52d1da70636134f049006672_14.t: Audran steps toward a console and taps in a series of commands. An ominous rumbling sound fills the room.
Some company for you while I'm gone. I trained them myself.

52d1da70636134f049006672_15.t: And now, if you'll excuse me, I have a job to do. One that I've been waiting a long, *long* time for.
As he turns to leave, he calls back over his shoulder.
Oh, and if you should happen to survive the next few minutes, don't worry... I won't come back to hurt you. That's a promise. I'll just leave you in there to die of thirst.

52d34510323437a433007ab4_0.t: Audran returns, his Vindicator minigun spinning up with a distinctive whir.
Didn’t I tell you that we’re on a timetable here? Now I'm gonna have to kill you myself.

52d604ba6361342c3f0063f9_36.t: Audran turns to stare at you through the glass. His lips curl into an insolent smile.
Hi again. Long time. How’s Amsel doing? That hole in his brainpan heal up okay?

52d604ba6361342c3f0063f9_41.t: The burned ork knocks on the heavy transplast window separating you.
‘Fraid that’s not gonna happen. This lab's locked down tight, like the Doc said. The test subjects that they keep in there don't play well with others. Dangerous, nasty things...

52d604ba6361342c3f0063f9_47.t: Listen, I gotta go. Have fun with the test subjects while I’m gone.
He grins and flips a switch on the console. You hear the hiss of doors sliding open nearby.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_2.t: So. That gunshot I heard... was that the Doc?

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_3.t: He raises an armored hand, then turns to you with a smile. You can see what he's holding - a bottle made of thick green glass.
He raises the bottle to his lips and takes a swig. Then he passes it to you.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_8.t: Better. Worse. Who the fuck cares? The world was going to hell anyway.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_10.t: His grin widens, and the light plays sickeningly off of his scar tissue.
The world's never done shit for me. Everything I am, I had to take for myself. So I say, fuck the world.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_11.t: He closes his eyes and takes another long drink.
I'm looking forward to watching it die.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_12.t: ...'Course, if you prefer, you could always follow the Doc's example. Take the coward's way out.
He pats a pistol on his hip. You recognize it instantly - it's the same hand cannon that he used to kill Amsel.
Guess I couldn't fault you for it. Things are gonna get ugly over the next few years.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_14.t: He hands you the bottle with a smile.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_19.t: He removes the weapon from its holster and passes it to you. It feels heavy in your hands.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_20.t: He turns back to the observation window.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_24.t: You cling to that high horse, pal. It'll do you a lot of good when everything starts falling apart around you.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_32.t: Changed. Doomed. Who the fuck cares? The world was going to hell anyway.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_33.t: Audran shrugs, then accepts the bottle.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_36.t: Really? That's what you've saved for the occasion? Well, it's your poison.

53bae97e636134dc2700036e_39.t: I'm guessing you lifted that from someone with much better taste.

52c5d802643431411bb69ee2_6.t: The man inclines his head slightly in introduction.

52c5d802643431411bb69ee2_7.t: My employer also wishes to hear about the Lady Feuerschwinge. A fascinating creature - so unlike her brothers and sisters.

52c5d802643431411bb69ee2_13.t: He inclines his head, and the corner of his mouth curls into a bit of a smile.

52c5d802643431411bb69ee2_14.t: He inclines his head, and the corner of his mouth curls into a bit of a smile.

52c5d802643431411bb69ee2_15.t: This is undeniable… and as it should be. The Great Dragons are very old and very powerful and very, very cunning. A cunning unknowable to you.

52c5d802643431411bb69ee2_16.t: He inclines his head, and the corner of his mouth curls into a bit of a smile.

52c5d802643431411bb69ee2_17.t: “Just another taker.” Interesting. Dragons take, this is undeniable, but they cannot be considered “just another taker,” as you put it.

52c5d802643431411bb69ee2_22.t: You will live and die between the beats of a dragon’s heart. What hope do you have of understanding them?

52c5d802643431411bb69ee2_23.t: Indeed she was. Indeed. She. Was.

52ab4537643431431fd37135_63.t: Her expression grows stern for a moment as she stares into her gin and tonic.
Maybe I’ve been down here long enough. Maybe I’m a ghost too.

52ab4537643431431fd37135_67.t: She turns to you with a shrug.
Sorry I couldn't get you anything else, but thanks for being a pal.

52ab4537643431431fd37135_69.t: Nice pun. All right, suit yourself, dude.
She raises her glass to you.

52ab4537643431431fd37135_71.t: She takes a swig from her glass, then eyes it suspiciously.
There’s *got* to be better boozeahol in Madrid.

52ab4537643431431fd37135_72.t: Her eyes widen, and she puts up her hands as though to push away her statements.

Whoa there, I didn’t mean to strike a nerve! It’s just the things like the turf wars and the racists and junk. Like those Humanis assholes, you know? Unnnn-pleasant. But that shit aside, I like the idea of a state without the Man.

52ab4537643431431fd37135_75.t: She laughs a little and takes a swig.
Not to say you're rich! But, if that’s who’s got your stuff, this is as far away from medieval England as you’re gonna get.

52ab4537643431431fd37135_76.t: Good luck finding it, man. I’ll be here if you need to drown your sorrows.
She flashes a grin at you and returns to pondering her drink.

52ab4537643431431fd37135_77.t: Suit yourself. If you change your mind though, I swear I’ll be a perfect gentleman… erm… gentlewoman.
She winks broadly, then returns to pondering her drink.

52ab4537643431431fd37135_78.t: Goldfish is warm and welcoming despite her nasal American accent.
Hey again, friend. I'd buy you a drink, but you heard the man... I've been cut off.

52ab4537643431431fd37135_79.t: Goldfish is warm and welcoming despite her nasal American accent.
Welcome back! Here for a beer?

52532b32626164681700018f_0.t: The door slides open, and the stench of stale sweat assaults your nostrils. Enstad steps forward, his bloody lips curled into a smile. The soles of his boots squeak on the polished tile floor.

52532b32626164681700018f_1.t: His smile widens into a sickly grin. Tar-stained teeth shine wetly in the light.
Didn't think I'd warrant a rescue. Whoever sent you, thank 'em for me.

52532b32626164681700018f_2.t: His eyes go wide. He raises his trembling hands in surrender.
Whoa there, chief. I... I don't know what your client told you...

52532b32626164681700018f_8.t: His hands clasp in front of him in a sign of supplication. You can see the terror in his eyes.

52532b32626164681700018f_15.t: Oh, for Christ's sake. Haven't *you* ever been on a job that went south?
Yeah. I can tell from that look in your eyes that you have. I'm no different than you are, pal... I just had the bad luck to take a job from the wrong client.

52532b32626164681700018f_16.t: He wets his lips with his thick, pale tongue and clears his throat.
Okay. I'm a rigger, right? Your client probably told you that already. But I'm also an inventor... a trained engineer. Fuchs probably didn't mention that, did he?

52532b32626164681700018f_21.t: Fifteen, huh? I... I can match that. You let me live, and I'll pay your wage. *Plus* the inducer. Hell, you could even tell your client I'm dead and bill him, too. *Double* your earnings. Whaddaya think about that?

52532b32626164681700018f_24.t: It's a narrow-band microwave projector. Causes debilitating pain in a target, and screws with the electrical systems of drones and the like. Think of it like a taser, only easier to use, and meaner, and a whole LOT more fun.

52532b32626164681700018f_30.t: He nods, eyes wide, and licks his lips again.

52532b32626164681700018f_42.t: His eyes go wide, and his voice becomes shrill.
Yeah, but... did he tell you that we did exactly what he told us to? Did he tell you that it was *his* bad intel that got me hung out to dry like this?

525378a1626164c822001545_0.t: Enstad slows to a stop at the window. His eyes are locked on the stack of corpses piled up on the cell floor.

525378a1626164c822001545_7.t: Yeah, they often do. They don't do it *well,* mind you. But they do.

525378a1626164c822001545_9.t: He rubs his hands together excitedly.

525378a1626164c822001545_11.t: Might've. Can't say one way or another until you show me what you're talkin' about.

525378a1626164c822001545_14.t: He lets out a low chuckle.
No loss, believe me. I hated those pricks.

525378a1626164c822001545_15.t: A deep rumbling sound emanates from the rigger's chest. He spits, and an enormous wad of phlegm spatters against the upturned face of the nearest corpse.

525378a1626164c822001545_17.t: If you knew 'em, you'd do the same. Miserable bastards, the lot of 'em... no appreciation for creativity.

52a8a94f636134242200491f_0.t: From the description that your client gave you, this must be your target. The rigger, Thorvald Enstad. You can see him waving and pounding on the plastic of his cell door, but from where you're standing, the scene is eerily silent.

52a8a94f636134242200491f_1.t: He looks like he's had a rough go of things. One of his eyes has been blackened, and his lower lip is split wide open. Not particularly surprising, given the temperament of his captors.

52a8a94f636134242200491f_2.t: The only incongruous element is his outfit. He's decked out, head to toe, in patched black riding leathers. A pair of oversized, studded combat boots shine in the halogen glare of the cell's lighting panels.

52a8a94f636134242200491f_3.t: From his style of dress, it's obvious that this dwarf is not a corporate employee. He's a shadowrunner.

52a90f6b6361340408008351_1.t: He looks you dead in the eye, and for a moment his expression remains unchanged. Then his smile broadens, and a stream of giggles comes pouring out of him.

52a90f6b6361340408008351_2.t: Okay! Okay, yeah, you caught me.
Red-faced, he wipes a tear from the corner of his eye.
This... this was one of mine. Couldn't tell you how excited I was to see this stuff... such incredible toys! It was WAY too good an opportunity to pass up.

52a90f6b6361340408008351_3.t: This was a *fun* one. Little prick couldn't believe it when I set the autosurgeon on him. He kept tryin' to get away, but wherever he went, one of my arms was there. Rrrrrrrrip. Rrrrrrrrip.

52a90f6b6361340408008351_4.t: And all the while, he was screaming like a little girl. "Eeeeeeeee! Eeeeeeeee!" Must've kept at it for... oh, ten, fifteen minutes? "Eeeeeeeee!"
He barks out a snorting laugh.
It was hilarious, man. I tell you.

52a90f6b6361340408008351_6.t: I'll point out other stuff when we see it... there was this one guy that I locked in a room with a manipulator arm...
He wipes a tear from the corner of his eye.

52a90f6b6361340408008351_8.t: The fuck is he talkin' about? We have a deal...

52a90f6b6361340408008351_13.t: The fuck is he talkin' about? We have a deal...

52a90f6b6361340408008351_17.t: The mirth has disappeared from the rigger's face. In its place are incredulity and anger.
The fuck is she talkin' about? I *told* you...

52a90f6b6361340408008351_22.t: The fuck is she talkin' about? We have a deal...

52a90f6b6361340408008351_25.t: The rigger's eyes widen in surprise.
Well, screw you, too. Didn't mean to offend your delicate sensibilities. I guess I just assumed that you'd have a sense of humo-

52a90f6b6361340408008351_26.t: Yeah, all right. Actually, hang here a second... I stashed the Pain Inducer just over there.
He waves his hand in the direction of the operating theater.
Your nuyen, too. I'll go grab it for you.

52a90f6b6361340408008351_30.t: His smile broadens, and a stream of giggles comes pouring out of him.
No, it's... uh... it's pretty horrible stuff, man.

52a90f6b6361340408008351_31.t: Well, I don't know. Why don't you tell me?
He looks you dead in the eye, his expression neutral. Then that sickly grin returns, and he starts to giggle again.

52aa51e3636134881b00602e_0.t: All riiiight! Good choice, m'friend. *Good* choice.
He takes a few steps forward and kicks the Company Man's corpse in the head.
I'll just nip into the other room and grab your payment for you. Then we can all be on our way.

52abb63966366194290053d5_4.t: Hell, just yesterday I heard someone claim they saw a member of the Rabengeister speed by in the U-Bahn tunnel, clinging to the side of a train car. Ha!

52abb63966366194290053d5_6.t: He shakes his head with amusement.
That's the Flux State for you though, full of the strange and unexpected.

52abb63966366194290053d5_7.t: Wish that I had, friend. It's too bad. This place used to be *soaring* with commerce. But now... now hardly anything passes through here, I'm afraid.

52abb63966366194290053d5_11.t: No... I don't think so, friend. But I thank you for the gesture.
The man squints at you. He looks thoughtful.

52abb63966366194290053d5_14.t: He was stealing from the French, you see.

52abb63966366194290053d5_23.t: Gesundbrunnen? Well, it's my home, for one. And we've got a small bar set up over there. But of course... you'll want to know about the market!

52abb63966366194290053d5_26.t: The man looks up from his book, smiling.

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_3.t: He stares you down for a long moment, then breaks into a chuckle.

Aye, I'll grant you we've been a little careless. I guess we figured with Monika Schäfer out of the picture, the Kreuzbasar would be an easy score. That's why you're here, right? We stole your supplies, now you want 'em back?

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_14.t: He pauses for a long moment, then cracks a smile.

Very well, I suppose that's worth the price of a crate of guns. Your medical supplies are already mostly gone, I'm afraid. But the weapons shipment is still being held in a locker at Frankfurter Tor. I'll have it sent back to the Kreuzbasar immediately.

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_17.t: He pauses for a long moment, then cracks a smile.

Very well, I suppose that's worth the price of a crate of guns. Your medical supplies are already mostly gone, I'm afraid. But the weapons shipment is still being held in a locker at Frankfurter Tor. I'll have it sent back to the Kreuzbasar immediately.

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_19.t: Maybe things are looking up around here. I suppose friends and enemies both can be found in strange ways, here in the Flux.

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_22.t: Quorin rolls his eyes suddenly, then turns to his crew.
Did you imbeciles *really* not search them for weapons? For fuck's sake.

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_23.t: Regardless. I had hoped to avoid bloodshed, but we cannot back down on this. Sorry, stranger.
Quorin sighs and draws his rifle.

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_24.t: He stares you down for a long moment, then breaks into a chuckle.
Not bad, right? We've been running jobs for this kiez for years, but we just started using this bunker as a permanent base. Nice and secure, easy place to hole up in.

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_25.t: This place is over a hundred years old, y'know. During the Second World War, they converted this part of the station into an air raid shelter. Made it nice and secure in case anyone spotted bombers over the city.
Quorin appears more relaxed, his tone more friendly. He seems to enjoy relating the history of this place.

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_26.t: *Then,* during the Cold War, they designated this whole station an emergency fallout shelter. You know, in case the Russians and the Americans decided to blow each other up. This was back when Berlin was divided in two, of course. Things got better for a while after that. Hell, a few decades ago, before the Awakening, I hear they used to give tours.

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_29.t: He looks irritated, then grows serious again.
Fine. You're from the Kreuzbasar. We stole your supplies, and now you want them back. That about right?

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_30.t: He stares you down for a long moment, then breaks into a chuckle.
Guess we were a little careless on that one. I figured with Monika Schäfer gone, the Kreuzbasar would be an easy score. So that's why you're here? We stole your supplies, now you want 'em back?

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_31.t: He chuckles, the cold stare evaporating.
You think a gang like us, we've only got one secret passage? There's all *kinds* of fun hidden passages down here.

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_35.t: The man before you is of average build, in nondescript dress. Nevertheless, he carries himself with a calm authority.

52af8d2d663661f82000102f_37.t: That option closed when you spilled blood in this kiez.

52af576d6161362808001bbe_0.t: An old couple tends to one of the many communal gardens scattered through the Kreuzbasar. They appear to be in their late sixties or early seventies, though it is increasingly hard to determine such things in this day and age. The woman is the first to notice you.
Oh! Mein Schatz, we have a guest.

52af576d6161362808001bbe_8.t: The old woman shakes her head in denial and opens her mouth to argue. But when the man puts his hands on her shoulders, she crumbles in resignation.
I... I understand. Thank you for telling us.

52af576d6161362808001bbe_17.t: The old woman's thin shoulders slump, making her seem even smaller than she already is.
She can't be gone. She... she's the heart of this place.

52af576d6161362808001bbe_80.t: Kristian and Karin Beck walk arm in arm, enjoying a stroll in the unseasonably warm air.

52af576d6161362808001bbe_88.t: Karin turns red in the face, positively fuming.
We know she was beaten. Assaulted by some cowardly drug dealer. (w)

521d44a83030319015005ab9_3.t: We're just, uh, improvising. Trying to stay in business.
He gives Mira a significant glance.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_7.t: He stares at you for a moment before speaking.
Look... you're obviously a professional. We work at a soykaf shop. We don't know what the hell we're doing. Mira likes to talk tough...

521d44a83030319015005ab9_10.t: He looks down at Mira and shakes his head.
I'm sorry, Mira, but I can't afford to be optimistic. And to be honest, you can't, either. Danni needs help, whether you want to admit it or not.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_21.t: Mira. Look at him. That's a *professional.* He can help us.
He takes a step toward you, his arms wide.
Please, man. We *do* need your help. She just doesn't want to admit it.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_23.t: Oh, thank God. And thank you, too.
Give them the spare keycard that Danni bought. they's going to fix this for us.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_34.t: Mira. Look at her. That's a *professional.* She can help us.
He takes a step toward you, his arms wide.
Please, ma'am. We *do* need your help. She just doesn't want to admit it.

521e77003030318403005ff8_2.t: While Danni and Mira are clearly furious, Heimerich just looks tired. He shakes his head.
Why'd you do it? You know that we can't fight the Azzies. You *know* what they're going to do to us.

521e77003030318403005ff8_11.t: All right. Yeah. You do all of that, and all is forgiven. We're golden. But if you don't, a *lot* of innocent people are going to get hurt.

521e77003030318403005ff8_12.t: He stares you in the eye.
Unless you want our blood on your hands, you *do* this. You hear me? For the love of God, please, just do it.

521e77003030318403005ff8_15.t: All right. There is one thing you can do for us. The only thing that can make this right. I need you to toss the Azzies' utility logs.

521e77003030318403005ff8_24.t: While Mira is clearly furious, Heimerich just looks tired. He shakes his head.
Why'd you do it? You know that we can't fight the Azzies. You *know* what they're going to do to us.

521e77003030318403005ff8_35.t: All right. There is one thing you can do for us. The only thing that can make this right. I need you to toss the Azzies' utility logs.

521e77003030318403005ff8_46.t: All right. There is one thing you can do for us. The only thing that can make this right. I need you to toss the Azzies' utility logs.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_131.t: The screen flickers to life, and Heimerich's face appears. He looks pale and haggard, and his eyes are rimmed with red.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_135.t: The screen flickers to life, and Heimerich's face appears. He raises a liter of beer to the screen in salute.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_181.t: The screen flickers to life, and Heimerich's face appears. He raises a liter of beer to the screen in salute.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_185.t: The screen flickers to life, and Heimerich's face appears. He looks pale and haggard, and his eyes are rimmed with red.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_0.t: The attendant behind the counter is an uncommonly fine-featured dwarf with a beatific smile. A yellow ID tag pinned to his chest announces his name in large, blocky letters: "PARSON." He nods at you and raises a hand in greeting.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_4.t: Communion is a spiritual experience, friend. Available to all for a small donation.
He gestures at the banks of terminals lining the walls.
The Hub acts as the core of the Communion. Those who jack into it are in turn linked to one another. Daisy-chained together at the brain stem.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_6.t: Imagine it, friend. Dozens of individual minds, all tied together into a single, cohesive whole. Sharing in a collective subconscious, connected through the Hub.
Parson raises his hands in supplication.
Join with us, and feel your limitations fall away.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_7.t: Parson inclines his head. His smile does not falter.
Whenever you are ready, you may return. Is there anything more that I can help you with in the meantime?

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_8.t: The dwarf's benevolent smile does not falter.
You are quite mistaken. The Hub is a place of peace. We are in no danger here.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_11.t: All at once, Parson's face goes blank. The benevolent smile dies away, and he begins to speak in a monotone.
The Hub is currently closed for routine maintenance. Please come back again later.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_12.t: The blank expression remains for a moment, and then, just like that, it's gone. The familiar, peaceful smile returns.
Oh! Yes, hello. The Hub is currently off limits... it must have slipped my mind. I'm so sorry that I didn't mention it earlier.
Parson's expression changes. You see confusion, bordering on panic. He shakes it off a moment later.
Is... is there anything else that I can do for you?

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_16.t: We do seem to have more than our fair share. Very well, I will answer your questions.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_20.t: If you choose to go exploring, I can't guarantee your safety.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_24.t: Parson lets out a heavy sigh.
A pack of snarling animals dressed up as a policlub. I'd steer clear of them if I were you.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_29.t: Now that they've claimed the upper levels of the building, it isn't safe to travel beyond the ground floor. Several members of our congregation have been mauled to death by the Magnifikers' pet spirits.
Now we travel only in groups.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_35.t: The smile fades from Parson's face.
Trithemius is a dangerous man... why do you ask?

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_44.t: Killing me will only allow me to join forever with the Communion, and you will have destroyed the only source of the information you require. Get me my parts, and you'll have your information.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_51.t: He studies your face for a moment, then nods.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_54.t: Beyond the Hub, there isn't much to speak of. There's a small shop down the hall if you're hungry, or in need of basic supplies.
He shrugs and offers you an apologetic smile.
And that's about it, I'm afraid.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_59.t: Very well. Perhaps another time. Now, is there anything else that you could use from me...?

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_66.t: As suddenly as it disappeared, the smile returns.
Yes. These are the parts that I need. Thank you, friend. I trust that there was no trouble? That you were able to attain them without bloodshed?

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_68.t: Oh, that is wonderful news. Truly, truly wonderful! And now, I have my end of the deal to uphold.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_72.t: Because that abandoned apartment was once mine. In the days before I discovered the peace of Communion, I was a very different man. I had need of such contrivances.
But not anymore. Finally, I am at peace.

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_82.t: Parson waves at you in greeting.
Hello again, brother. What can I do for you?

52853f33626164bc0b0064fa_83.t: Parson waves at you in greeting.
Hello again, sister. What can I do for you?

52aa4522626164fc160010af_0.t: Parson rushes at you. His eyes are blank, and his face is an expressionless mask. A stream of thin drool runs down the corner of his mouth.
The Communion must be protected! You are a threat to the Communion! YOU MUST DIE!

52152ed0663661680f0001ec_0.t: The stranger smiles and raises his drink in salute.

52152ed0663661680f0001ec_7.t: The stranger offers you a broad smile and a shrug.

52152ed0663661680f0001ec_12.t: The stranger offers you a broad smile and a shrug.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_1.t: Damn it. I knew this was gonna happen. I fuckin' knew it.
He cranes his neck around to look you in the eye.
Everybody pile out of the van. And get ready... we're gonna need some muscle in a second.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_5.t: Maxim licks his lips, his eyes scanning over the gangers' hastily assembled roadblock.
This kiez belongs to Surfturf, slag. Has for months. You'd better clear on out of our way before they show up and force you out.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_7.t: Maxim's expression turns stony, but his voice remains calm.

Congratulations on your victory. You must've taken some losses in the fight, though... I'm betting you're not at a hundred percent. Why don't you let us cross the bridge in peace? There's no need for any bloodshed today.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_10.t: Maybe I was wrong. Maybe this *does* need to get violent.
Get ready to ventilate these assholes. We've got a schedule to keep.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_11.t: He nods and draws his weapon.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_12.t: Maxim's voice comes out in a roar.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_13.t: Maxim's eyes go wide, and he begins to backpedal away from you.
Damn you. God damn you to hell.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_16.t: Maxim wheels around to face you, his face a mask of fury. His voice explodes out in a harsh whisper.
Quiet the fuck down! You don't speak for me, and you don't speak for this organization!

525c3d08303031d410005d43_17.t: Maxim stares at you, bristling, but he holds his tongue. After a few seconds, he nods.
Do it. But you'd better not fuck this up.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_21.t: He shakes his head at you.
You want to pay them, that's your business. But that money ain't coming out of our cut.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_23.t: Do you even *have* 750 nuyen? 'Cause that ain't coming out of *our* cut.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_24.t: He shakes his head at you.
You wanna pay them, that's your business. I still think you're crazy, but I won't say no.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_25.t: Maxim's frown instantly disappears, replaced with a toothy grin.
I like you. You're gonna go far in this business.
He draws his weapon, cocks it, and turns to face the rest of his crew.

525d6f9e303031bc0a0072cd_0.t: Maxim wipes his face with a handkerchief. The cloth comes away wet with blood-streaked sweat.
Well. That was... unpleasant. All a part of the job, though, eh?

525d6f9e303031bc0a0072cd_3.t: Couldn't have said it better myself.

525ddfa63030310818005fa5_8.t: You've gotta be kidding me. That's a *Humanis* compound, pal... you go in there, and you aren't gonna come back out.

525ddfa63030310818005fa5_11.t: You're not hired muscle at all, are you? You're a shadowrunner, here on a job.

525ddfa63030310818005fa5_12.t: You're serious, aren't you? You're really going in there.
Well, be my guest... I won't get in your way. When you make it back - *if* you make it back - we'll be out here waiting for you.

525ddfa63030310818005fa5_13.t: He shakes his head at you, then shrugs.
Be my guest, go on inside. I won't try to stop you. When you make it back - *if* you make it back - we'll be out here waiting for you.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_139.t: The screen flickers to life, and Maxim's face appears. He's wearing a broad grin, and you can hear the sounds of celebration in the background.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_141.t: Anyway, just thought you'd want to share in the good news. And thanks for your help back there... I don't think we'd've made it out of there without you.
He raises a hand in salute.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_191.t: Anyway, just thought you'd want to share in the good news. And thanks for your help back there... I don't think we'd've made it out of there without you.
He raises a hand in salute.

52b2266f346238601800433d_4.t: Ezkibel tries to hide the surprise in his voice. He doesn't do a very good job.
What... what are you doing on this line, my friend?

52b2266f346238601800433d_10.t: Do that, and you'll be getting your ghoul friends killed! The Mettbachs will storm down there with their arsenal and wipe them out!

52b2266f346238601800433d_11.t: There is a pause. When he speaks again, his tone is guarded.
What do you mean by that?

52b2266f346238601800433d_13.t: There is a long pause. Finally, you hear a sigh.

52b2266f346238601800433d_14.t: Tell your friends that I've changed my mind. Their feedings will resume directly, at no additional cost.
There is a click as he disconnects the line.

52b2266f346238601800433d_22.t: There is a short pause, then he replies in a bemused voice.

52b2266f346238601800433d_24.t: The line goes quiet for a moment.
I might be able to work with that.

52b2266f346238601800433d_32.t: To keep them fed indefinitely, you mean?
