52b2266f346238601800433d_33.t: In light of my desire to foster a good working relationship with you, and the ghouls' past service to the Kreuzbasar, I suppose that I could continue feeding them for... oh, say...

52b2266f346238601800433d_42.t: "Indefinitely" is a long time, my friend.
But maybe we can work something out.

52b2266f346238601800433d_43.t: In light of my desire to foster a good working relationship with you, and the ghouls' past service to the Kreuzbasar, I suppose that I could continue feeding them for... oh, say...
Another pause, punctuated by a soft tutting sound.

52b2266f346238601800433d_54.t: It's never a good idea to threaten your doctor. You never know what might happen the next time you're lying on the operating table.

52b2266f346238601800433d_55.t: There is a long pause. Finally, you hear a sigh. When Ezkibel speaks again, his voice heavy with resignation.

52b2266f346238601800433d_59.t: Ezkibel tries to hide the surprise in his voice. He doesn't do a very good job.

52b2266f346238601800433d_61.t: Ezkibel tries to hide the surprise in his voice. He doesn't do a very good job.

521bde1f3030316814004b4f_4.t: Brave words, but her tone tells a different story. It's plain as day that she's worried sick.

521bde1f3030316814004b4f_9.t: We could have, sure. Before Danni spent our last handful of nuyen on those forged keycards.

521bde1f3030316814004b4f_10.t: She straightens up. Puts on a brave face.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_0.t: She looks up at you, her expression guarded.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_2.t: She gestures broadly at her own body.
Do we *look* like shadowrunners to you?

521d44a83030319015005ab9_5.t: That place is everything to us. We live there, we work there. It's all that we've got.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_11.t: The facade cracks, and Mira's face crumples.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_13.t: You *knew* that we didn't have any money! Why the hell did you string us along like that if you were only going to say no?

521d44a83030319015005ab9_16.t: Heimerich opens his mouth to speak, then seems to think better of it.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_20.t: We'll be *fine.* What we don't need is a *stranger* butting into our business. So just go away, all right? Have a nice day.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_24.t: You can see the mistrust in her eyes, but after a moment's hesitation, she begins to fish around in her bag. Finally, she produces a thick plastic keycard. She hands it to you.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_28.t: Screw you! We *don't* need you. We don't even *know* you! For all we know, you could be an Aztechnology spy!

521d44a83030319015005ab9_31.t: Mira stares at you, her eyes full of mistrust. Then something changes, and the brave facade cracks. Her face crumples.
Yeah. I know. And I'm worried about Danni.

521d44a83030319015005ab9_32.t: She fishes in her bag for a moment, then produces a thick plastic keycard. She hands it to you.
This'll get you into the utility tunnel. Danni went down there about a half an hour ago.

521e729d3030318403005fb6_4.t: She reaches up. Grabs Danni's hand. Clutches it tight.

521e729d3030318403005fb6_7.t: Mira looks up at you, desperation in her eyes.

521e729d3030318403005fb6_11.t: She starts in the direction of the utility hatch.

521e729d3030318403005fb6_13.t: She collapses to her knees, sobbing.

521e729d3030318403005fb6_14.t: Mira looks up at you, her face contorted in grief.
*You* get the hell *out* of here! GET OUT! And DON'T COME BACK!

521e77003030318403005ff8_16.t: Yeah, that'd do it. And while you're at it, plant false evidence to make it look like the power surge came over the grid, rather than from our tap.

521e77003030318403005ff8_19.t: Mira looks up at you, desperation in her eyes.

521e77003030318403005ff8_23.t: She wheels on you, her face contorted in a mix of grief and rage.

521e77003030318403005ff8_29.t: Still blinking away the tears, Mira cuts in.

521e77003030318403005ff8_36.t: Yeah. Yeah, that'd do it. And while you're at it, plant false evidence to make it look like the power surge came over the grid, rather than from our tap.

521e77003030318403005ff8_38.t: Her face crumples, and she begins to sob.
Get the hell out of here. I never want to see your face on our property again.

521e77003030318403005ff8_47.t: Yeah, that'd do it. And while you're at it, plant false evidence to make it look like the power surge came over the grid, rather than from our tap.

521e77003030318403005ff8_49.t: Her face crumples, and she begins to sob.
Get the hell out of here. I never want to see your face on our property again.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_2.t: I put six of 'em in the ground personally. And when I find out who they are... where they *lived*...

523b9421346238e41d00754c_3.t: He shakes his head, and returns his attention to you.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_5.t: The Romani patriarch is an impressive figure: an enormous man in his late sixties, burly and broad-chested despite his age. His voice is deep and resonant, and his breath is heavy with the stench of pipe tobacco.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_10.t: You show me respect. This is good.
Now. Unless you'd like to take yours elsewhere, let's get back to business, eh?

523b9421346238e41d00754c_12.t: Mettbach's face flushes purple. His slate-gray eyes narrow to slits.
The Mettbach family loves its customers. But if you don't buy anything, you're not a damned customer, are you?
From the set of Mettbach's jaw, you can see that he's furious.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_15.t: There is a brief pause as Mettbach considers your request. Finally he nods with a grunt.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_16.t: Aye. A gang from a nearby kiez has been hijacking shipments bound for the Kreuzbasar, takin' money out of *our* pockets. I could use your help to stop this from happening.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_22.t: Not much, I'm afraid. From what I've heard... the Rabengeister are tricky. They have a reputation for stirring up trouble, then disappearing without a trace. I would tread carefully in Gesundbrunnen if I were you.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_28.t: A faint smile plays across the normally taciturn merchant's face.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_31.t: A faint smile plays across the normally taciturn merchant's face.
Yes, and my people have already retreived the shipment - exactly where you said it would be. Good work. How were you able to manage that?

523b9421346238e41d00754c_32.t: A cold smile flickers across his face.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_34.t: The Romani patriarch gives you an approving nod.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_35.t: A cold smile flickers across his face.
Good. Sometimes a little mess is... necessary.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_37.t: Indeed? That is good news. Were you able to retreive the shipment of weapons they stole as well?

523b9421346238e41d00754c_38.t: And you just let them get away with this?

523b9421346238e41d00754c_40.t: But... I suppose what's done is done. Very well. I won't question your decision.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_42.t: You make a purchase, you can ask as many questions as you like.

Make a big enough purchase, and who knows? I might even answer a few.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_45.t: Mettbach's face hardens. After a brief pause, he gives you a curt nod.
Very well, gadjo. But if you come 'round again, you come with money and you come to buy, understand?
He makes a shooing motion with his hand, waving you away.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_46.t: Mettbach's face hardens. After a brief pause, he gives you a curt nod.

523b9421346238e41d00754c_47.t: Very well. Until next time, then.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_1.t: She appears as Amsel described her: an impressive-looking woman of Aztlaner descent, with coffee-colored skin and dark, wavy hair. Everything about her screams "corporate executive," from her expensive-looking suit to her tastefully understated makeup. She's obviously nervous, but she's hiding it well. She locks eyes with you and hesitates only slightly before speaking.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_3.t: She holds her silence for a moment, then nods.
Yes. Yes, I do. In truth, I'm in desperate need of your help.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_4.t: She offers you a small smile.
An assumed name, obviously. But I have been told that this is how you people do business.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_5.t: She straightens in her seat and tries on a smile. It hangs there for a second, then fades away. She sighs.
From my accent, you’ve no doubt inferred that I’m an Aztlaner. Further, you may have guessed who I work for.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_6.t: I’ve been with the company for over a decade. In that time, I’ve worked on all manner of projects. From the innocuous to the obscene, my hands have touched it all.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_10.t: She looks you in the eye, and for the first time, you can see the cracks in her composure. She's doing a good job of hiding it, but deep down, this woman is terrified.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_13.t: She takes a deep breath, then lets it out slowly.
Seeing such things… it changes you. Makes you numb. But what I found when I arrived here was worse. This project… it horrifies me. And it must be stopped.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_14.t: The less that you know, the better. For both of us. You'll just have to trust me on that.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_17.t: She leans in and looks you in the eye.
I want you to bring the building down.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_19.t: I'm sure that you have unanswered questions. I'm not in the habit of dealing with shadowrunners. In truth, I've spent the majority of my career living in fear of you people. But I do know that you're the only ones who can get this done.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_35.t: In that case, it's a deal! I can't tell you how relieved I am... thank you for helping me to bring this nightmare to an end.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_37.t: Then I've thrown away my life for nothing.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_40.t: Then the project will reach completion, and nobody in your line of work will ever be safe again.
I will leave my contact information with your Herr Amsel in case you change your mind. I hope that you'll reconsider... for all of our sakes.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_41.t: They won't have to. My employers have a way of seeing the truth of things. Of reading the intent behind a person's eyes. When I took this step, I signed my own death warrant.

52b0f33b636134f804001950_44.t: She leans in and looks you in the eye.
I want you to bring the building down.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_1.t: Anyway. Thanks for chasing 'em off, pal... I owe you one.
He bites his lip. The anxiety is plain on his face.
I guess that we all do.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_3.t: Ol' Zaak had to use his magic on a few of 'em. See that puddle over there?
He lazily points to a yellow-green stain on the cobblestones, then gives you a slow nod.
Yeah, that was one of 'em. Attacker, thy name is "goo."

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_4.t: Zaak bows down with a flourish.
Thank you, friend. Thank you. So, what can ol' Zaak getcha?

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_5.t: Yeah, sure. That's what it looks like to *you.* If you had the Sight, you'd know better. Anyway. What can ol' Zaak get for you?

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_6.t: One of these days. Absolutely. But today, you're here for a reason. What can ol' Zaak get for you?

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_7.t: This elf has clearly seen better days. His skin is weathered and emaciated, as though it has been stretched too tightly over his frame. Track marks line the crooks of his arms, and dirty bandages wrap his knuckles. Despite all of this, he seems cheerful enough. The elf fixes his twinkling, bugged-out eyes on yours and offers you a broad smile, displaying a set of impossibly white teeth. When he speaks to you, his voice is surprisingly deep.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_8.t: Guten Tag. You here for some magic? Because Zaak Flash...
He gestures at himself with a dramatic flourish.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_9.t: Zaak cocks his head and offers you a lopsided grin.
Well, then, I might be able to help you.
He begins to twist a strand of his long, stringy hair around one of his spidery fingers. His smile remains fixed, a pearly crescent set into a face caked with grime.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_10.t: Oh, a little bit of everything. I've got your zen and your hyper, your nitro and your novacoke. If you want it, I've got it.
Zaak leans toward you and lowers his voice to a conspiratorial whisper.
I've even got a special concoction of my own design, but I wouldn't recommend it unless you're serious about getting high.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_14.t: Flash's grin broadens even further. His mouth is disturbingly large, and there's something menacing about the sight of those too-white teeth.

This is the real deal, chummer. A proprietary blend, designed and delivered by yours truly. Pharmaceutical-grade kamikaze cut with genuine spirit residue from the Tir and then mixed with my secret blend of herbs and spices.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_15.t: He produces a small packet filled with iridescent purple granules from the pocket of his dirty jeans.
You wanna get high? Buy some novacoke and throw yourself a party. But if you wanna *soar,* you get yourself some Flash.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_16.t: Zaak lets out a throaty, barking laugh.
Street mage? Me? Do I look like a street mage to you?
He shakes his head, but his smile doesn't waver. He fishes a small bag of pills out of his back pocket and dangles it before your eyes.
No, chummer, I'm not a "street mage." I've got the magic in my blood and the magic in these bags, but you won't catch ol' Zaak studying any spellbooks.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_18.t: Zaak snorts out a short burst of laughter. He waggles his bandaged hands in front of you.
Oh, these? Nothin' much, chummer, nothin' much. Tussled with a spirit back in Amsterdam. Y'know, we elves are a very spiritual people.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_19.t: Zaak lets out a short, barking laugh.
Oh, come on! Stop denying yourself! Embrace the power inside of you! You've got the mojo, just like me! Accept it!

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_20.t: Zaak's bug eyes light up with excitement.
You heard right! Damn right, chummer.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_22.t: Flash lowers his voice to a conspiratorial whisper.

Between you and me, my mojo is stronger than any corp wagemage's. Take those rat-scum bastards at Aztechnology. They cast spells all day and all night, right? *Fire* this and *lightning* that, just tossin' 'em around like candy.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_24.t: Trust me, friend. I have secret knowledge.
He slowly turns his head from side to side to give you a good look at his upturned ears.
Like I said. It's in the blood.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_25.t: The scars are subtle, but to your trained eye they stand out as though they were drawn on with red ink. It appears that Zaak's ears have been surgically cut, folded, and stitched back together again. The implication is clear: Zaak Flash is not an elf, but a human who's had cheap plastic surgery to try to make himself look like one. You've heard of elf posers before, but you've never seen one in Germany until now.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_26.t: All at once, the smile vanishes from Zaak's face. A bead of sweat traces its way down his cheek.
Look, you can't tell anyone about this, all right? The "I'm a magic elf" schtick boosts my sales.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_28.t: Yeah. I figured. Hang on a second, hang on.
He thrusts his hand into the pocket of his filthy jacket and rummages around. Eventually, he fishes out a small vial and a filthy credstick.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_30.t: He recoils as though he's been struck.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_31.t: Well... thanks, pal! Thank you so much!

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_33.t: All at once, the smile vanishes from Zaak's face. A bead of sweat traces its way down his cheek.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_35.t: Zaak offers you a broad, exaggerated wave.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_36.t: Zaak winks at you, his smile twisting up into a licentious smirk. You can't help noticing that his eyelashes appear to be dusted with glitter.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_37.t: You're calling me a liar? To my face?

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_38.t: In an instant, Zaak's disturbingly white smile returns.

523b74fe346238e41d00704a_39.t: Zaak shrugs, then slowly raises his right hand and waggles his fingers.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_97.t: His mouth twists into a bloody grin.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_99.t: His mouth twists into a bloody grin.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_100.t: His mouth twists into a bloody grin.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_142.t: He pauses a moment before croaking out an answer.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_145.t: His mouth twists into a bloody grin.
This Wednesday? I'll be putting your mother to work on the street corner. And when she's all worn out, I'll give your father a shot at the job.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_148.t: His mouth twists into a bloody grin.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_149.t: His mouth twists into a bloody grin.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_156.t: You're wrong, slag. I'll give you that much for free.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_188.t: The chip doesn't drive you insane. It OPENS YOUR EYES.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_191.t: Suddenly, he lurches forward. Every muscle in his body goes tight, and the cords stand out on his neck.
GIVE ME THE CHIP! IT WAS PROMISED TO ME! I *NEED* IT!

5356d86b323437d02500679a_196.t: His body begins to tremble. It looks like the drugs are wearing off.
T-they have money, she-troll. M-money and influence enough to put the syndicates to shame. Even I cannot say how much, but I d-do know that what I've seen is just the tip of the s-s-spear.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_199.t: You th-think that I asked them? It is n-n-not my p-place to know. All th-that I know is what was p-promised to m-m-me.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_205.t: He licks his lips and nods feverishly.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_207.t: O-over there. In the c-c-corner. Th-there's a duffel b-bag under the f-f-floor plate. N-now GIVE IT TO ME.

5356d86b323437d02500679a_266.t: He snarls up at Eiger, his face screwed into a look of pure hatred.
The chip is everything. EVERYTHING! It's worth DYING FOR! I was ignorant once, like you. But then my EYES WERE OPENED, and I SAW! The chip... slot one, and you become a *GOD!*

5356d86b323437d02500679a_267.t: The Engineer lets out a chuckle. It's a hoarse, barking sound.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_3.t: Oh, you know that I can’t do that! After last week’s security meeting? They’d have my job!

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_4.t: She jerks her head in the direction of a biometric fingerprint scanner set into the door frame.
If you want in this door, you're going to have to clock in like everyone else.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_5.t: The janitor shakes her head, her tired eyes narrowing.

Nice try, but no. I have two kids at home. I'm not risking my job so that you can be lazy. It only takes a few seconds, anyway... if you'd just do it instead of arguing about it, you could be inside already.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_6.t: The janitor lets out an exasperated sigh and folds her arms across her chest.
Okay, but just this once. You forget something else, and you're on your own.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_7.t: Her eyes pass from you to your teammates, then lock on yours.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_8.t: Really? Huh. This is the first I've heard of it.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_9.t: Oh, that's fun... but, um, should they be here tonight? Considering that it's crunch time, and that big speech last week about increasing our internal security, and all?

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_10.t: All right, fine, whatever. I didn't see you. Go ahead and do your thing. But if you get caught, it's on you, got that?

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_11.t: Okay, you know what? I've about had it with you.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_12.t: In a flash, her hand darts out to smack the panel set into the wall behind her. The shrill wail of an alarm klaxon fills the air.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_14.t: Oh, that's fun... but, um, is tonight really the best time? Considering that it's crunch time, and that big speech last week about increasing our internal security, and all?

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_15.t: All right, fine, whatever. I didn't see you. Go ahead and do your thing. But if you get caught, it's on you, got that?

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_16.t: Well, some of us get paid better than others. Wanna trade your workstation for my bucket?

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_30.t: The janitor stops for a second to consider.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_36.t: She pauses for a moment, weighing her options, and then her shoulders relax. She has evidently come to a decision.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_40.t: She pauses for a moment, considering, and you can see her body relax as she finally lets her guard down.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_43.t: Pleasure meeting you! I'm sure I'll catch you around.

5232a5b5353165f00c005f96_44.t: You're telling me. My husband has been unemployed for over a year now. He takes care of the kids, so that's something, but money is pretty tight.

5266f14d663661b825002f27_5.t: Well, let's see... I think that I've got somethin' that'll work fer ya.

5266f14d663661b825002f27_6.t: Ah, here we go. An old Korean player that I dug up last week. 2010 model, a real beauty.

5266f14d663661b825002f27_8.t: I suppose, given the time an' trouble I took puttin 'er back togther, that I'd be willin' to part with 'er for, oh, say...

5241cc1f3030314c16003f68_12.t: In a single, smooth motion, he pulls a knife from his belt and draws it along his own forearm. The blade leaves a stripe of bright red blood in its wake.

521e4028303031501600601e_4.t: Oh, Heimerich worries about *everything.* Price of being a dad, I guess.

527431c4663661902e0039a9_11.t: Yes. Yes, that was her name.

527431c4663661902e0039a9_16.t: Yes, please! Whatever you can do. Our situation is desperate.

527431c4663661902e0039a9_18.t: Yes. Yes, perhaps you can convince him where we could not. We have a commlink, provided to us by the decker. It is a direct line to Ezkibel's office. If you call him, he will answer.

527431c4663661902e0039a9_24.t: Of HMHVV. The virus that has infected us... that has *twisted* us... into *this.*

527431c4663661902e0039a9_26.t: The ghoul's voice comes out in a rasping sob.

527431c4663661902e0039a9_27.t: I speak for the Kreuzbasar's ghoul population. The Krieger strain may have ravaged my body...

527431c4663661902e0039a9_29.t: The shambling figure eagerly clasps its hands.

527431c4663661902e0039a9_30.t: Tell me, what sorts of jobs does he have in mind?

527431c4663661902e0039a9_34.t: The ghoul turns away in misery.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_7.t: A half-second later, a real-time simulation of Alice appears, seated behind her mahogany desk. Something about it reminds you of a figure from a wax museum; lifelike, but dead inside. It greets you with a facsimile of Alice's joyless smile.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_8.t: Its voice is hollow, synthetic. An automaton masquerading as a living woman... one who you've never even met in the flesh.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_14.t: You may interrupt this presentation at any point by saying “Alice, I have a question.”

527abf9e636134b83400302e_17.t: The simulacrum goes rigid for a moment, then seems to relax. When it speaks, it sounds more natural… as though the real Alice is speaking through it.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_29.t: The simulacrum hitches again, and you can feel the life drain out of it. It looks up at you robotically.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_36.t: The simulacrum stares at you impassively.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_57.t: By way of explanation, I would like you to read the following document.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_73.t: The simulacrum jerks back to life, then settles into a neutral pose.
There are no official records in the Saeder-Krupp database to support the existence of the APEX project. But the thing that I encountered *was* APEX. Of that, I am certain.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_88.t: The simulacrum hitches again. The pause stretches. Finally, it comes back to life.
The AI has a kill switch. But Alice deemed any additional contact with APEX to be an unjustifiable risk. Attempting to reach the kill switch would be inadvisable.

527abf9e636134b83400302e_90.t: The simulacrum stares at you with dead eyes.

An old Saeder-Krupp research lab, long since abandoned and fallen into disrepair. The facility is now hotly-contested gang territory.

528676ab626164981e004f2a_6.t: He nods, a look of pure misery on his face.

528676ab626164981e004f2a_9.t: He looks you over, paying special attention to your gear. Finally, he nods.

528676ab626164981e004f2a_20.t: He looks away, uncertainty in his eyes.

528676ab626164981e004f2a_21.t: No, you're right. We can't ask you to risk yourself without some kind of compensation.

528676ab626164981e004f2a_27.t: Franz's face lights up in excitement.

528676ab626164981e004f2a_32.t: He leans toward you and lowers his voice.

53ce8f8e303031f014001550_2.t: Right. The last thing we'd want is to come between you people and... whatever it is that you do.

53ce8f8e303031f014001550_6.t: Yes, he'll need another one in a year. And every year after that, from now until college. But I suppose that's the price of success.

53ce8f8e303031f014001550_9.t: Well, I suppose that *someone* has to be at the bottom of the pile.

53ce8f8e303031f014001550_11.t: It's an unfortunate situation, of course.

53ce8f8e303031f014001550_25.t: We're both under a lot of stress, and this isn't a purchase that we're making lightly. You see, Hans is enrolled in a very prestigious - and very competitive - school. Buying him AugmenTech enhancements isn't a luxury, it's a *necessity.*

53ce8f8e303031f014001550_26.t: Is that dangerous? We should call security!

53ce8f8e303031f014001550_27.t: He holds up the display model that they were inspecting so that you can see it. A small chip, along with a glossy, attractive information sheet.

53e021c4303031d807002814_1.t: You! The one from the cyberware kiosk! What the hell is going on here?

53e021c4303031d807002814_10.t: Misunderstanding, my ass. Your friend just called me an idiot in my own home!

528a6c366261645024008009_0.t: A rail-thin elf wearing a comically ornate wizard's robe stands at the center of the room. When he speaks, his voice wavers and cracks.

528a6c366261645024008009_3.t: He steeples his fingers, looking for all the world like a kid in a Halloween costume.

528a6c366261645024008009_13.t: Trithemius claps his hands and rubs them together vigorously.

528a6c366261645024008009_14.t: ...and here, as promised, is your spirit focus.

528a6c366261645024008009_24.t: Well, this is welcome news indeed! You have my thanks, stranger.

52ab4537643431431fd37135_42.t: He slides a tumbler full of sweet-smelling pear schnaps down your way. It’s warm.

53f7778d6361340021004fe8_21.t: The intercom crackles to life again.

536e83b43739378018006774_23.t: Didn't, particularly. But I found myself a new second-in-command who didn't like Hasenkamp much. His name is Plotz, and he runs this building. Your pal Blitz is gonna meet him any second, by the way.

Oh? And what might that be?

536e83b43739378018006774_25.t: Nice try, but I doubt that's true.

536e83b43739378018006774_26.t: If that was supposed to wound me, well... it didn't.

536e83b43739378018006774_27.t: He leers at you through the security grate.

53c4653830303198110015a5_6.t: The guard jerks upright in alarm.

53c5a0543030315803002800_0.t: The guard's stance shifts slightly as you approach.

53c5a0543030315803002800_1.t: You can't read the guard's expression through his mirrored face mask, but his body language makes it clear that he's staring at you.

53c5a0543030315803002800_5.t: Yeah, I hear ya. I swear, it's amazing that anyone gets anything done at all around here. Tell you what. 53c5a0543030315803002800_6.t: Unbelievable. Y'know, it makes my blood boil to think of how much money they make... it's people like you and me that actually keep this place *running.* And yet, the idiots at Dispatch pull in triple what we do for sitting on their asses and fouling everything up.

53c5a0543030315803002800_9.t: Best of luck, pal. Do us all a favor and call out those idiots at Dispatch... not that it'll do any good. On second thought, don't bother saying anything. They'd just wind up getting another raise.

5261d0f96361348409001bcb_0.t: The girl in the corner is a model stim junkie, all wild eyes and nervous energy. She greets you with a smile so wide as to border on the grotesque.

5261d0f96361348409001bcb_15.t: Her face scrunches into an exaggerated expression of disgust.

53c42515303031d40b000286_5.t: Staring daggers at the mage, he spits out a long string of foreign words in a single, susurrant breath. The sound of it is lovely, but his expression leaves little doubt that the message behind the words is an ugly one.

53c42515303031d40b000286_30.t: The elf blinks uncomprehendingly and takes a half-step back.

53d2d5ee3030316007006010_6.t: A stream of caustic syllables pours out of the elf's mouth. His back is stooped under the weight of his pack, but his hands have clenched into fists.

53d2d5ee3030316007006010_19.t: A stream of caustic syllables pours out of the elf's mouth. His back is stooped under the weight of his pack, but his hands have clenched into fists.

53e2c232303031081d007ffa_5.t: He exhales heavily, and a jumble of broken German pours out of his mouth.

53e2c232303031081d007ffa_33.t: He holds his tongue, still examining your face. A long, awkward silence descends over the both of you.

525724d23030318015002f24_6.t: Best of luck, and take care of yourself!

525724d23030318015002f24_16.t: It's an old family recipe. Can I getcha anything else?

525724d23030318015002f24_17.t: The smile on her face is not reflected in her eyes.
Good day to you, then! Come back any time!

5266f911663661b825003110_23.t: We are joined tonight by a survivor of this latest - and most horrifying - attack.
The reporter turns to face a pale man standing behind him.
Sir, I understand that you've been through a terrible ordeal... thanks for taking the time to speak with us tonight.

5266f911663661b825003110_109.t: We are joined tonight by a survivor of this latest - and most horrifying - attack.
The reporter turns to face a pale man standing behind him.
Sir, I understand that you've been through a terrible ordeal... thanks for taking the time to speak with us tonight.

52abb7126636619429005443_2.t: The troll's eyes flicker back and forth.
N... no… can't say that I have.

52abb7126636619429005443_3.t: The troll's eyes flicker back and forth.
I... I don’t know anything about weapons. I just sell scraps of salvage and home remedies.

52abb7126636619429005443_4.t: Well… I, uh... sometimes I get a lucky salvage.

52abb7126636619429005443_5.t: ...No? I don't know anything about crates.

52abb7126636619429005443_13.t: He takes a step back, managing to appear small despite his hulking troll frame.
Okay, okay, fine! Here's the money. Just take it, and leave me alone!

52abb7126636619429005443_14.t: He takes a step back, managing to appear small despite his hulking troll frame.
Please, no! Don't hurt me. I'm sorry. I just...

52abb7126636619429005443_15.t: The troll pulls nervously at his collar.
No worries. I, uh... should get back to work here.

5356d6a0303031c8120012eb_9.t: There is a long pause, and then the initiate looks to you. Screwing up her courage, she speaks.
Can... can I ask you a question?

5356d6a0303031c8120012eb_18.t: She shuffles her feet. Eventually, she looks up with a small smile.

5356d6a0303031c8120012eb_36.t: She shuffles her feet. Eventually, she looks up with a small smile.

5359b1b030303168150008a7_2.t: The voice is deep, but faint, and marred by echoes. It sounds as if he's shouting from the far end of a long tunnel.
You shouldn't have come here. I was content to let you live out your life in peace.

5359b1b030303168150008a7_4.t: I don't want to kill you, Glory. And I won't, unless you force my hand.
The flickering silhouette spreads its arms wide.
Ask me for my protection, and I will usher you out of this place. You can leave Feuerstelle in peace. If you ask me politely, I might even let your friend leave with you.

5359b1b030303168150008a7_6.t: The air all around him begins to pulse with scintillating bursts of color. His rippling, transparent form glows with heat from within.

5359b1b030303168150008a7_7.t: He turns his head to stare at her, a sad smile on his face.
I've given you everything. Will you repay my love with betrayal?

5359b1b030303168150008a7_9.t: Betrayal it is, then. Very well.
The air all around him begins to pulse with scintillating bursts of color. His rippling, transparent form glows with heat from within.

5359b1b030303168150008a7_10.t: The voice is deep, but faint, and marred by echoes. It sounds as if he's shouting from the far end of a long tunnel.
You shouldn't have come here. I was content to let you live out your life in peace.

5359b1b030303168150008a7_12.t: I don't want to kill you, Glory. And I won't, unless you force my hand.
The flickering silhouette spreads its arms wide.
Ask me for my protection, and I will usher you out of this place. You can leave Feuerstelle in peace. If you ask me politely, I might even let your friend leave with you.

5359b1b030303168150008a7_14.t: As you did to poor Marta?
The air all around him begins to pulse with scintillating bursts of color. His rippling, transparent form glows with heat from within.
That was a terrible thing, Glory. You've brought this upon yourself.

5359b1b030303168150008a7_15.t: The air all around him begins to pulse with scintillating bursts of color. His rippling, transparent form glows with heat from within.

5359caba3030316403004de3_3.t: Shadows in the shape of a man smile back at you. The distance in his voice has increased - it sounds like he's shouting from miles away.
I don't think so. You don't even know where the real me *is,* Glory.

5359caba3030316403004de3_4.t: The projection flashes you a condescending grin.
Believe whatever you like, fool. It won't do you any good. Years from now, I'll still be doing what I do, and you will have accomplished nothing.

5359caba3030316403004de3_7.t: The projection chuckles. The sound is thin and hollow.
You know better than that. But nothing makes me happier than the sound of you lying to yourself. Good-bye, Glory.

53f283ab303031481f003b44_4.t: The projection of Harrow convulses and bursts, and the scraps are consumed by shadow. The shadows dissipate, and Harrow is gone.

5362c867373937040e006702_6.t: Who the HELL are you, and why are you leaning on the GODDAMNED door buzzer?
The voice has a shrill, tremulous quality that you recognize instantly. This guy is riding a stim high - you're sure of it.

5362c867373937040e006702_8.t: The voice calms. Goes soft. Somehow, it isn't an improvement.
Now, you tell me who you are. Answer up. Chop-chop.

5362c867373937040e006702_9.t: Wariness creeps into the voice on the other side of the speaker.

5362c867373937040e006702_11.t: The good stuff? You mean, like, the "juice"? Well... okay. Maybe just a sip. Or two.

5362c867373937040e006702_13.t: There is a long pause, then the voice warbles out again.

5362c867373937040e006702_14.t: After a pregnant pause, you hear the voice again.
I... I guess I must've imagined it. Gotta lay off the stims, Jon... they're making you go all squirrelly again.

5362c867373937040e006702_16.t: After a pregnant pause, you hear the voice again.
Stupid thing. Must be busted again.

5362c867373937040e006702_24.t: Don't test me, Albert. You've always been a royal pain in the ass, you know that?
There is a burst of static as he coughs into the microphone.
...By the way, what happened to your voice? You don't sound like yourself.

53cea3413030316c09001713_1.t: Good afternoon, and welcome to AugmenTech, the boutique that makes a better "you" of you!
Her voice is overflowing with forced cheerfulness. Idly, you wonder how many times she has to give this exact speech every day.
Tell me how I can help you today!

53cea3413030316c09001713_2.t: Then you've come to the right place!
She gestures at the terminal in front of you.
Please feel free to browse our catalog. We have a *huge* stock of performance-enhancing augmentations to make *you* into the person you've always dreamed you could be!

53cea3413030316c09001713_4.t: A wonderful device - simply wonderful! And we're doing a special on it right now! All yours for only ¥250.

53cea3413030316c09001713_8.t: She glances at the item on the screen.
Looking at the new Encephalon, huh? Good choice. Our regional supervisor has one, and it is *amazing.* Totally worth the price!

53cea3413030316c09001713_12.t: Oh, I'm sorry. I'm afraid that you don't understand. We're a boutique shop, not a cybersurgery clinic. We can sell you the device and have it sent to the clinic of your choice for installation, but we don't do the surgery here.

53cea3413030316c09001713_15.t: You are now the proud owner of an Encephalon NEXT! We'll have it sent to the TRIAGE Cyberclinic, as per your request. When you arrive, just show the doctor or a member of his staff the proof of purchase that you're holding, and he'll know what to fetch for you.

53cea3413030316c09001713_17.t: Another time, then. Have a great day!

53cea3413030316c09001713_18.t: Thank you for browsing! If you need anything else, I'll be right here.

53cea3413030316c09001713_20.t: Her smile drops, but only slightly.
Oh, be my guest. Be sure to come back to me if you need any help!

524b4ce43030312c040004e9_4.t: Text continues to scroll down the terminal screen.
A problem has been detected with a core component of MCPOS. Restore MCPOS to factory default settings? Y/N WARNING: RESTORE PROCESS WILL TAKE SEVERAL MINUTES TO EXECUTE. CONNECTED DOORS AND PE

5282f52b30303104140000a4_0.t: This terminal shows a nearly featureless login screen.

5282f52b30303104140000a4_1.t: You find a selection of email files from various staff members.

53bb14cf3030316c02003f30_2.t: Suit yourself. Have a good one.

53bb14cf3030316c02003f30_3.t: All out of the good stuff, I'm afraid. How about a nice cup of soykaf?

53bb14cf3030316c02003f30_6.t: Glad you like it! It's a big hit with the street people around here, and the price is right.

53bb14cf3030316c02003f30_7.t: The waitress steps forward to greet you, a drink menu in her hand.
Welcome to KafShack, home of the budget-friendly caffeine fix. May I help you?

53bb14cf3030316c02003f30_12.t: I've got just thing for that big presentation, or that make-or-break annual report! It's called "Jolt," and it's AMAZING. Imagine a great, big cup of 'kaf, only without the nasty crash or the jittery stomach. You can take as many as you need without winding up in the hospital, and each one will keep you up for a couple of hours!

53bb14cf3030316c02003f30_14.t: Well, it's pricey. And there are some minor side effects.... if you take more than five in a single sitting, you'll probably get a nosebleed. I don't think that anyone has ever tried drinking more than ten at once.

5220fd7a3030316c1400164b_3.t: She looks up at you. The scowl has disappeared from her face, but she doesn't look happy, either.
Look. I really appreciate the help, but I have to do this myself. I'm already *way* behind, and Corporate's gonna have my ass if I don't get it done before lunch.

5220fd7a3030316c1400164b_4.t: A confused scowl crosses her face.
I don't even *know* you, buddy. Why in God's name would I leave you alone with a panel that *I'm* responsible for?

5220fd7a3030316c1400164b_5.t: The scowl drops off of her face, replaced with a blank stare. A second later, she finishes processing what you've said. A sly smile creeps across her lips.

5220fd7a3030316c1400164b_6.t: Y'know what? I *do* deserve an efficiency bonus.
She snatches her PDA off of her belt and presses it to your credstick. Once the transfer is completed, she steps away from the panel.
And now, I am going to go get plastered. Efficiently.

5220fd7a3030316c1400164b_8.t: Thank you for wasting my time.
Without another word, she turns back to her work, ignoring you.

5220fd7a3030316c1400164b_9.t: She swallows, then steps away from the panel.
I'm a big fan of my kneecaps. Tell you what... I'm going to climb back in my van and drive them somewhere far, far away.

5220fd7a3030316c1400164b_12.t: She doesn't even bother looking over this time.
Seriously. I'm on a tight schedule here. Leave me alone, or I'm calling security.

5220fd7a3030316c1400164b_13.t: She touches a commlink stud on her collar. You hear a burst of static.
Security! I've got intruders at the south gate!

522128b4303031100f001663_4.t: She waves her arms in exasperation.
I wasn't even working on the power system, you dolt! How could I have done ANYTHING to the power grid? It DOESN'T WORK LIKE THAT!

52606754303031cc10006e9a_2.t: The youth blinks in surprise. He half-lowers his weapon.

52606754303031cc10006e9a_11.t: I'm *not* your son. My father was a drunken bastard who used to burn me with his cigarettes when he got angry.
But then, *you* already knew that, didn't you? He's *your* brother.

52606754303031cc10006e9a_20.t: Shakily, Alexander raises his rifle. Levels it at Dietrich's head.

52606754303031cc10006e9a_22.t: The barrel of the rifle trembles in Alexander's arms.

52606754303031cc10006e9a_32.t: Alexander levels his rifle at Dietrich's head.

52f82113346238b41e005432_1.t: The young man looks shell-shocked. He holds his rifle clenched tight in his trembling hands.
I'm... yeah, Uncle Dietrich. Yeah, I'm okay. But I... those guys, I knew them... one of them was my bunkmate. I killed him...

52f82113346238b41e005432_7.t: He takes a deep breath, and stands up straight. Does his best to put a brave face on.
We've got two vans here... no sense in leaving one behind. You all pile into that one, and I'll take the other. I'll meet you back at the Kreuzbasar.

53c44895303031e81e0013ae_3.t: Like a janitor? Yes I do, because that's what you are. If you want to keep wearing that uniform, you'd better watch the attitude.

53c44895303031e81e0013ae_6.t: With a visible effort, he calms himself.

Look, sorry. I'm not trying to be unpleasant. Its just that I have a huge presentation to give to the board in twenty minutes... I stayed up all night practicing it. And now, I'm so tired that I can't even remember my own name.

53c44895303031e81e0013ae_8.t: I'm going to botch this. And the worst part is, most of those bastards will assume that it's because I'm an ork. They already make jokes behind my back. After this, it's gonna get a lot worse.

53c44895303031e81e0013ae_10.t: He searches his pockets, growing increasingly frantic.
God *damn* it. I left my credstick at home.

53c44895303031e81e0013ae_11.t: He pats his pockets again. Nothing.

All right, look. I need to get to my meeting right away, but here's what we can do. I'll give you my room code. You pop in and take the credstick off of the table - there's a couple hundred nuyen on there, and that should more than compensate you.

525822ac6636614c17004db9_1.t: Please don't hurt me! I'm just a line cook!
The cook's darting eyes are wide open, and behind them you see pure, animal terror.
If you let me go, I won't tell anyone! I promise!

525822ac6636614c17004db9_3.t: I thought you were one of THEM. Y'know, the gangers that took over and shot up the place. I work...
...That is to say, WORKED. I worked for the previous owners.

525822ac6636614c17004db9_7.t: That's the head chef, Felix. He took a hit to the chest when they raided this floor. I thought I could stop the bleeding. But, um...
The cook lowers his gaze to the floor at his feet.

525822ac6636614c17004db9_8.t: He glances over at the body again, and swallows. Hard.
...That'd probably be a good idea. He's already starting to smell.

525822ac6636614c17004db9_11.t: Oh... y-yeah, you're probably right. I'll just stay in here, then.
The line cook steals a glance at the dead chef lying crumpled by the sink.

525ccb07626164e02600603b_44.t: The Cyberzombie's massive hands flex twice, and it takes a half-step forward. Its mouth twists into a wide grin.

52619cb6626164a00b00490c_0.t: As you approach the MKVI, its head turns to track you. Its body is held at rigid attention.

52619cb6626164a00b00490c_6.t: Glory takes a moment to examine the protrusion at the base of the MKVI's spine. Then she pops the case on the Cyberzombie's control unit. Her sculpted fingers flicker and dance in the MKVI's circuitry. A moment later, a strange shiver runs through its body.

52619cb6626164a00b00490c_8.t: Blitz takes a moment to examine the protrusion at the base of the MKVI's spine. Then he pops the case on the Cyberzombie's control unit. He connects an optical cable to the jack above his temple and the other end into the MKVI's circuitry. A moment later, a strange shiver runs through its body, and he snatches the cable loose.

52619cb6626164a00b00490c_9.t: Now that you have a moment to examine the cyberware in question, it's surprisingly easy to disable. It only takes a minute or two to expose the system's control circuits. It'd be the easiest thing in the world for you to "flip the switch."

52619cb6626164a00b00490c_10.t: You input a single command into the MKVI's control circuit. A strange shiver runs through its entire body.

52619cb6626164a00b00490c_11.t: It stumbles forward, lurching awkwardly on its feet. A few seconds later, it seems to recover. It stares at its hands for a moment, then turns to face you.

52619cb6626164a00b00490c_12.t: The MKVI speaks two words. Its voice is as brittle as broken glass.

52619cb6626164a00b00490c_13.t: Slowly, mechanically, the Cyberzombie reaches up to grip its head in both hands. And it *squeezes.*

52619cb6626164a00b00490c_14.t: Caught in the viselike grip of those heavy, industrial cyberarms, the MKVI's cyberskull might as well be made of tinfoil. The air fills with the tortured sounds of tearing plastic and buckling steel.

52a0bac0636134f018001d6d_1.t: Almost every inch of the thing is covered in dull steel and gleaming chrome. Its mechanical arms end in articulated hands that wouldn't look out of place on an industrial machine... bulky, cumbersome things, designed for crushing power rather than finesse. Clutched in one of those crushing hands, a chaingun glints with sinister purpose.

52a0bac0636134f018001d6d_5.t: The figure stands completely still, but its eyeless head fidgets in agitation. Something about it reminds you of a snake tasting the air. Off to one side, the screen of a control console glows cheerfully in the gloom.

52951a323030310017004693_2.t: At the lightest touch, the papers crumble to dust. You can make out one small scrap that still contains legible writing.

Look, Jörg, I don't care what you heard down there! Just because you heard something and a few people got sick from some "weird disease" doesn't mean that there are monsters wandering around. We've got to finish our blasting, or we won't get paid - no amount of weirdness is going to change that.

523bfc69346238e41d008008_35.t: And a scone! I was going to say, “and a scone as well”! Nothing is too good for them!

523bfd34346238e41d0080bf_0.t: The voice that comes from the man in the chair is as enormous as its owner: a deep, booming roar, dripping with unrestrained mirth.
A fine day for a soykaf, yes?

523bfd34346238e41d0080bf_2.t: Goldschmidt responds with a raucus belly laugh; apparently he finds the shopkeeper's insults to be hilarious.
Ah, Altuğ. You are as quick-witted and sharp-tongued as ever. I bow to you.

523bfd34346238e41d0080bf_4.t: Goldschmidt smiles up at you, his small eyes glittering.
Enough of this senseless bickering. You have approached me for a reason, yes? Tell me: what can Jan Goldschmidt do for you?

523bfd34346238e41d0080bf_6.t: Oh, don't mind our dear Altuğ. Yes, he is a peevish man, but his ire was not directed at you.
Goldschmidt grins up at you from his overstuffed chair.
I have the distinction of being the sole target of Altuğ's rage and derision. It is a badge that I delight in wearing.

523bfd34346238e41d0080bf_7.t: I put up with them because they amuse me. The fact that they amuse me infuriates my dear friend Altuğ, who in turn hurls more insults.
Goldschmidt raises his soykaf cup in salute.
And thus, the cycle continues. It is two years now that I've been your customer, yes, Altuğ?

523bfd34346238e41d0080bf_10.t: Goldschmidt gives you a deep nod, his jowls quivering.

52af8984663661f820000fd9_1.t: As you approach, the machine makes a series of sputtering sounds as the dust-covered screen flickers dimly to light.
>>Time since last update: 52:07:11:05:22. >>ERROR: Primary Database not found. Restarting in offline mode. >>Restarting...

52af8984663661f820000fd9_11.t: You hear a loud click, and a section of the wall nearby slides away, revealing a narrow passage into darkness.

53558fe830303114080016c6_5.t: Another voice, high-pitched and pleading. An adolescent boy.
But what if it *is* someone? What if the bad people have found us? What if they followed me here, and they've come to...

53558fe830303114080016c6_7.t: You hear another boy's voice. This one is deeper.

53558fe830303114080016c6_19.t: The adolescent voice trembles out a response.
A-all right, Marta. H-have a good night.

53558fe830303114080016c6_32.t: The adolescent voice trembles out a response.
A-all right, Marta. H-have a good night.

5356a321303031f00f0012f6_16.t: ...You shouldn't have come here. The tools of government aren't welcome at Feuerstelle.
You hear a soft sound. You recognize it instantly: a blade being drawn from its sheath.
We have a use for people like you.

53bae95d636134dc2700035c_14.t: It's probably good that you're keeping your distance, Taylor... we're hearing some frightening things about this rift. I'm looking at a statement from renowned hermetic theorist Elizabeth Alder, which I'm going to read to you now...
He squints up at his teleprompter.

53ce8f8e303031f014001550_1.t: Upon seeing your uniform, her nose wrinkles.
Oh, hello. Are we in your way...? We wouldn't want to keep you from your work.

53ce8f8e303031f014001550_18.t: She flashes you a brilliant smile.

53ce8f8e303031f014001550_24.t: She gives you a withering look.
You really have no idea what it's like out there for people like us, do you? You have the luxury of remaining unenhanced. Dull. Slow.

53f0fa5463613480180096d5_10.t: The corporate spokesman turns to address the security officers behind him.
You two. Enter the building, find whoever's on that intercom, and silence him.

53f0fa5463613480180096d5_12.t: He turns back to face you, reaching for the sidearm on his hip.

53f0fa5463613480180096d5_14.t: The security personnel raise their weapons into an attack posture.

53f0fa5463613480180096d5_16.t: He shakes his head in disgust.
Idiot shadowrunner. You people don't know when to keep your mouths shut. Men, OPEN FI--

525c3d08303031d410005d43_3.t: Sweeping your gaze over the gangers assembled before you, you identify the owner of the voice: a leathery woman with wild hair and a mirthless smile.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_4.t: She calls out again, her voice brimming with pride.
Hello out there! Welcome to Rammbock territory.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_6.t: Surfturf? Those tunnel rats? They're ancient history... go ahead and call 'em if you don't believe me.
Her face twists into an ugly smile.
Scream as loud as you want. They won't hear you. We buried them last week.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_9.t: Otherwise, you'd better find another way to get where you're goin'. 'Cause this bridge is ours, and nobody crosses without paying the toll. Nobody.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_14.t: Heh. And here I thought that today was gonna be boring. RAMMBOCK! KILL 'EM ALL!

525c3d08303031d410005d43_15.t: Good. That's a real good idea, pal.
The ganger boss jeers at Maxim.
See that? Your muscle is smarter than you are. You should put them in charge.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_19.t: Maxim steps back, leaving you to negotiate with the Rammbock leader. The ganger boss stands there, hands on her hips, awaiting your response.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_20.t: The ganger breaks into a toothy grin.
That's smart of you, buddy... real smart.

525c3d08303031d410005d43_22.t: There is a brief pause, then the Rammbock boss yells out a response.

52af846f663661f820000f95_2.t: And I wouldn’t suggest asking questions about such things around here.

52af846f663661f820000f95_4.t: I wouldn't know. I'm not in charge of the cleaning around here.
Hell... nobody is these days, the way things have been going. Hopefully our luck will turn, sooner or later. It'd be nice to take some pride in this place again.

52af846f663661f820000f95_6.t: Fine. I don't know who you are, or what you're doing snooping around down here. But you'd better come with me, now. Our leader's going to want to speak with you.
Her free hand reaches down to produce a thick leather blindfold.

52af846f663661f820000f95_8.t: She reaches out to blindfold you, and the world goes dark.

52af846f663661f820000f95_9.t: Look. I don't want to do this any more than you do. But we're not going to let you keep sniffing around here.

52af846f663661f820000f95_11.t: She reaches out to blindfold you, and the world goes dark.

52367040353165d4090012ee_0.t: HALT! This is a RESTRICTED AREA!
The guard raises his weapon. His voice is brimming with excitement.

52367040353165d4090012ee_1.t: The guard sounds both confused and frightened.
Look, it's my first day, but... the Captain didn't say anything about--

52367040353165d4090012ee_3.t: After a long pause, he stammers out a response.
Yeah... yeah, okay. You go ahead and do what you need to do. But don't leave this room, you hear? I want to keep an eye on you.

52367040353165d4090012ee_5.t: After a long pause, he stammers out a response.
He steps aside, mumbling to himself.
Like you said... they say jump, we jump.

52367040353165d4090012ee_7.t: He squints at your badge, scowling. After a few seconds, he lowers his weapon.
Sorry to have bothered you. You're cleared to be here, but stay where I can see you, and don't leave this room.

52367040353165d4090012ee_8.t: Finally, some action! INTRUDER AT MY POSITION!
He pulls back the slide on his rifle and opens fire.

523749c0303031940c0001ec_1.t: He looks suprised to see you, but not alarmed.
Oh, hey... I don't usually see Knight Errant personnel wandering around this part of the building. What's up?

523749c0303031940c0001ec_2.t: All right... well, I won't keep you. But it makes me feel safer, knowing that you guys are around.

523749c0303031940c0001ec_6.t: The expression on his face is one of pure horror.
P-please... please tell our "friends" that I hear them, loud and clear. And tell them that I'm making the call.

523749c0303031940c0001ec_7.t: You poor fool. You don't even realize what a pawn you are.

523749c0303031940c0001ec_10.t: Y-yes! I... I know who sent you. Please, tell your masters that I won't tell anyone that you were here!

5355a5313030316c16006cca_5.t: I don't know who you are, but thank you.
He grimaces and claps a hand on his left forearm. Blood oozes between his fingers.
Those monsters were going to bleed me out. They took turns cutting me - carved up my arm like a holiday roast.

5355a5313030316c16006cca_8.t: He struggles back to his feet.
I... I think that I can make it out of here on my own. My truck isn't far from here.

5355a5313030316c16006cca_10.t: He stares at Glory for a moment. Then his eyes flit to you.

5387cc6d636134001b008359_1.t: Oh, thank God. And thank you for walking in when you did... you saved my life.

5387cc6d636134001b008359_3.t: He limps a few steps forward, then looks back at Marta.

52409cd06261649814003f65_9.t: Good God, I could've clocked out an hour ago. Well, pleasure meeting you... and good night.

52409cd06261649814003f65_32.t: Yeah, crunch time again. Gotta love it.

What you can see of his face looks raw and slick, like old scar tissue stretched tight over his skull. He wears an expression of supreme confidence.

524c5c7a30303194170016ab_2.t: But now it's too late for that sort of generosity. Visitors aren't welcome down here.
The gaping maw of the minigun jerks upward. An impatient gesture, loaded with malice.

524c5c7a30303194170016ab_6.t: True! I can't deny it. But wouldn't it be easier if you didn't fight back?
The ork shrugs, and you hear the distinctive whir of his Vindicator's motor spinning up to speed. The barrels begin to blur as he wheels the weapon to face you.

524c5c7a30303194170016ab_7.t: The ork's gauntleted fist tightens, and you hear the distinctive whir of his Vindicator's motor spinning up to speed. The barrels begin to blur as he wheels the weapon to face you.

524c5c7a30303194170016ab_8.t: The ork's gauntleted fist tightens, and you hear the distinctive whir of his Vindicator's motor spinning up to speed. The barrels begin to blur as he wheels the weapon to face you.

52d3674b323437281e004b53_11.t: The nasal woman’s voice can be heard again in a singsong tone.
Guten Tag. How may I help you?

52d3674b323437281e004b53_12.t: Her tone changes, becomes more businesslike.

52d3674b323437281e004b53_13.t: I told him it wasn’t time to make a move yet.
What do you think I am, an idiot?

52d3674b323437281e004b53_14.t: The council needs to meet again.
I know. Getting everyone in the same room is... challenging. Getting them to agree on a course of action is going to be even more challenging.

52d3674b323437281e004b53_15.t: From my perspective, the Kreuzbasar was only stable because of her. If she really is… out of the way… well… we’ll see, won’t we?

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_18.t: He stalks toward you, his hands twitching.

53cd96c1323437c03b00323e_22.t: He continues his rant without pausing for breath.

53cec80c303031b41700171d_6.t: The saleswoman looks up from her register.

53cec80c303031b41700171d_7.t: No, not at all! You've got a good eye - that's one of the nicest outfits in the store.
Here, let me show you to a dressing room...

We're doomed. The world is going to die.

The Schockwellenreiter's contact for this kiez is no more. You are listed as a follow up contact. This is our only secured line to this kiez. Please listen to the following instructions carefully, if you are a supporter of our cause.

52322da83531650c0500443b_4.t: Who... who are you? How the hell did you get in here?

52409cd06261649814003f65_3.t: The wageslave turns and blinks at you.

52409cd06261649814003f65_6.t: Oh! Well. Hello, then. I'm Klaus.
Good God, I could've clocked out an hour ago. Well, pleasure meeting you... and good night.

52409cd06261649814003f65_7.t: I've never seen you before. I'm sorry, but I'm going to have to call security.

52af73ce663661f820000f5d_1.t: Not gonna happen, stranger. Give the money back, and my friend here doesn't have to raise his voice.

52af73ce663661f820000f5d_2.t: Sure, we can talk. But not here. You've overstepped your bounds, stranger. Now you need to come with us.

52af73ce663661f820000f5d_4.t: Naturally. We'll take you safely to see him.

5356d1b5303031e00e004929_5.t: The initiate eyes you nervously. His gaze flits to Glory, then falls to her cyberlimbs. He swallows.

5356d1b5303031e00e004929_6.t: The initiate eyes you nervously. His gaze flits to Glory, then falls to her cyberlimbs. He swallows.

5356d1b5303031e00e004929_10.t: Uh... yeah. Yeah, okay. You do that.

536a763a303031d404004926_3.t: Aren't they a little old? Harrow says that people get spoiled by the world when they grow to adulthood outside of Feuerstelle. He says that--

536a763a303031d404004926_5.t: The acolyte's smile withers and dies.

536a763a303031d404004926_7.t: An outsider. We found him poking around the garden, so we were punishing him.

536a763a303031d404004926_11.t: Anything for you, Marta. You and Harrow. He's all yours.

5241c1dc3030314c16003ee6_6.t: He looks at his PDA again.

But Titonius! The Jubuthon hordes will smite you if you stay! The people in that valley are only peasants. You needn't throw your life away for them! Flee with me, into the Enchanted Wood of Trala-sheen!

5260338a63613410230047f5_6.t: Understanding slowly shows on the young woman's face. Her eyes go wide.

5260338a63613410230047f5_8.t: She sets her jaw, a look of disgust on her pallid face.

53bae95d636134dc2700035c_24.t: We... ah... we sent some volunteers. Brave chaps, you know - they walked into the rift itself, carrying food an' water and towing rope lines. My own cousin went in there.

Frag off, chumbnuts! Or on second thought, maybe we'll *frag* you off!

53ebf2cb32343718420073fb_4.t: Her frown inverts into a cruel smile.

529540e23030316c14004567_5.t: With two whole strike teams already set in ambush? I don't see what chance they have.

52b9ead8643431e75a3c9ada_3.t: In the back of the room, an ominous presence hovers silently at chest-height. Light glints off of the machine's pitted metal carapace. Eiger sees the look on your face, and her voice cuts through the quiet in the room.

5390eb2c323437243a001be8_6.t: The enormous machine hovers impassively, its turbofan rotors keeping it silently aloft. A small arsenal of heavy weaponry juts out from the seams of its armor plating.

53558fe830303114080016c6_6.t: The female voice cuts in, soft and comforting.
